name: SPELL NAME
description:
Spell description.

**Casting Time** less than 1 round is a penalty to the caster's initiative that round. Casting times of 1 round or more resolve at the end of the stated period.

**Range** of 0 means the spell centers on or affects only the caster. Touch requires physical contact. All other ranges require line of sight to a visible target.

**Components:** V \= verbal (must speak clearly; casting silently or in a whisper halves range, or halves duration for touch and self spells). S \= somatic (both hands free; casting with restricted hands halves area of effect, or gives the target \+4 to their saving throw for single-target spells). M \= material (specified in description; casting without it halves potency or halves duration for utility spells — consumed unless otherwise noted). Components marked **\[Mandatory\]** cannot be omitted or substituted.

Specialist Mages may ignore material component requirements for spells from their school without penalty. All other arcane casters require all listed components for full effect.

**Save:** Will resists mind-affecting effects. Reflex resists area effects and anything avoidable by quick movement. Fortitude resists poison, death, physical transformation, and direct physical trauma. Negate means a successful save cancels all effects. Half means a successful save reduces damage by half. None means no save is permitted.

**Reversible spells** are noted in the heading. The reversed version requires a separate memorized slot. Reversed versions are described at the end of the spell entry.

Spells that grant similar bonuses do not stack — the stronger effect takes precedence. A spell caster can dismiss their own spell by concentrating for a round within casting range.

name: AFFECT NORMAL FIRES
description:
Choose one effect at casting: alter illumination or extinguish flames.

*Alter Illumination.* Adjust any non-magical fire in the area from ember-dim to double its normal radius. Does not affect fuel consumption or damage. The caster can freely vary the adjustment during the duration. Does not affect magical fires or fire-based creatures.

*Extinguish Flames.* Snuff any non-magical fire in the effective area. The spell ends immediately after this use.

name: ALARM
description:
Wards an area against entry. Any creature larger than six cubic inches or weighing more than 3 pounds that enters, touches, or contacts the warded area without the password triggers an audible ringing — clearly heard to 60 feet, minus 10 feet per closed door and 20 feet per wall in between. The alarm sounds for one round. Ethereal and astral creatures do not trigger it; invisible, incorporeal, levitating, flying, and gaseous creatures do.

The caster may speak a single word to dismiss the alarm at any time.

*Material component:* A tiny bell and a length of fine silver wire.

name: ARMOR
description:
Creates an invisible field of magical force that raises the recipient's AC to 14\. This replaces worn armor if the worn armor would provide a lower AC, but stacks with Dexterity and magical bonuses. The spell absorbs up to 8 \+ the caster's level in hit points of damage before dissipating; after that threshold, it ends.

*Material component:* A piece of cured leather.

name: AUDIBLE GLAMER
description:
Creates sounds originating from any point within range — whispering, normal conversation, shouting, or any non-verbal sounds the caster can describe. The volume can range from a whisper to a roar equivalent to 16 or more people. The sound is convincing enough that listeners will react to it as real. Intelligent creatures may attempt to disbelieve.

*Material component:* A bit of wool or a small lump of wax.

name: BURNING HANDS
description: A fan of searing flame jets from the caster's spread fingers. Deals 1 point of fire damage per caster level (maximum 5\) to everything in the arc. Flammable materials in the area ignite. The caster cannot harm themselves with this spell.

name: CANTRIP
description: Produces minor magical effects too trivial for formal spell slots — cleaning a surface, lighting a candle, chilling a drink, producing a harmless spark, creating a small sound or smell, slightly altering color, or other effects judged by the GM to deal no damage and produce no lasting change. Each use is a minor action. The effects persist only as long as the caster maintains the spell duration.

name: CHANGE SELF
description: Alters the caster's apparent height, weight, facial features, hair, skin tone, and clothing. The caster cannot appear as a specific known individual convincingly enough to fool close associates. Size changes are limited to one foot taller or shorter and roughly 50 pounds of apparent weight difference. The illusion does not alter voice, scent, or touch. Observers who physically handle the caster or scrutinize them closely may attempt a Will save to disbelieve.

name: CHARM PERSON
description:
Causes one humanoid creature (human, non-human, or giant-class creature of roughly human shape) to regard the caster as a trusted friend and ally. The charmed creature will not fight for the caster but will follow reasonable requests, answer questions, and extend trust in ambiguous situations. It will not take actions it considers suicidal or obviously harmful to those it cares about.

The subject is allowed a new save to break the charm at intervals based on Intelligence: INT 3–8, one save per month; INT 9–12, one save per three weeks; INT 13–18, one save per two weeks. Each save is modified by the caster's Charisma reaction modifier (positive for the caster).

The spell applies only to beings with humanoid form and reasoning ability. It does not work on constructs, undead, or creatures without a language.

name: CHILL TOUCH
description:
The caster's touch drains life energy. Each successful touch attack (using the caster's Attack Bonus) deals 1d4 damage and drains 1 point of Strength on a failed Fortitude save. Strength loss recovers at 1 point per turn after the spell ends. Against undead, chill touch deals no damage but forces a Will save or causes the undead to flee for 1d4 rounds.

The caster may make one touch attempt per round for the spell's duration.

name: COLOR SPRAY
description:
A burst of clashing colors erupts from the caster's palm. Creatures in the cone are affected based on HD:

* 2 HD or fewer: Unconscious for 2d4 rounds  
* 3–4 HD: Blinded for 1d4 rounds  
* 5+ HD: Stunned for 1 round

A successful Will save negates all effects. The caster can choose to exclude allies from the cone if they are at least 10 feet from the edge.

*Material component:* A pinch each of powder from a red, yellow, and blue gem.

name: COMPREHEND LANGUAGES
description:
The recipient can understand the spoken or written language of any intelligent creature they encounter. The recipient cannot speak or write the language, only comprehend it. The spell works on text — but is blocked by magical encryption (such as Illusory Script).

*Reversed — Confuse Languages:* Makes one target's speech or writing unintelligible for the duration.

*Material component:* A pinch of soot and salt.

name: DANCING LIGHTS
description:
Creates one of the following, chosen at casting: up to four torch-bright lights moving in any pattern within range; up to four 1-foot spheres of dim light moving in formation; or a single glowing humanoid-shaped figure. The caster can move all lights freely within range as a minor action. The lights do not emit heat and do not ignite flammables.

*Material component:* A bit of phosphorus or a glowworm.

name: DETECT MAGIC
description: The caster can sense magical emanations ahead of them in a corridor-shaped area. Items, creatures, and locations with active magical properties glow faintly. The caster can identify the school of a detected aura with a successful Intelligence check. Illusions and mind-affecting magic may block detection at the GM's discretion.

name: DETECT UNDEAD
description:
Detects the presence and general direction of undead creatures within the area. Does not reveal exact location, type, or number. Undead hidden behind walls or closed doors can still be detected.

*Material component:* A bit of earth from a grave.

name: ENLARGE
description:
Increases the size and weight of one creature or non-magical object by up to 10% per caster level (max 200% of original size). Enlarged creatures deal \+1d6 additional damage with melee weapons per doubling of size. Armor must be resized for the effect — magic armor adjusts automatically. The spell cannot enlarge an object already at its maximum possible size.

*Reversed — Reduce:* Shrinks the target by up to 10% per level, to a minimum of 10% of original size. Reduced creatures deal reduced damage accordingly.

*Material component:* A pinch of powdered iron.

name: ERASE
description: Removes writings — mundane or magical — from up to two pages or the equivalent surface area. Magical writing has a 90% chance of being erased (roll percentile); the chance drops to 30% against Wizard Mark. A successfully erased magical text is permanently destroyed.

name: FEATHER FALL
description:
Can be cast instantly as a free reaction when the caster or an observed creature begins to fall. Affected creatures and objects descend at 2 feet per second regardless of actual fall distance, taking no falling damage when they reach the ground. The spell can affect up to one creature plus one additional creature per two caster levels.

*Note:* The purely verbal component and instant casting time mean this spell can be cast on someone else's initiative, including while surprised.

name: FIND FAMILIAR
description:
Summons a small animal to serve as the caster's familiar. The caster cannot specify what animal appears — the GM determines what creature in the area answers the call. Possible familiars include cats, crows, hawks, owls, weasels, lizards, frogs, and similar small animals. Some extremely unusual familiars may have additional properties.

While bonded, the familiar can communicate with the caster at close range and shares their senses. The caster gains a minor benefit specific to the familiar's type (see individual familiar tables in Appendix A). The familiar has the caster's saving throws and uses their hit points, but does not gain additional HP — if killed, the caster takes 2d6 damage and cannot summon another familiar for one year. The familiar departs if the caster dies.

*Material component:* 1,000 gp worth of incense and brazier with charcoal.

name: FLOATING DISC
description:
Creates a slightly concave disk of magical force, 3 feet in diameter, that floats at waist height and follows the caster within 20 feet. It can carry up to 100 pounds per caster level. The disc moves with the caster but cannot ascend or descend stairs or inclines greater than 45 degrees. If the caster moves more than 20 feet from the disc, it disappears and any contents fall to the ground.

*Material component:* A drop of mercury.

name: FRIENDS
description:
Temporarily improves the caster's apparent Charisma by 2d4 points for reaction rolls and social interactions. Creatures that fail their Will save treat the caster with noticeably greater warmth and openness for the duration. When the spell ends, creatures who were affected and notice the sudden change in their attitude become suspicious or hostile — the degree depends on how they were influenced.

*Material component:* Chalk, lampblack, and vermilion applied to the face.

name: GAZE REFLECTION
description: Creates a mirror-like field around the caster that reflects gaze attacks back at their origin. Any creature using a gaze attack against the caster must make a Will save against its own ability. If the save fails, the creature suffers its own gaze attack. The spell does not protect against other attack forms used by gaze-attack creatures.

name: GREASE
description:
Coats a surface with viscous magical grease. Creatures moving through or across the area must succeed on a Reflex save or fall prone. Getting up from prone in the greased area requires another Reflex save. The grease is also flammable — it burns for the round it ignites, dealing 1d6 fire damage to anyone in the area before burning off.

Alternatively, the caster can target a held object. The holder makes a Fortitude save or drops the item.

*Material component:* A bit of pork fat.

name: HOLD PORTAL
description: Holds a door, gate, or similar portal shut as if locked and barred. The holder of a wizard lock spell can freely open a held portal. Creatures with at least 4 more HD than the caster can burst through with a successful Strength check. Knock dispels hold portal.

name: HYPNOTISM
description: Captures the attention of 1d6 creatures with 3 HD or fewer by chanting and making rhythmic hand movements. Affected creatures become highly susceptible to suggestion: any request by the caster that seems reasonable (not suicidal or obviously against the target's interests) will be followed for the duration. This is not charm, but temporary compulsion of attention that breaks immediately when the effect ends or the target takes damage.

name: IDENTIFY
description:
Reveals the magical properties of items — their function, number of charges (if any), command words, and whether they are cursed. For each item examined, the caster rolls against their chance to Learn Spell (Table 1.4). On success, one property is revealed. Each casting allows multiple rolls during the spell's duration — one per round — until the duration expires or the caster stops.

Cursed items reveal their properties as benign unless the caster succeeds on a separate roll (penalized by the item's curse strength). A false result is possible.

*Material component:* A pearl worth at least 100 gp (consumed) and an owl feather. The caster must not have eaten for 24 hours before casting.

name: JUMP
description:
Dramatically enhances the recipient's leaping ability. Running broad jump: 30 feet forward, up to 10 feet high. Standing broad jump: 10 feet. Vertical jump: 10 feet. The recipient lands safely from any height reached by the jump, but not from additional falling afterward.

*Material component:* A grasshopper's hind leg.

name: LIGHT
description:
Creates light equivalent to a bright lantern at a chosen point in range, illuminating a 20-foot radius. The caster can target a creature's eyes to blind them — a failed Fortitude save results in blindness for the spell's duration. An affected creature that succeeds on the save is not blinded.

*Reversed — Darkness:* Creates an area of magical darkness that extinguishes non-magical light sources in a 20-foot radius. Creatures with darkvision are not affected.

name: MAGICAL AURA
description:
Causes a non-magical item to register as magical under detect magic, identify, or similar effects. The caster can specify what school of magic the false aura should appear to belong to. Creatures using identify get a Will save to detect the false aura; on success, they know the aura is fake.

Alternatively, the caster can mask an existing magical aura, making it appear non-magical.

*Material component:* A small square of silk.

name: MAGIC MISSILE
description: Fires one glowing bolt of pure magical force that strikes unerringly, dealing 1d4+1 force damage. An additional bolt is created for every two levels above 1st: two bolts at 3rd level, three at 5th, four at 7th, five at 9th (maximum five bolts). Multiple bolts can be directed at a single target or distributed among multiple targets within range. Magic missiles never miss and cannot be blocked by mundane obstacles, but are stopped by a successful magic resistance check and by specific protective spells such as shield.

name: MENDING
description:
Repairs a single break or tear in a non-magical object up to 1 cubic foot in size per caster level. Two broken pieces must be present. The spell restores the object to its original integrity as if the break never occurred. It does not restore structural damage from prior aging, multiple breaks, or magical damage.

*Material component:* Two small magnets or lodestones.

name: MESSAGE
description:
Establishes a whispered communication link between the caster and up to one additional creature per level. The caster whispers a message; designated recipients in range hear it clearly regardless of ambient noise and can whisper a reply audible only to the caster. The link does not penetrate magical silence.

*Material component:* A short piece of copper wire.

name: MOUNT
description:
Summons a light riding horse appropriate to the climate and terrain. The horse is loyal and steady during the spell's duration, then disappears. If slain, it simply vanishes with no ill effects to the caster. The horse carries normal equipment loads and can move at its normal movement rate.

*Material component:* A bit of horse hair.

name: PHANTASMAL FORCE
description:
Creates a visual illusion of any object, creature, or effect chosen at casting. The illusion has no sound, temperature, smell, or substance. Creatures that interact with it (attempt to touch it, walk into it, pass through it) receive a bonus to their disbelief save equal to their Intelligence modifier. The illusion disappears if the caster stops concentrating, after two rounds without concentration, or when successfully disbelieved.

*Material component:* A bit of fleece.

name: PROTECTION FROM EVIL
description:
Creates a magical ward with three effects. First, summoned and conjured creatures of opposing alignment suffer −2 to their attack rolls against the warded creature, who gains \+2 to all saves against their attacks. Second, the warded creature cannot be touched by mind-controlling effects from evil creatures (the effect breaks if the warded creature attacks the charming creature). Third, extraplanar evil creatures attempting to touch the warded creature must succeed on a Will save or be unable to contact them.

*Reversed — Protection from Good:* As above but directed against good-aligned creatures and effects.

*Material component:* Holy water (or unholy for the reverse), a circle traced in powdered silver.

name: READ MAGIC
description:
Allows the caster to read magical inscriptions — spells on scrolls, Wizard Marks, Illusory Script, magical writing on items. A scroll read with this spell can subsequently be used without further casting of read magic. Without this spell, a wizard cannot learn spells from scrolls.

*Material component:* A clear crystal or mineral prism.

name: SHIELD
description: Generates an invisible disk of magical force on the caster's arm. Provides AC 18 against missile attacks from the front and AC 16 against melee attacks from the front. Completely blocks magic missiles directed at the caster. Does not stack with armor worn — use whichever is better for each attack type. Does not provide any benefit to the rear.

name: SHOCKING GRASP
description: Charges the caster's hands with electrical energy. The next successful melee touch attack delivers 1d8 \+ 1 electricity damage per caster level (maximum 1d8+8). Metal armor provides no protection. Creatures wearing or made of metal suffer maximum damage. The charge dissipates after a successful hit or at the end of the duration.

name: SLEEP
area: Up to 8 HD in a 15-ft radius
description:
Puts creatures into a magical slumber. The spell affects a total of 2d4 HD worth of creatures, starting with the lowest HD present and moving upward. Creatures with 5+ HD are unaffected. No save is permitted — affected creatures simply fall asleep unless physically disturbed. Sleeping creatures are helpless; waking them requires a full round of jostling or a slap.

Undead and constructs are immune.

*Material component:* A pinch of fine sand, rose petals, or a live cricket.

name: SPIDER CLIMB
description:
Allows the recipient to move across walls and ceilings at half their normal movement rate. Both hands and feet must be available — the recipient cannot carry large objects or wield weapons without losing the effect. The adhesion works through gloves and most light footwear but not through thick gauntlets or heavy boots.

*Material component:* A drop of bitumen and a live spider.

name: SPOOK
description: Makes one creature with Intelligence 6 or higher perceive the caster as a terrifying entity. The creature must succeed on a Will save (penalized by 1 for every 3 points of the caster's Intelligence above 12\) or back away from the caster as quickly as possible. The creature will not take other hostile actions while retreating. Does not cause panic or full flight — just steady withdrawal.

name: TAUNT
description: The caster delivers a magically enhanced verbal barrage of insults and provocations. Intelligent creatures with 2 HD or fewer that fail their Will save must immediately move toward the caster and attack with whatever is at hand. The effect lasts one round. Even creatures that succeed their save are noticeably irritated.

name: UNSEEN SERVANT
description:
Creates an invisible, mindless force that follows the caster's verbal instructions to perform simple tasks — carrying, cleaning, opening unlocked doors, holding items, pouring drinks, and similar chores. It cannot fight, cannot carry more than 20 pounds, and cannot move further than 30 feet from the caster. It has no ability to distinguish what it is handling and follows instructions literally.

*Material component:* A piece of string and a bit of wood.

name: VENTRILOQUISM
description:
Allows the caster to make their voice appear to come from any location within range — a different person, object, direction, or creature. The voice sounds like the caster's own voice unless they mimic effectively. Intelligent observers who are already suspicious of deception may attempt a Will save.

*Material component:* A parchment rolled up into a cone.

name: WALL OF FOG
description: Creates a bank of thick, opaque fog. Creatures inside the fog are blinded and cannot see more than 2 feet in any direction. The fog does not impede movement. Wind disperses it (strong wind in 4 rounds, gale in 1 round). The caster can shape the fog into any configuration within the maximum area.

name: WIZARD MARK
description:
Inscribes the caster's personal mark on any surface — stone, metal, flesh, or other — up to 1 square foot in size. The mark may be visible or invisible, as the caster chooses. An invisible mark is only detectable by detect magic or read magic. The mark cannot be physically erased without magical assistance. Up to 6 additional characters can be added to the mark.

*Material component:* A pinch of diamond dust worth 2 gp.

name: ACID ARROW
description:
Conjures a magical arrow that attacks using the caster's Attack Bonus as if they were a warrior of equal level. The attack receives no modifiers from Strength or Dexterity. A hit deals 2d4 acid damage immediately and continues to deal 2d4 per round thereafter for 1 additional round per 3 caster levels. Exposed items worn by a struck target must save vs. acid each round or be damaged. The acid can be neutralized by alkaline substances.

*Material component:* A dart, powdered rhubarb leaf, and an adder's stomach.

name: ALTER SELF
description: Changes the caster's physical form to that of any roughly humanoid bipedal creature, with height and weight varying up to 50% from the original. If the adopted form has wings, the caster can fly at one-third MV. If the form has gills, the caster can breathe underwater. The caster's AC, attack rolls, saving throws, and abilities remain unchanged — they gain no special abilities, senses, or attacks of the adopted form. Reversion ends the spell; death reverts the caster to their original form.

name: BIND
area: Up to 50 ft of rope or similar
description:
Animates a rope, vine, strip of leather, or similar flexible material. The caster can direct it to coil, tie knots, attach to an anchor, or bind a creature. To bind a creature, the caster makes an attack (using their Attack Bonus as a warrior of equal level) — on a hit, the target is restrained until the binding is broken (requiring a successful Bend Bars check) or the spell ends.

*Material component:* The rope or material to be animated.

name: BLINDNESS
description:
Creates a magical blindness in the target. The effect is permanent until removed by dispel magic, remove curse, or similar magic. A blinded creature suffers all standard blindness penalties: −4 to attack rolls, −4 to Reflex saves, AC reduced by 4, loses Dexterity AC bonus.

*Reversed — Deafness:* Permanently deafens the target. A deaf creature suffers −1 to surprise rolls, −1 to initiative, and 20% spell failure on verbal-component spells.

name: BLUR
description: Distorts the caster's outline and position, imposing −4 to all attack rolls made against them. Missile attacks suffer an additional −2. The blur also grants a \+1 bonus to all saves against targeted magical effects. A creature making a successful Will save to disbelieve the blur still sees the distortion and continues to suffer the attack penalty at half value (−2).

name: CONTINUAL LIGHT
description:
Creates a permanent light source equal to full daylight in a 60-foot radius. If targeted on a creature's eyes, a failed Fortitude save results in permanent blindness until dispelled. Can be cast on an object to create a permanent light-emitting item.

*Reversed — Continual Darkness:* Creates a permanent 60-foot radius of magical darkness. Infravision does not penetrate it.

name: DARKNESS, 15-FOOT RADIUS
description:
Creates magical darkness that extinguishes all non-magical light within 15 feet. Infravision is not effective within the area. A light spell of equal or lower level negates it. Can be targeted on a creature (which then carries it) or fixed to a point.

*Material component:* A bit of bat fur and a drop of pitch.

name: DEEP POCKETS
description:
Enchants two pockets, pouches, or similar compartments on the caster's clothing. Each treated pocket can hold up to 100 cubic inches of material while weighing no more than 1/10th its actual weight. Contents are not visible from outside. Items retrieved must be specifically named — general rummaging does not work. The pockets collapse and their contents are deposited on the ground if the garment is removed.

*Material component:* The garment to be enchanted, plus a piece of thin, flexible leather.

name: DETECT EVIL
description: The caster senses the presence of evil intent or magical evil within the area ahead. Reveals presence and general direction, not identity or exact location. Creatures with evil alignment detected at the GM's discretion; magical items and creatures with specific evil properties are more reliably detected.

name: DETECT INVISIBILITY
description:
Allows the caster to see any invisible, hidden, ethereal, or astral objects and creatures within the area. Illusions of invisibility are also penetrated. Does not detect hidden characters who are not using magical concealment.

*Material component:* A pinch of talc and powdered silver.

name: ESP
description:
The caster concentrates for one round on a creature within range and reads the surface thoughts of one target. The caster must direct attention to a specific creature and concentrate for an entire round to receive thoughts. The spell does not translate languages — the caster hears thoughts in the target's language and must understand it (or use Comprehend Languages) to interpret them. Creatures with Intelligence 0 cannot be read. Undead and extraplanar creatures are immune.

*Material component:* A copper coin.

name: FLAMING SPHERE
description:
Creates a rolling ball of fire that the caster can direct with a mental command (no action required). Moving it up to 30 feet per round. Any creature that the sphere moves adjacent to or that it rams takes 2d4 fire damage; a Reflex save negates the damage. Flammable materials ignite. The sphere can climb grades up to 45 degrees. Attacking the sphere directly does nothing; it lasts its full duration unless dispelled.

*Material component:* A bit of tallow, a pinch of brimstone, and a dusting of powdered iron.

name: FOG CLOUD
description: Creates a thick bank of fog that reduces visibility to 5 feet within it. Creatures inside have a 75% chance of moving in a random direction each round unless they remain stationary. Can be shaped at casting but cannot be moved. A strong wind disperses it in 2 rounds; gale winds in 1 round.

name: FOOL'S GOLD
description:
Causes copper coins, brass items, or pyrite to appear as gold in every observable respect. Creatures handling the false gold directly receive a Will save modified by Intelligence. Alchemical testing automatically reveals the deception.

*Material component:* Gold dust worth at least 4 gp.

name: FORGET
description: Causes 1–4 creatures to forget events of the past round. If cast on a spellcaster who is currently casting, the spell forces them to forget the spell entirely — it must be re-memorized. The memory loss is not unconsciousness; the target simply has no recollection of the moment.

name: GLITTERDUST
description:
A cloud of golden powder coats all creatures in the area. On a failed Fortitude save, targets are blinded for 1d4 rounds. Regardless of saving throw, invisible creatures become visible — they shed faint golden light for 1d4 rounds per caster level and cannot benefit from invisibility during this time.

*Material component:* Ground mica.

name: HYPNOTIC PATTERN
description:
A shifting pattern of colors and light fascinates creatures who observe it. Up to 24 HD of creatures with 5 HD or fewer that fail their Will save become fascinated — standing transfixed, unable to act but not harmed. The effect ends when the caster stops concentrating. Any physical disturbance breaks the fascination for the affected creature.

*Material component:* A glowing stick, which is not consumed.

name: IMPROVED PHANTASMAL FORCE
description:
As Phantasmal Force, but the illusion includes basic sounds (not voices) and persists for 3 additional rounds after the caster stops concentrating. The illusion cannot simulate tactile sensation or scent.

*Material component:* A bit of fleece.

name: INVISIBILITY
description:
Renders the recipient invisible. Carried equipment and worn items are also invisible. The invisibility ends immediately if the recipient attacks, casts a spell, or performs a hostile action. Non-combat actions (movement, opening doors, picking pockets) do not end the effect. The caster can end their own invisibility at any time as a free action.

*Material component:* An eyelash encased in a bit of gum arabic.

name: IRRITATION
description:
Causes intense itching or a rash on affected creatures. Choose one effect:

*Itching:* Target scratches themselves compulsively for 3 rounds; −4 to attack rolls and saving throws. Any concentration-dependent spell or ability fails automatically.

*Rash:* Victim develops a disfiguring rash lasting 4 days; −1 Charisma for social purposes and −1 to saves against touch-based attacks.

*Material component:* A leaf of poison ivy.

name: KNOCK
description: Opens one or more locked, barred, or magically sealed portals within the area. Functions against locks, bars, bolts, and magical sealing (including wizard lock up to 9th level). Does not bypass physical obstacles or destroy doors — it simply removes the securing mechanisms. One application of knock opens one closure.

name: KNOW ALIGNMENT
description: Reveals the true alignment of one creature or object. Magical items and creatures protected by nondetection or similar spells are shielded. Each round, the caster can examine one additional creature or object.

name: LEPRECHAUN'S TRAP
description:
Creates an illusion of a desired object — a chest of gold, a weapon, a rare artifact — to function as a lure. Any creature that approaches and attempts to interact with the illusory object must succeed on a Will save or become convinced the object is real. Creatures who fail become fixated on acquiring it and will not willingly leave the area for 1d6 rounds.

*Material component:* A small shiny object.

name: LEVITATE
description:
Allows the caster to move themselves, one willing creature, or one object straight up or down at a movement rate of 20 feet per round. Horizontal movement is not provided — the subject must push off walls or similar surfaces. Maximum weight: 100 lbs/level. Unwilling creatures receive a Will save.

*Material component:* Either a small leather loop or a piece of golden wire bent in a circle.

name: LOCATE OBJECT
description:
The caster concentrates on a specific object they have seen before, or on the general type of object, and senses the direction of the nearest such object within range. Does not reveal distance or obstacles. A specific unique item provides a more reliable result than a general type. The spell is blocked by lead sheeting.

*Material component:* A forked twig.

name: MAGIC MOUTH
description:
Enchants a surface with a short recorded message and the conditions for its playback. The caster specifies a trigger (a creature approaching within 30 feet, a password spoken, a specific time, etc.) and a message of up to 25 words per caster level. When triggered, the message plays once in the caster's voice from the location of the object. The magic mouth can be tuned to ignore specific types of creatures.

*Material component:* A small bit of honeycomb and a jade gem worth 10 gp.

name: MIRROR IMAGE
description: Creates 1d4+2 perfect duplicates of the caster that surround them, mimicking movement exactly. Attackers targeting the caster must determine which figure is real — each image has the same AC as the caster. When an image is struck, it disappears. The images are shattered in sequence: each attack hits an image before reaching the caster while any images remain.

name: MISDIRECTION
description: Causes divination magic targeting the affected creature or object to return false information. The caster specifies the false information at casting (false alignment, false location, false magical aura). Detection magic returning results about the target will find what the caster specified rather than the truth.

name: PROTECTION FROM CANTRIPS
description: Protects the target from cantrips and other minor magical effects of 0-level power. Cantrips cast against a protected target simply fail. Provides no protection against 1st-level or higher spells.

name: PYROTECHNICS
description:
Transforms any fire source in range into one of two effects, chosen at casting:

*Fireworks:* A vivid burst of light fills a 120-foot radius for 1 round. Creatures that fail a Fortitude save are blinded for 1d4+1 rounds.

*Choking Smoke:* Produces a thick bank of smoke in a 20-foot-per-level radius for 1d4+4 rounds. Total concealment within; non-wind movement halved.

*Material component:* One fire source (torch, lantern, campfire, or larger) consumed by the spell.

name: RAY OF ENFEEBLEMENT
description: A ray of negative energy strikes the target. A failed Fortitude save reduces the target's Strength by 25% \+ 2% per caster level (maximum 50% reduction). Round down to the nearest whole number. The Strength reduction affects attack rolls, damage, and carrying capacity for the duration.

name: ROPE TRICK
description:
A rope up to 30 feet long rises into the air and disappears into an extradimensional space at its top end. Up to eight man-sized creatures can climb into this space, which provides a safe resting area invisible from the outside. Creatures inside can observe events below and can leave at any time by climbing back down. The rope and the extradimensional space vanish when the duration ends, depositing any occupants at the top of the rope's original position.

*Material component:* Powdered corn and a twisted loop of parchment.

name: SCARE
description:
Causes one creature with fewer than 6 HD to flee in terror. The creature moves away from the caster at double speed and takes no other actions for the duration. A successful Will save negates. Undead are immune.

*Material component:* A bit of bone from an undead creature.

name: SHATTER
description:
Creates a crushing resonance that shatters brittle objects. Against crystalline or glass objects: automatic destruction up to a maximum weight of 1 lb/level. Against larger objects or constructs: all objects in area must save vs. crushing or be destroyed. Against living crystalline creatures: 1d6 damage per level (Fortitude for half).

*Material component:* A chip of mica.

name: SPECTRAL HAND
description: Creates a ghostly floating hand under the caster's control that can deliver touch spells at range. The caster sacrifices 1d4 HP to create the hand (restored when the spell ends). The hand moves at MV 30 ft and counts as the caster for purposes of touch attacks, using the caster's AB \+2. Only touch spells of 4th level or lower can be delivered this way.

name: STINKING CLOUD
description:
Creates a cloud of nauseous vapors. Each creature within the cloud must succeed on a Fortitude save each round or be incapacitated — unable to act except for stumbling movement at half speed. The effect lasts 1d4+1 rounds after leaving the cloud. Wind disperses it in 1 round (strong) or 4 rounds (moderate). The cloud spreads around corners.

*Material component:* A rotten egg or a skunk cabbage leaf.

name: STRENGTH
description:
Increases one creature's Strength. The amount gained depends on the recipient's class: Warriors gain 1d8 points; Priests and Rogues gain 1d6 points; Wizards and Sorcerers gain 1d4 points. Strength cannot exceed 18/00 through this spell (Warriors) or 18 for others. Exceptional Strength on a Warrior is determined by a percentile roll if they didn't already have it.

*Material component:* A few hairs or nails from a strong animal.

name: SUMMON SWARM
description:
Summons a swarm of insects, rats, or similar small creatures to fill the target area. The swarm inflicts 1 point of damage per round to every creature within it. Anyone in the swarm who attacks must make a Will save or flee. The swarm lasts as long as the caster concentrates plus 2 rounds. Dispersing it requires fire or area damage (such as a fireball), not direct attack.

*Material component:* A bit of sugar and blood.

name: UNCONTROLLABLE HIDEOUS LAUGHTER
description:
The target perceives everything as hysterically funny and is overcome with laughter. A failed Will save leaves the target incapacitated — unable to act, falling prone, and laughing uncontrollably. The save is modified by the difference in Intelligence between the target and caster. Very high-Intelligence creatures are less susceptible; those with Intelligence significantly higher than the caster receive a bonus.

*Material component:* A bit of feather and small lumps of wood.

name: WEB
description:
Creates a tangled mass of thick, sticky strands. Creatures caught in the web are restrained. Strong creatures (Strength 13 or higher) can break free in 2 rounds; weaker creatures require 4 rounds. Creatures with Strength 3 or less cannot escape without magical assistance. The web is anchored to two solid surfaces; without anchoring, it collapses. It is flammable and burns in 2 rounds if ignited, dealing 2d4 fire damage to trapped creatures.

*Material component:* A bit of spider web.

name: WHISPERING WIND
description: Sends a message of up to 25 words to a location the caster has visited, where it repeats once at a whisper volume. The wind travels at up to 1 mile per turn. The caster can specify a trigger condition instead of immediate delivery (e.g., "play when someone opens the door").

name: WIZARD LOCK
description: Magically seals a door, gate, or similar portal. The casting wizard can freely bypass the lock. Other wizards of at least 3 levels higher than the caster can pass through without disrupting the lock. Knock or dispel magic removes the seal permanently. Physically forcing a wizard-locked portal requires a Strength check with a penalty equal to the caster's level.

name: BLINK
description: The caster randomly teleports 2 feet per caster level in a random direction at the end of each round. Each random blink has equal probability of occurring before or after any attack directed at the caster during that round — effectively giving the caster a 50% miss chance against melee and ranged attacks (this does not stack with other evasion bonuses). Roll 1d8 at the end of each round for direction (1 \= north, and so on clockwise) and 1d6 for height (1–2 \= down, 3–5 \= level, 6 \= up).

name: CLAIRAUDIENCE
description:
The caster hears sounds from any location they have personally visited, without regard for distance (but blocked by lead sheeting). The caster concentrates to maintain the link. They hear whatever could be heard normally at the target location.

*Material component:* A small horn of at least 100 gp value.

name: CLAIRVOYANCE
description:
As Clairaudience, but sight rather than hearing. The caster sees from any location they have personally visited. The viewpoint can be adjusted within the location but cannot be moved to a new location during the duration.

*Material component:* A small crystal ball.

name: DELUDE
description: Alters the caster's apparent alignment. Anyone using Know Alignment, Detect Evil, or similar divination on the caster detects the false alignment specified at casting. The caster's behavior and actions are not affected — only the magical reading.

name: DISPEL MAGIC
description:
Attempts to remove magical effects from a target area, object, or creature. For each effect targeted, the chance of success is: 50% \+ 5% per level the caster is above the spell's original caster, − 5% per level the caster is below the original caster. Minimum chance: 5%. Maximum: 95%. Spells cast by creatures of greater power are harder to dispel.

Dispel magic cannot affect artifacts or items of divine origin. It does not affect a wild magic surge already in progress.

name: EXPLOSIVE RUNES
description: Inscribes nearly invisible runes on any readable surface. When anyone other than the caster reads the text, the runes explode for 6d4+6 damage to the reader (no save) and 6d4+6 fire damage to everyone within 10 feet (Reflex for half). Only read magic or a successful detect magic reveals the runes before triggering.

name: FEIGN DEATH
description: Puts the recipient into a state of deep suspended animation indistinguishable from death by any non-magical examination. The subject breathes so slowly that no breath is detectable. Normal healing continues during the spell. The recipient is aware of their surroundings but cannot act, and is immune to paralysis, poison, and level drain while feigning death. They can be woken early by a sharp tap.

name: FIREBALL
description:
A streaking mote of fire detonates at the target point. Deals 1d6 fire damage per caster level (maximum 10d6). The blast expands to fill the available space — it may expand through corridors or rooms if the maximum volume cannot be achieved at the target point. All unattended flammable objects in the area ignite. Exposed items on creatures that fail their save must save vs. magic fire.

*Material component:* A tiny ball of bat guano and sulfur.

name: FLAME ARROW
description:
Creates up to one fire-tipped arrow per caster level. Each arrow deals \+1d6 fire damage in addition to normal arrow damage on a hit. Alternatively, the caster can conjure a single fiery bolt that deals 1d6 fire damage per level (maximum 10d6) in a jet to a target 30 yards away (Reflex save for half).

*Material component:* A drop of oil and a small piece of flint.

name: FLY
description:
Grants flight at a movement rate of MV 18 with Good maneuverability. The exact duration is concealed from the caster — only the GM knows when the extra 1d6 rounds will expire. The recipient can carry normal loads.

*Material component:* A wing feather from any bird.

name: GUST OF WIND
description:
A strong gust of wind disperses gases, vapors, and clouds in its path instantly. Small creatures (halfling-sized or smaller) must succeed on a Fortitude save or be knocked prone and pushed 1d4 × 10 feet back. Tiny creatures are blown away entirely. Missile attacks through the area suffer −4 to attack rolls. Does not affect most undead or constructs.

*Material component:* A legume seed.

name: HASTE
description:
Each affected creature gains an extra action each round — effectively doubling their normal attack rate and allowing them to move at double speed. Creatures affected by haste age one additional year at the spell's conclusion. Haste and slow cancel each other exactly.

*Material component:* A shaving of licorice root.

name: HOLD PERSON
description:
Paralyzes one to four humanoid creatures. The caster can target one creature (−2 penalty to the target's save), two creatures (normal save), three creatures (+2 bonus to all saves), or four creatures (+3 bonus to all saves). A paralyzed creature cannot move or act but remains conscious.

*Material component:* A small, straight piece of iron.

name: HOLD UNDEAD
description:
Paralyzes one to three undead creatures. Divide caster level by HD of undead to determine base hold chance. Undead with more HD than the caster's level are unaffected.

*Material component:* A pinch of sulfur and powdered bone.

name: ILLUSORY SCRIPT
description:
Creates writing that appears as a meaningless scrawl to all but the intended reader. The caster designates one recipient — anyone else who reads it (fail Will save) is confused for 5d4 rounds. The intended recipient reads the true text normally. Read magic does not reveal the hidden script to unauthorized readers.

*Material component:* A lead-based ink with cold fire (10 gp).

name: INFRAVISION
description:
Grants infravision to 60 feet to a creature that does not already possess it, or extends existing infravision by 60 feet. Functions identically to ancestral infravision.

*Material component:* Either a pinch of carrot or a gemstone of at least 50 gp.

name: INVISIBILITY, 10-FOOT RADIUS
description:
As Invisibility, but affects all creatures within 10 feet of the target at casting. The radius moves with the target. Creatures leaving the radius become visible. Creatures entering the radius after casting do not become invisible. The effect on each creature ends individually when that creature attacks or performs a hostile action.

*Material component:* An eyelash encased in gum arabic.

name: ITEM
description:
Shrinks a non-magical object to 1/12th of its normal size while simultaneously changing it to cloth-like consistency. The miniaturized object can be carried in a pocket. When spoken to by the caster, the object returns to its normal size and composition. Reverting inside a confined space creates obvious structural problems.

*Material component:* A piece of the item to be affected.

name: LEPRECHAUN'S TINY HUT
description:
Creates a small hemisphere of magical force centered on the caster, large enough for up to seven man-sized creatures. Inside is comfortable at any external temperature from −30°C to 55°C. External observers cannot see inside (it appears as a sphere of slightly hazy air). The hut can withstand winds up to hurricane force. The caster can allow or deny entry at will.

*Material component:* A small crystal bead.

name: LIGHTNING BOLT
description:
A bolt of lightning strikes from the caster's hand through to its maximum range, dealing 1d6 lightning damage per caster level (maximum 10d6). If the bolt reaches a solid obstacle before reaching its full length, it ricochets back along the same line, potentially hitting the caster. Both the outbound and the ricocheting bolt deal the full damage.

*Material component:* A bit of fur and a glass or crystal rod.

name: MINUTE METEORS
description:
Creates 1 small flaming sphere per caster level (maximum 10). Each sphere can be hurled as a free action (one per round, or all at once as a single action at −1 per attack after the first). Each strike deals 1d4 fire damage and ignites flammable materials. Spheres that miss explode harmlessly.

*Material component:* Nitre and sulphur formed into a ball.

name: MONSTER SUMMONING I
description:
Summons 1d3 creatures of approximately 1 HD from a predetermined list. These creatures fight for the caster until defeated or dismissed. If the summoned creatures are turned or flee morale, they disappear. See Appendix C for summoned creature tables.

*Material component:* A tiny bag and a small candle.

name: NON-DETECTION
description:
Shields the target from all divination magic. Detect magic, clairvoyance, ESP, locate object, and similar spells all fail against the warded target. A caster attempting to use a higher-level divination against the ward must succeed on a Will save vs. the original caster's level.

*Material component:* A pinch of diamond dust worth 300 gp.

name: PHANTOM STEED
description: Creates a quasi-real black horse with white mane. It has AC 16, 7+7 HP, and a MV equal to 4 × the caster's level (maximum MV 48, equivalent to God-like speed class). The steed can walk on water (at half speed in rough conditions), move at speed over sand and mud without sinking, and jump over water up to 24 feet wide at full speed. Only the caster can ride it. If it takes any damage, it disappears.

name: PROTECTION FROM EVIL, 10-FOOT RADIUS
description: As Protection from Evil, but extends to all creatures within 10 feet of the recipient. The ward moves with the recipient.

name: PROTECTION FROM MISSILES
description:
Renders the recipient immune to non-magical, non-siege missile attacks — arrows, bolts, sling stones, hurled weapons, and similar ranged projectiles. Magical missiles (magic missile, flame arrow) bypass this protection. Siege weapons and thrown boulders bypass it as well.

*Material component:* A piece of tortoiseshell or turtle shell.

name: SECRET PAGE
description:
Conceals the contents of one page — the true text appears as something else (a meaningless design, different text) to all observers. The caster reads the real page normally; all others see the false text. The concealment is dispelled by read magic, dispel magic, or the caster speaking a word of reversal.

*Material component:* Powdered herring scales.

name: SEPIA SNAKE SIGIL
description:
Creates a small sepia serpent drawn on a surface that animates and strikes the next creature to view it (other than the caster) who fails a Fortitude save. On a failed save, the target is encased in stasis — suspended in a golden amber-like field, unable to act or be harmed, for 1d4 days \+ 1 day/level. The target appears dead and does not age during the stasis.

*Material component:* 100 gp of powdered amber, a snake scale, and a pinch of mushroom powder.

name: SLOW
description:
Affected creatures are slowed to half their normal movement rate. They can make only one attack per round regardless of their normal attack rate. Initiative rolls suffer −2. Haste and slow cancel each other exactly.

*Material component:* A drop of molasses.

name: SPECTRAL FORCE
description:
As Improved Phantasmal Force, with the additional ability to incorporate thermal sensations (heat and cold). Illusions created with Spectral Force are significantly harder to disbelieve because they engage multiple senses.

*Material component:* A bit of fleece.

name: SUGGESTION
description:
Plants a reasonable-sounding suggestion in the target's mind — a single course of action phrased as a request. The suggestion cannot be obviously harmful or suicidal. Affected targets carry out the suggestion as if it were their own idea for the duration, then resume normal behavior. The suggestion must be phrased in a language the target understands.

*Material component:* A snake's tongue and honeycomb.

name: TONGUES
description:
Allows the caster to speak and understand any language they hear, including exotic tongues of animals and monsters. Does not enable writing, only spoken communication.

*Reversed — Babble:* Subjects 1d6 creatures in 30 feet to speaking in an incomprehensible mix of languages. Affected creatures cannot communicate coherently for the duration (Will negates per creature).

name: VAMPIRIC TOUCH
description: The caster's touch drains 1d6 HP per 2 caster levels (maximum 6d6) from the target into themselves. The stolen HP are added to the caster's current HP and can exceed their maximum, though excess HP dissipate after 1 hour. Only one successful touch is needed to discharge the spell; unused charges dissipate at duration's end.

name: WATER BREATHING
description:
Grants the ability to breathe water as easily as air for the duration. The recipient can still breathe air normally.

*Reversed — Air Breathing:* Grants a water-breathing creature the ability to breathe air for the duration.

*Material component:* A short reed or hollow bone.

name: WIND WALL
area: 10 ft wide × 5 ft high × 2 ft/level long
description:
Creates a vertical curtain of strong wind that deflects missile attacks (small and medium missiles automatically veer off; larger missiles have a 30% chance of deflection). Gases and gaseous creatures cannot pass through. Creatures attempting to pass through must succeed on a Fortitude save or be pushed back. Flying creatures entering the wall are buffeted and move at half speed.

*Material component:* A tiny fan and a feather.

name: WRAITH FORM
description: Transforms the caster into a ghostly, semi-transparent form that can pass through solid objects at MV 12\. The caster cannot harm or be harmed by physical objects while in wraith form, but can only manipulate the physical world if they solidify (ending the spell). The form is visible as a faint outline. Magic affects the wraith form normally. Can pass through any opening a wisp of air could pass through.

name: BLACK TENTACLES
description:
Causes a mass of writhing black tentacles to erupt from any surface — floor, wall, ground, even water — within range. The spell generates 1d4 tentacles plus one per caster level, each 10 feet long, AC 14, with hit points equal to the caster's maximum. The tentacles are mindless and strike any creature within reach at random. A creature struck must succeed on a Reflex save (modified by Dexterity) or be constricted: taking 2d4 damage on the round they are seized, then 3d4 per round until the tentacle is destroyed or the spell ends. A successful save reduces the initial hit to 1d4 with no constriction.

*Material component:* A piece of tentacle from a giant octopus or squid.

name: CHARM MONSTER
description:
Functions as Charm Person but affects any living creature regardless of type or size. The GM rolls 2d4 when the spell is cast. The caster may choose to target one creature of any HD, or may target a group of creatures each with fewer than 4 HD whose combined HD total does not exceed the 2d4 roll. Aelár and half-aelár retain their normal resistance.

The charm may end at any point; the GM checks secretly each week, with odds based on the charmed creature's level.

| Level or HD | Weekly Chance of Breaking |
| ----- | ----- |
| 1st or up to 2 HD | 5% |
| 2nd or up to 3+2 HD | 10% |
| 3rd or up to 4+4 HD | 15% |
| 4th or up to 6 HD | 25% |
| 5th or up to 7+2 HD | 35% |
| 6th or up to 8+4 HD | 45% |
| 7th or up to 10 HD | 60% |
| 8th or up to 12 HD | 75% |
| 9th or over 12 HD | 90% |

name: CONFUSION
description:
Affects 1d4 creatures plus one per two caster levels. Each affected creature rolls a Will save at −2 (modified by Wisdom) each round, on failure:

| 1d10 | Action |
| ----- | ----- |
| 1 | Wanders away for the remainder of the duration |
| 2–6 | Stands confused for one round |
| 7–9 | Attacks the nearest creature for one round |
| 10 | Acts normally for one round |

A creature that rolls a 1 continues wandering for the full duration without further checks. Wandering creatures move as far from the caster as possible. Confused creatures attacked will fight back according to their basic nature.

*Material component:* Three nutshells.

name: CONTAGION
description: Inflicts a debilitating disease on the target. On a failed Fortitude save, the victim develops painful symptoms immediately — boils, lesions, cramps — reducing Strength, Dexterity, and Charisma by 2 points each, and imposing a −2 penalty to attack rolls. Without magical treatment (Cure Disease) or one to three weeks of complete bed rest, the disease progresses, imposing an additional −1 to those scores every two days. A creature reduced to 0 in any affected attribute dies.

name: DETECT SCRYING
description:
The caster becomes aware of any magical surveillance operating within the area — scrying, clairvoyance, crystal balls, ESP, and similar effects. When a scrying attempt is detected, the caster has a 1% chance per caster level (maximum 95%) to identify the scryer and the scryer's location as a mental image. The spell does not end or disrupt the scrying.

*Material component:* A small mirror and a copper wire coiled in a circle.

name: DIG
description:
Excavates one 5-foot cube of earth, sand, or mud per round. The removed material scatters evenly around the pit. Pits collapse based on depth and material: solid earth at 25 feet (and every 5 feet after), sand at 10 feet (every 5 feet after), mud every 5 feet. Any creature within 1 foot of the pit edge must succeed on a Reflex save or fall in. Creatures that stumble toward an undetected pit save vs. spell.

Against creatures made of earth or stone, dig deals 4d6 damage (Reflex for half).

*Material component:* A miniature shovel and a tiny bucket, held during the spell and consumed on completion.

name: DIMENSION DOOR
description: The caster teleports instantaneously to any point within range, carrying up to 500 lbs of nonliving or 250 lbs of living material. The destination can be visualized or stated as direction and distance. Arrival is always accurate, but if the destination is solid rock or occupied, the caster is trapped in the Astral Plane in a stunned state until extracted by Dismissal or equivalent magic. After arrival, the caster is disoriented for one round and cannot act.

name: EMOTION
description:
Generates one chosen emotional response in all creatures in the area. Unwilling creatures save. Effects:

*Courage:* \+1 to attack, \+3 to damage, 5 temporary HP; combatants drop shields, never check morale, fight only in melee. Counters and is countered by Fear.

*Fear:* Targets flee at full speed for 2d4 rounds. Counters and is countered by Courage.

*Friendship:* Reaction rolls improve by one step. Counters Hate.

*Happiness:* \+4 to reaction rolls; targets highly unlikely to initiate attack. Counters Sadness.

*Hate:* Reaction rolls worsen by one step. Counters Friendship.

*Hope:* \+2 to saves, attack rolls, damage, and morale. Counters Hopelessness.

*Hopelessness:* 50% chance to surrender, 25% to do nothing, 25% to retreat for 2d4 rounds. Counters Hope.

*Sadness:* −1 to surprise rolls, −1 to initiative. Counters Happiness.

name: ENCHANTED WEAPON
description:
Temporarily enchants one medium or large weapon, or up to two small weapons, to function as \+1 magical weapons. Multiple castings on the same weapons can stack to a maximum of \+3. This spell can also improve an existing magical weapon to a combined maximum of \+3. Ammunition and hurled weapons lose the enchantment after the first hit; melee weapons and bows retain it for the full duration.

This spell is also a step in the crafting of permanent magical weapons — see Chapter 13\.

*Material component:* Powdered lime and carbon.

name: ENERVATION
description: The caster fires a crackling bolt of negative energy. On a failed Fortitude save, the target loses one level per four caster levels (see Energy Drain in Chapter 10 for full level loss effects). A creature drained to 0 levels must make a System Shock check or die; survivors fall comatose. Lost levels return when the duration expires or after 6 hours of undisturbed rest. Lost spells must be re-memorized. Undead are immune.

name: EXTENSION I
description: Extends the duration of any previously cast 1st through 3rd level wizard spell (with a non-instantaneous duration that does not require concentration) by 50%. Must be cast within the first round after the spell to be extended. Cannot stack with other Extension spells; each spell may be extended only once.

name: FEAR
area: 60-ft cone, 30 ft wide at the end
description:
Creates a wave of terror that moves outward from the caster. Creatures that fail their Will save flee at maximum speed for 2d4 rounds, dropping items they hold. A creature successfully saved becomes frightened but does not flee. Magic, cold iron, and certain other substances may suppress the fear at the GM's discretion.

*Material component:* The heart of a hen or a white feather.

name: FIRE CHARM
description:
Surrounds a non-magical fire source with a veil of hypnotic multicolored flame. Creatures observing the flames must save or become transfixed, after which they become susceptible to a single individualized suggestion of up to 12 words (as the Suggestion spell, maximum 1 hour duration). Charmed creatures break free if attacked or if their view of the flames is obstructed.

*Material component:* A small piece of multicolored silk thrown into the fire.

name: FIRE SHIELD
description:
Surrounds the caster in thin wispy flames that shed half the light of a torch. Choose one version at memorization:

*Warm Shield (blue or violet):* Protects against cold attacks — \+2 to saves against cold, half damage on a failed save, no damage on success. Melee attackers take fire damage equal to their attack damage. Fire-based attacks deal double normal damage to the caster.

*Chill Shield (blue or green):* Protects against fire attacks — \+2 to saves against fire, half damage on failed save, no damage on success. Melee attackers take cold damage equal to their attack damage. Cold-based attacks deal double normal damage to the caster.

*Material component:* For warm shield, a bit of phosphorus. For chill shield, a live firefly or glowworm, or the tails of four dead ones.

name: FIRE TRAP
description:
Inscribes a magical trap on a closeable object — a book, bottle, chest, door. When opened by any unauthorized creature, the trap explodes for 1d4 \+ 1 per caster level fire damage in a 5-foot radius. Knock does not trigger or remove the trap. A failed attempt to remove it triggers detonation. Only one magical trap, ward, or lock may be placed on an object at a time.

*Material component:* Sulphur or saltpeter traced around the closure and touched to the object.

name: FUMBLE
description:
Causes one creature per two caster levels in the area to become stumbling and fumbling with all actions. Affected creatures suffer −4 to attack rolls and Dexterity-based checks, have a 50% chance per round of dropping any held item, and move at half speed. A successful Will save (at −2 for creatures with fewer HD than the caster) means the creature is merely slowed: −2 to attacks, half speed, no dropping.

*Material component:* A cobblestone and a pinch of sand.

name: HALLUCINATORY TERRAIN
description:
Disguises terrain within the area to appear as another type of natural terrain — hills masquerading as open fields, a swamp appearing as meadow, or vice versa. The illusion has visual, auditory, olfactory, tactile, and thermal components. Buildings, creatures, and equipment are not affected. A creature physically passing through terrain that contradicts its appearance (walking into an "open field" that is actually a bog) receives a strong bonus to disbelief. The illusion persists for those who believe it even after individual creatures disbelieve.

*Material component:* A stone, a twig, and a piece of green plant.

name: ICE STORM
description:
Choose one effect at casting:

*Hail:* Large hailstones rain for one round, dealing 3d10 damage to all creatures in the area. Negates any Heat Metal damage on the same round.

*Sleet:* Driving sleet falls for the full duration, blinding creatures in the area, extinguishing open flames, and creating icy ground. Creatures in combat or moving through the area are slowed to half speed and have a 50% chance of falling prone each round.

*Material component:* A few drops of water and a pinch of dust.

name: ILLUSORY WALL
description:
Creates a permanent illusion of a wall, floor, ceiling, or similar architectural surface, complete with texture, color, and shadow. The illusory surface has no substance — creatures who disbelieve can pass through freely. A creature that touches the surface immediately gets a disbelief save.

*Material component:* A small sheet of parchment painted with the desired appearance.

name: IMPROVED INVISIBILITY
description: As Invisibility, but the effect does not end when the recipient attacks or takes other hostile actions. The recipient remains invisible while fighting, casting spells, or otherwise acting. Attacks against an invisible target suffer the standard −4 penalty, and the target's attacks gain \+4.

name: LEPRECHAUN'S SECURE SHELTER
description:
Conjures a sturdy one-story structure of opaque, waterproof, fire-resistant material in an unoccupied space. The interior contains simple furnishings for up to four creatures, with beds, a table, a locked door, and shuttered windows. The structure is impervious to normal weather and provides shelter from severe cold or heat. It vanishes and all contents fall to the ground when the duration expires.

*Material component:* A miniature clay model of a house.

name: MAGIC MIRROR
description:
Transforms a suitable mirror into a scrying device that functions as a crystal ball, allowing the caster to observe events at a chosen location. The mirror must be polished silver worth at least 1,000 gp. When activated, the caster may use the mirror's surface to observe a known location. The caster can hear sounds at the scried location if they are significantly loud. Lead sheeting and magical protections block the effect.

*Material components:* A silver mirror, powdered eye of a hawk, nitric acid, and gold dust to frame the mirror.

name: MASS MORPH
description: Temporarily alters the appearance of willing creatures within the area, giving each the semblance of a different creature of similar size. Unlike Alter Self, the change is purely visual — no capabilities of the new form are gained. The caster must designate the new appearance of each affected creature at casting and cannot change it during the duration.

name: MINOR CREATION
description:
Creates a non-magical object made entirely of vegetable matter — wood, rope, cloth, and similar organic materials. The created object is permanent for the spell's duration, then crumbles to dust. It is physically genuine: it can bear weight, hold liquid, and be handled normally. It cannot duplicate the appearance of a specific object well enough to fool a knowledgeable observer, and magical properties cannot be created.

*Material component:* A tiny piece of the type of material to be created.

name: MINOR GLOBE OF INVULNERABILITY
description:
Creates a shimmering sphere that prevents all spells of 3rd level or lower from passing inward. Spells cast from within the globe can pass outward normally. Higher-level spells, physical attacks, and missile weapons pass through freely.

*Material component:* A small crystal sphere.

name: MNEMONIC ENHANCER
description:
Allows the caster to memorize three additional spell levels beyond their normal capacity — for example, three extra 1st-level spells, or one 2nd-level and one 1st-level, or one 3rd-level spell. Alternatively, the caster can retain any three spell levels already cast that day, recovering them as if they were still memorized. Both uses require a full turn of casting and are lost if the spell's duration expires before use.

*Material component:* A piece of string, a small coin, and an octopus tentacle.

name: MONSTER SUMMONING II
description:
As Monster Summoning I, but summons 1d3 creatures of approximately 2 HD. See Appendix C for summoned creature tables.

*Material component:* A tiny bag and a small candle.

name: PHANTASMAL KILLER
description: Creates a manifestation of the target's deepest terror visible only to them. The target first makes a Will save — on a success, the image is recognized as illusory and the spell ends. On a failure, the creature perceives the phantasm as completely real and must immediately make a Fortitude save or die from shock. If the Fortitude save succeeds, the creature takes 3d6 damage from terror.

name: PLANT GROWTH
description:
Causes normal plants to grow to overgrown profusion, creating a dense tangle. Any creature moving through the affected area is slowed to one-third normal movement and cannot charge or run. The growth does not harm the plants and is permanent until removed by mundane clearing (taking one day per square yard of dense growth) or Transmute Rock to Mud, Disintegrate, or a similar mass effect.

Alternatively, the caster can increase the size and productivity of a crop or garden, improving its yield by up to 50%.

name: POLYMORPH OTHER
description:
Permanently transforms one creature into any other creature of the same approximate HD. The target's mind does not change — it retains its intelligence and memories — but the new body's instincts and nature exert constant pressure. For every week in the new form, the creature makes a Will save or lose one point of Intelligence as the new form's psychology asserts itself. At 0 Intelligence, the creature becomes the new form in mind as well as body.

The new form's AC, movement, attack forms, and physical capabilities replace the original's. Spell ability cannot be exercised in a form that cannot speak or gesture. The caster cannot specify the new form down to individuals — they can specify species, not specific creature.

*Material component:* A caterpillar cocoon.

name: POLYMORPH SELF
description: The caster assumes the physical form of any creature they can imagine, with HD ranging from 1⁄4 to twice the caster's level. The caster does not gain the new form's special attacks, magical abilities, or supernatural powers — only physical capabilities: movement modes, physical form, size. The caster retains their own hit points, saving throws, and spellcasting ability (provided the new form can speak and gesture). The caster can change forms freely during the duration.

name: RAINBOW PATTERN
description:
A shifting, spiraling pattern of light fascinates up to 24 HD of creatures that observe it. Fascinated creatures are transfixed, unable to act while the caster moves or adjusts the pattern. The effect lasts as long as the caster maintains it (no concentration required, but the caster must move to maintain the pattern). Creatures that follow the pattern into danger do so willingly — the caster can slowly lead them. Physical disturbances break individual creatures' fascination. A light spell cast into the pattern neutralizes it.

*Material component:* A piece of crystal or a clear gem.

name: REMOVE CURSE
description:
Removes one curse from a creature or object. Does not automatically undo the effects of a curse — it removes the magical binding, but physical changes caused by the curse may require additional magic to reverse. Against a cursed item, this allows the carrier to remove or discard the item, but does not make the item non-cursed. Does not remove all curses simultaneously; one casting removes one curse.

*Reversed — Bestow Curse:* The caster touches a creature and imposes one of the following effects (choose at casting): −4 to a specified ability score; −4 to all attack rolls and saving throws; 50% chance each round of being unable to act. The curse is permanent until removed. The target receives a Will save.

name: RESILIENT SPHERE
description:
Creates a globe of shimmering force around one creature. The sphere is impervious to all damage and cannot be breached from without or within by any physical or magical means less than Disintegrate. Creatures inside can breathe and see normally but cannot act outside the sphere or use spell or item effects through it. The sphere can be rolled but not flown; it moves at the target's normal ground speed. A Reflex save allows a willing creature to step outside the range before the sphere closes.

*Material component:* A small, hollow glass sphere.

name: SHADOW MONSTERS
description: Creates semi-real monsters formed from shadow matter. Each creature has 20% of the normal HP of the creature it imitates. Creatures that disbelieve treat the shadow monsters as having AC 10 and dealing 20% normal damage. Creatures that believe the monsters are real take full illusory damage (see Illusions, Chapter 14 opener).

name: SHOUT
description: The caster emits a cone of devastating sound. All creatures in the cone take 2d6 sonic damage. Creatures failing a Fortitude save are deafened for 2d6 rounds. Crystalline objects in the area must save vs. crushing or shatter. Fragile structures take double damage. A creature using Shout in a confined space risks deafening themselves as well — a Will save is required to use this spell successfully indoors.

name: SOLID FOG
description:
Creates a thick, nearly tangible bank of yellowish fog. The fog is so dense that creatures within it move at one-third normal speed and cannot charge or run. Visibility is limited to 2 feet. Unlike Fog Cloud, the fog cannot be dispersed by wind — only fire (including Fireball and Flame Strike) can destroy it.

*Material component:* A pinch of dried peas and powdered animal hoof.

name: STONESKIN
description:
Renders the recipient immune to all mundane physical attacks — non-magical weapons simply do not harm them. The protection absorbs 1d4+2 hits per 4 caster levels (maximum 1d4+6 at 16th level), after which the spell expires. Magical weapons, spells, and area effects bypass the protection. Touch spells that involve physical contact are blocked.

*Material component:* Granite and diamond dust worth 250 gp.

name: VACANCY
description:
Makes an area appear empty and uninhabited to observers from outside the area. Creatures inside become invisible and inaudible to those outside — not silenced, but undetected. Observers from outside see an empty room, a vacant camp, a deserted courtyard. The effect is an illusion of vacancy over reality, not a full concealment — creatures with true sight or detect magic see through it normally.

*Material component:* Two small pieces of empty cloth.

name: WALL OF FIRE
description:
Creates a sheetof magical fire. The wall can be a flat plane up to 20 feet long and 20 feet high, or a ring up to 10 feet in diameter per two caster levels. Creatures passing through the wall take 2d6+4 fire damage; creatures within 10 feet take 1d6+2 per round from radiant heat. The wall is opaque — creatures on one side cannot see the other. Can be shaped to exclude areas.

*Material component:* Phosphorus.

name: WALL OF ICE
description:
Creates a sheet of ice 1 inch thick per caster level. The wall can be a flat plane (10 sq ft per level) or a dome hemisphere (10-ft radius per 4 levels). Creatures passing through take 2d6 cold damage, plus an additional 1d6 if they lack fire protection. Creatures of cold origin cannot pass through it at all. Fire effects striking the wall remove 1d6 HP of wall per die of fire damage — a 6d6 fireball removes 6d6 HP of wall.

*Material component:* A small piece of white quartz or crystal.

name: WIZARD EYE
description:
Creates an invisible, immobile magical sensor that the caster can direct within range. The eye moves at MV 9, cannot pass through solid objects but can slip through any opening large enough to be seen through (keyholes, cracks). The caster sees through the eye as normal vision (infravision if the caster has it). The eye creates a visible sensor that can be detected by detect magic and dispelled by dispel magic. The caster must remain stationary to control it.

*Material component:* A piece of glass or crystal lens.

name: ADVANCED ILLUSION
description:
As Spectral Force but requires no concentration — the illusion runs on a pre-programmed sequence set by the caster at casting. Visual, auditory, olfactory, and thermal components. Creatures attempting to disbelieve make a Will save modified by Wisdom.

*Material component:* A bit of fleece and a pinch of sand.

name: AIRY WATER
description:
Creates a breathable environment within a bubble of transformed water. Creatures inside move and breathe as if in air. Water-breathing creatures cannot breathe inside the bubble and naturally avoid it, though they may enter willingly. The bubble moves with the caster and cannot be cast underwater while silenced — but the verbal component is minimal enough to be spoken quietly while submerged.

*Material component:* A small handful of alkaline or bromine salts.

name: ANIMAL GROWTH
area: Up to 8 animals in a 20-ft square
description:
Doubles the size of up to eight animals within the area. Doubled animals have doubled HD, HP, and damage. Their movement and AC are unchanged. Their weight increases eightfold. The reverse, Shrink Animal, halves size, HD, HP, and damage.

*Material component:* A pinch of powdered bone.

name: ANIMATE DEAD
description:
Animates the bones or bodies of the recently dead as skeletons or zombies. The total HD of undead created cannot exceed the caster's level. Humans and humanoids with fewer than 2 HD in life become 1 HD skeletons or 2 HD zombies. The animated undead obey the caster's simple commands. They are not affected by Dispel Magic but can be turned or destroyed.

The ethical weight of this spell is substantial and varies by culture and tradition. Creating undead from unclaimed corpses may be legally acceptable in some regions; animating individuals without consent, or using bodies claimed by a faith's burial rites, is treated as desecration in most civilized lands. Very few Clerics of good or neutral alignment will animate humanoid dead without first seeking express consent through Speak With Dead.

*Material component:* A drop of blood and a pinch of bone dust.

name: AVOIDANCE
description:
Enchants an object to be avoided. Any creature approaching within 10 feet that fails a Fortitude save is repelled and cannot move closer for the spell's duration — they simply do not want to approach and will find reasons to leave. The spell cannot repel creatures of greater HD than the caster's level.

*Material component:* A small magnet and a repellant (garlic, sulfur, or similar).

name: BIG INTERPOSING HAND
description:
Creates a Large-sized, human-shaped hand of magical force. The hand interposes itself between the caster and one designated opponent within range, moving each round to maintain its position. The hand does not attack — it blocks, providing full cover (+4 AC) and absorbing attacks directed at the caster. The hand has AC 10, HP equal to the caster's maximum, and saves as the caster. It can hold back creatures of up to Large size. Slows creatures of Huge size by 50% if they weigh more than 900 kg.

*Material component:* A soft glove.

name: CHAOS
description:
Functions as Confusion except only warriors, enchanters, non-intelligent creatures, and creatures with Intelligence above 20 or with more HD than the caster are allowed a Will save.

*Material component:* A small bronze disc and a small iron rod.

name: CLOUD KILL
description: A drifting mass of sickly yellowish-green vapors, heavier than air, moves from the caster at 10 feet per round. Creatures with fewer than 4+1 HD caught in the cloud die instantly. Creatures with 4+1 to 5+1 HD must make Fortitude saves (−4 penalty) each round or die; those with 6 HD or more take 1d10 damage per round. Moderate wind deflects the cloud; strong wind disperses it in 4 rounds; extremely thick vegetation disperses it in 2 rounds. Cannot be cast underwater.
