name: CONE OF COLD
description:
A blast of intense cold erupts from the caster's hands. Deals 1d4+1 cold damage per caster level to all creatures in the cone.

*Material component:* A small glass or crystal cone (shattered upon casting).

name: CONJURE ELEMENTAL
description:
Summons an 8 HD elemental — Air, Earth, Fire, or Water — as chosen when the spell is memorized (each type may only be conjured once per day). The caster must maintain concentration to control the elemental; if concentration breaks or the caster is damaged, the elemental turns on the caster. The elemental can be directed within 30 yards per caster level; beyond this it goes rogue. There is a 5% cumulative chance per round after the first that the elemental breaks free regardless of concentration.

*Material component:* Appropriate to elemental type — incense for Air, soft clay and earth for Earth, sulphur and phosphorus at a bonfire for Fire, water and sand at a body of water for Water.

name: CONTACT OTHER PLANE
description:
Allows contact with an extra-planar being to ask one question per two caster levels. The being answers in single words. The caster risks temporary insanity based on how distant a plane they contact. Modifiers to the insanity check are reduced by 5% per point of Intelligence above 15\.

| Plane | Insanity Check | Duration | Knowledge | Veracity |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Elemental | 20% | 1 week | 55% | 62% |
| Other Inner | 25% | 2 weeks | 60% | 65% |
| Astral | 30% | 3 weeks | 65% | 67% |
| Outer, Int 19 | 35% | 4 weeks | 70% | 70% |
| Outer, Int 20 | 40% | 5 weeks | 75% | 73% |
| Outer, Int 21 | 45% | 6 weeks | 80% | 75% |
| Outer, Int 22 | 50% | 7 weeks | 85% | 78% |
| Outer, Int 23 | 55% | 8 weeks | 90% | 81% |
| Outer, Int 24 | 60% | 9 weeks | 95% | 85% |
| Outer, Int 25 | 65% | 10 weeks | 98% | 90% |

If insanity occurs, a Remove Curse must be cast, or the caster faces a 1% chance per week of insanity of dying at a random moment before recovery. When recovery is complete, the caster remembers the answer.

name: DEMI-SHADOW MONSTERS
description: As Shadow Monsters, but the creatures have only 40% of normal HP. Creatures that disbelieve take only 40% damage and fight the shadow as AC 8\.

name: DISMISSAL
description:
Banishes one extraplanar creature to its home plane. The creature's HD versus caster level modifies the save (higher creature HD \= bonus; higher caster level \= penalty). Willing creatures are automatically dismissed. A 20% chance exists that an unwillingly dismissed creature arrives at a random plane rather than its own.

The caster also has a 50% chance to banish a rogue elemental.

*Material component:* An item distasteful to the target creature.

name: DISTANCE DISTORTION
description:
Requires an earth elemental conjured and on hand before casting. The elemental voluntarily merges with the spell, making the effective area either twice as large or half its size — travellers crossing the area take twice as long or half as long depending on the chosen distortion. The distortion is visually undetectable, though it radiates a dim magical aura. When the spell ends, the elemental returns home and the space reverts.

*Material component:* A small lump of soft clay.

name: DOMINATION
description: As Charm Person but the caster gains direct mental control rather than affection. Once established, mental commands function at any range on the same plane — no verbal communication required. The subject refuses suicidal commands and commands against their deepest nature (each such command allows a new save at \+1 to \+4). Protection from Evil blocks further commands but does not dispel the initial effect.

name: DREAM
description:
The caster (or a designated creature touched during casting) enters a trance and delivers a message of any length into a sleeping target's dream. The target must be asleep; if awake when the spell is cast, the caster waits in the trance until the target sleeps. The target remembers the message perfectly. The caster is completely defenseless while in the trance.

*Reversed — Nightmare:* Sends horrifying visions. On a failed Will save, the target takes damage equal to their HD+1 (max 10 points), wakes fatigued, and cannot memorize spells that day. Damage cannot kill — victims reduced to 0 or less fall comatose.

name: EXTENSION II
description: As Extension I, but affects 1st through 4th level wizard spells.

name: FABRICATE
description:
Transforms raw material into a finished product of the same material — wood becomes planks or furniture, fiber becomes cloth, stone becomes shaped masonry. The quality of the product reflects both the raw material's quality and the caster's proficiency in the relevant craft. Items requiring exceptional craftsmanship require a caster who is also a skilled craftsman. Cannot create magical items or work with living matter.

Casting time: one round per cubic yard (or foot) of material.

*Material component:* The raw material itself.

name: FALSE VISION
description:
Creates a false magical "image" of the area that will be reported by any scrying spell or device looking in. The caster programs the false image at casting — it can be simply an empty room, or a detailed fabrication including activity. Any scrying directed at the area during the spell's duration receives the false image.

*Material component:* Ground jade and powdered lodestone.

name: FEEBLEMIND
description:
Reduces the target's Intelligence to 1, effectively destroying cognitive function while leaving basic survival instincts intact. The target cannot cast spells, speak coherently, use skills requiring thought, or recognize friends. The save is modified by Intelligence and class: wizards save at a −5 penalty, priest-wizards at −3, bards at −2. Clerics, druids, and characters without arcane training save normally.

*Material component:* A handful of clay, crystal, glass, or mineral spheres.

name: HOLD MONSTER
description:
As Hold Person but affects any creature except undead. Targeting one creature imposes a −3 save penalty; two creatures −1; three or four, no penalty.

*Material component:* A small, hard metal bar or nail for each creature targeted.

name: LEPRECHAUN'S LAMENTABLE BELABORMENT
description: The caster assaults one creature with an escalating barrage of demanding questions and confusing commentary. Each round the creature fails a Will save, it is confused as per the Confusion spell. After the first failed save, subsequent saves use the target's previous result as a modifier (cumulative). Once the creature finally fails, the target becomes bereft — standing stupidly and unable to function — for 1 turn per caster level.

name: LEPRECHAUN'S SECRET CHEST
description:
Hides a chest and its contents in the Ethereal Plane for up to 60 days. The chest can hold up to 250 kg of non-living material. The caster recalls it to the Prime Material by touching a small replica chest (which must be crafted from the same materials as the hidden chest, worth at least 5,000 gp) and concentrating for a round. If the replica is lost or destroyed, the chest is lost permanently.

*Material component:* The miniature replica chest.

name: MAGE'S FAITHFUL HOUND
description:
Conjures an invisible spectral hound that stands guard at a chosen location. Any creature approaching within 30 feet that the caster would not recognize as friendly triggers the hound to bark — a sound that wakes the caster at any distance. Any creature touching or attempting to pass the hound within 5 feet triggers an attack: \+3 to AB, 2d6 damage per bite, capable of multiple attacks per round equal to the caster's level. The hound only attacks; it cannot be harmed physically but can be dismissed by Dispel Magic.

*Material component:* A tiny silver whistle and a snippet of bone.

name: MAGIC JAR
description:
The caster's life force leaves their body and enters a prepared receptacle — the magic jar — from which they can attempt to possess any creature within range. The caster makes an opposed Will check against the target; on success, the caster's consciousness inhabits the target's body, with access to its physical abilities but not its spell memory or class abilities. The caster's own body remains comatose. If the possessed body dies, the caster's life force returns to the jar. If the jar is destroyed, the caster dies.

*Material component:* The prepared receptacle (a gem or crystal of at least 100 gp).

name: MAJOR CREATION
description:
As Minor Creation but can create mineral matter in addition to vegetable matter. Duration for vegetable materials: 1 hour/level. Duration for minerals: 1 turn/level. Duration for metals: 2 rounds/level. The more valuable or exotic the material, the shorter the duration.

*Material component:* A tiny piece of the material to be created.

name: MONSTER SUMMONING III
description: As Monster Summoning I, but summons 1d3 creatures of approximately 3 HD. See Appendix C.

name: PASS WALL
description:
Creates a 10-foot-deep passage through any solid wall of stone, plaster, or similar material. The passage is 5 feet wide and 8 feet tall. The passage closes when the duration expires, potentially trapping those inside. Multiple Pass Wall spells in the same location can create a passage of any depth.

*Material component:* A pinch of sesame seeds.

name: SEEMING
description: As Alter Self but affects one creature per two caster levels simultaneously. The caster specifies the appearance of each recipient. The changes are purely visual — each recipient's physical abilities, senses, and capabilities are unchanged. After 12 hours the illusion ends; creatures observing its end are entitled to reflect on what just happened.

name: SENDING
description:
Delivers a short message of up to 25 words to any known creature on the same plane. The message arrives instantly; the recipient can send a 25-word reply. Extra-planar sending attempts may fail at the GM's discretion. The spell does not identify the sender unless they choose to be identified.

*Material component:* Fine copper wire.

name: SHADOW DOOR
description: Creates an illusory door in a wall, floor, or ceiling. The caster can freely pass through it; other creatures see only a door and are subject to the illusion. On the caster's side, the door appears open; on the other side, it appears closed. The caster emerges from shadow near the illusory door and is effectively invisible until they attack or move at full speed.

name: SHADOW MAGIC
description: Duplicates any Evocation spell of 4th level or lower using shadow matter from the Plane of Shadow. If the target disbelieves, the effect is only 20% effective (20% damage, etc.). The caster must know the spell being duplicated to use it through Shadow Magic.

name: STONE SHAPE
description:
Reshapes one piece of stone into any form the caster desires. The stone's mass remains constant — it cannot be dissolved or expanded. Simple shapes (a plug, a step, a blade) are easy; complex or moving parts have a 1 in 4 chance of not functioning properly. Cannot create precious stones or magical effects within the stone.

*Material component:* Soft clay (shaped into the desired form before casting).

name: SUMMON SHADOW
description:
Summons 1 shadow per three caster levels from the Plane of Shadow. The shadows serve the caster and attack on command, lasting until destroyed or the duration expires. Shadows created by creatures slain by these summoned shadows do not serve the caster.

*Material component:* A piece of material from a shadow.

name: TELEKINESIS
description: Moves objects or creatures at a distance by mental force. Sustained Telekinesis can move up to 25 kg per caster level at MV 2\. Violent Telekinesis moves up to 7 kg per level as a hurled weapon — attack roll required, 1d6 damage per 750 grams (max 15d6), Reflex half. The caster may also attempt to seize and move a creature; unwilling creatures make a Fortitude save to resist.

name: TELEPORT
description:
Transports the caster and up to 250 lbs per level to a known destination. The success of the teleport depends on how well the caster knows the destination.

| Familiarity | On Target | Off Target | Mishap |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Studied carefully | 01–97 | 98–99 | 00 |
| Seen casually | 01–94 | 95–97 | 98–00 |
| Viewed once | 01–88 | 89–94 | 95–00 |
| Described | 01–76 | 77–88 | 89–00 |

An "Off Target" result places the teleporter 1d10 × 10 metres in a random direction. A "Mishap" result is fatal — the caster materializes partially inside solid matter.

name: TRANSMUTE ROCK TO MUD
description:
Transforms rock or stone into mud. Natural stone becomes 2 feet of mud per caster level, typically creating a deep mire. Creatures caught in the new mud may sink (Fortitude to avoid). The mud dries to rock over 1d6 days or can be instantly reverted by the reverse.

*Reversed — Transmute Mud to Rock:* Solidifies existing mud into rock permanently.

*Material component:* Clay and water (for rock to mud); sand, lime, and water (for mud to rock).

name: WALL OF FORCE
description:
Creates an invisible, flat plane of magical force. Completely impervious to physical damage, almost all spells, and most magical effects. Disintegrate destroys it instantly. Creatures and objects cannot pass through from either direction. Transparent to vision.

*Material component:* A pinch of powdered quartz.

name: WALL OF IRON
description:
Conjures a vertical wall of solid iron. The wall is permanent and can fall if unsupported — the caster can choose to make it fall on a specific side immediately, dealing 4d10 damage to creatures beneath (Reflex to dodge). The wall can be attacked and damaged as normal iron.

*Material component:* A small piece of iron sheet.

name: WALL OF STONE
description:
Creates a permanent wall of stone. The wall merges with existing stone if adjacent, making it significantly harder to destroy. Can be shaped into any flat or curved form within the area, including arches and tunnels. Can trap creatures if shaped around them (Reflex to avoid). Permanent and cannot be dispelled.

*Material component:* A small block of granite.

name: ANTI-MAGIC SHELL
description: Creates a mobile shell around the caster that blocks all magical effects from entering or leaving. No spell can be cast into or out of the shell. Magical items do not function within it. The caster is sealed within the shell — they cannot cast spells either, and any ongoing magical effects on the caster are suppressed. The shell does not affect physical attacks or non-magical items.

name: BIG FORCEFUL HAND
description:
As Big Interposing Hand but the hand actively pushes opponents back. The hand can push one creature per round: Small creatures are pushed back 5 yards per round; Medium creatures 3 yards; Large creatures 1 yard. The hand can also make a bludgeoning attack — AB equal to a warrior of the caster's level, 2d6+5 damage. The hand blocks passage through its space for creatures of Large size or smaller.

*Material component:* A soft glove.

name: CHAIN LIGHTNING
description:
Discharges a bolt of electricity at one primary target for 1d6 per level (max 12d6). The bolt then arcs to the nearest creature (chosen randomly if equidistant), reducing damage by 1d6 with each arc. No creature can be struck twice. The bolt arcs until damage is reduced to 0d6, it strikes a large metal grounding object, or no more valid targets exist. The caster can be struck. Saves apply to each arc individually.

*Material component:* Fur, a piece of amber, glass, or crystal rod, and one silver pin per caster level.

name: CONJURE ANIMALS
description: Conjures a number of HD of animals up to twice the caster's level (random animals) or up to the caster's level (requested species). The animals fight on command against designated opponents. If commanded to perform any non-combat action, they may refuse, flee, or turn on the caster.

name: CONTINGENCY
description:
Cast alongside a secondary spell (of level ≤ one-third the caster's level, max 6th), the caster sets a trigger condition. When the condition occurs, the secondary spell activates automatically and instantly. Only one Contingency can be active at a time. The secondary spell must be one that affects only the caster.

*Material component:* 100 gp worth of quicksilver, an eyelash from a mage or similar creature, and a carved statuette of the caster in elephant ivory.

name: CONTROL WEATHER
description:
Shifts weather in the area by one category in the desired direction per aspect (precipitation, temperature, wind speed, wind direction). The change takes 1d4 turns to manifest. Cannot reduce weather below minimum or above maximum categories. Subsequent castings can stack. Common sense governs — blizzards in tropical heat are not possible.

*Material component:* Incense and small bits of earth, wood, and water mixed together.

name: DEATH FOG
description:
Creates a fog bank identical in appearance to Fog Cloud, but the vapors are acidic. Creatures in the fog take escalating acid damage: 1 point round 1, 2 round 2, 4 round 3, 8 round 4 and each subsequent round. Plants die in 2–16 rounds depending on size. Immune to Gust of Wind — only fire (Fireball, Flame Strike, Wall of Fire) can destroy it in 1 round.

*Material component:* Dried and powdered peas and powdered animal hoof.

name: DEATH SPELL
description:
Instantly kills a number of creatures based on their HD. Roll 4d20 — this is the "death pool." Subtract the HD of each creature killed from the pool, starting with lowest HD and working upward. Creatures slain by this spell cannot be raised by any means short of Wish. Not effective against creatures with 9+ HD, lycanthropes, undead, or extraplanar creatures.

| HD/Level | Death Factor |
| ----- | ----- |
| Under 2 | 1 |
| 2 to 4 | 2 |
| 4+1 to 6+3 | 10 |
| 6+4 to 8+3 | 20 |

*Material component:* A crushed black pearl worth at least 1,000 gp.

name: DEMI-SHADOW MAGIC
description: As Shadow Magic but can duplicate Evocation spells of 5th level or lower. If disbelieved, effects are 40% effective.

name: DISINTEGRATE
description:
A thin green ray permanently destroys its target — living or undead creature, or a single non-artifact object. Creatures reduced to dust by this spell cannot be raised without a Wish. If a creature saves successfully, its equipment is also safe. If they fail, all worn items must save independently vs. disintegration.

*Material component:* A lodestone and a pinch of dust.

name: ENCHANT AN ITEM
description:
The prerequisite enchantment for all permanent magical item creation. Opens the magical pathways in a prepared vessel, making it ready for further enchantments. Requires 8 hours of uninterrupted handling per day for the duration. No other magical items may be within 10 metres during this time. At the end of the duration, the caster makes a Will save — failure means the process must restart from scratch. On success, the caster has 24 hours to begin casting additional spells into the vessel before the pathways close.

*Material component:* Rare materials specific to the item being created, determined through role-playing and research.

name: ENSNAREMENT
description:
Lures an extraplanar creature through a gate into a prepared warding circle. The creature must fail an Intelligence check to be drawn in. If no warding circle is present, the creature arrives freely and attacks. The caster can attempt to negotiate a service in exchange for the creature's release.

Warding circles: A hand-drawn circle (1 turn, 1,000 gp, 20% base failure) or a permanent carved circle (3 days, 5,000 gp, 10% base failure). Each day of confinement weakens the circle by 2%. Any imperfection in the circle's perimeter — a hair, a thread — immediately frees the captive.

name: EXTENSION III
description: As Extension I, but doubles the duration of 3rd-level or lower wizard spells, and extends 4th- or 5th-level spells by 50%.

name: EYE BITE
description:
The caster gains a gaze attack, used once per round by meeting a target's eyes and speaking a command word. Choose one effect at memorization (cannot be changed during duration):

*Charm:* Functions as Charm Monster; charmed creatures may be forced to dangerous actions normally against their will.

*Fear:* Target flees at full speed for 1d4 rounds; for 1 turn per level after, has 50% chance to cower or flee if the caster approaches.

*Sicken:* All ability scores and movement halved for 1 turn per level; ability scores return at 1 per turn of rest.

*Sleep:* As Sleep but no HD restriction; Aelár and half-Aelár resist normally.

Undead and creatures from other planes are immune. If the gaze is reflected, the caster is the target.

name: FREEZING SPHERE
description:
Choose one effect at casting:

*Frigid Globe:* Creates a globe of absolute-zero temperature that freezes up to 100 square feet of water per caster level into solid ice (6 inches deep, lasting 1 round/level).

*Cold Ray:* A narrow ray extending 10 yards per level deals 1d4+2 cold per level to the first creature it hits (Reflex \= miss entirely; ray continues past a successful save to the next creature in line).

*Globe of Cold:* A hurled sphere that explodes on impact for 6d6 cold in a 10-foot radius (Reflex half). If not thrown within 1 round/level, explodes automatically.

*Material component:* Varies by effect — thin crystal sheet (Frigid Globe); white sapphire worth 1,000 gp (Cold Ray); diamond worth 1,000 gp (Globe of Cold).

name: GEAS
description: Places a magical compulsion on the target to carry out a specified task. The task must be possible — it cannot require suicidal action — but can be difficult or dangerous. The target does not receive a saving throw. Refusing or delaying the geas causes the target to weaken: −2 to ability scores per week of refusal. Remove Curse, Wish, or Limited Wish can end it.

name: GLASS SEE
description: Makes any solid material — stone, wood, metal, flesh — temporarily transparent, allowing vision through up to 4 inches per caster level of material. The caster can see normally through the transparent area but cannot see through lead. Creatures cannot see through the affected area from the other side.

name: GLOBE OF INVULNERABILITY
description:
As Minor Globe of Invulnerability, but blocks spells of 4th level or lower from entering. Spells cast outward from within the globe pass through normally.

*Material component:* A small crystal sphere.

name: GUARDS AND WARDS
description:
Secures a large area — a tower, dungeon level, or building floor — with multiple defensive effects simultaneously: corridors filled with fog, stairs covered in grease, doors locked with Wizard Lock, intersection-triggered confusion, and mirror images of the caster at key locations. The specific effects and their placement are determined at casting within the area limits. A 2d4 penalty to saving throws against further confusion effects applies to any creature in the area.

*Material component:* Various items depending on effects used — burning incense, a tiny silver rod, a piece of phosphorus.

name: INTENSE TRANSFORMATION
description:
An extreme version of Polymorph Self that also grants one of the new form's supernatural abilities (breath weapon, gaze attack, or similar) for the spell's duration. The caster gains the full physical capabilities of the form plus one chosen special ability. The caster must survive the transformation — System Shock check required if the form's physical mass differs dramatically from the caster's.

*Material component:* A scale, feather, or skin fragment from the desired form.

name: INVISIBLE STALKER
description:
Summons an Invisible Stalker — a neutral elemental servant from the Air Plane — to perform one task. The task must be clearly stated; the Stalker follows instructions to the letter, not the intent, and is not loyal to the caster's goals. The Stalker cannot be returned to its plane until it completes its mission; if given an unreasonably complex task, it finds the most literal fulfillment possible. The Stalker has 8 HD and is always invisible.

*Material component:* Burning incense and a piece of horn.

name: LEGEND LORE
description:
Gathers legendary information about a person, place, or object of great importance. Casting time and reliability depend on how much information the caster already knows. With only a name or rough description, casting takes 1d10 days; partial information, 1d6 days; significant information, 1d4 days; the subject in hand, 2d6 rounds. The GM provides a brief legend or critical fact. The information may be incomplete, metaphorical, or obscure.

*Material component:* Incense worth 250 gp and strips of ivory worth 50 gp.

name: LOWER WATER
description:
Lowers the surface of a body of water within the area by 5% per caster level (minimum 1 foot, maximum 50%). Channels and rivers may be affected enough to permit crossing. Salt water may be lowered by as little as half the normal percentage. Creatures composed of water take 1d6 damage per round in the affected area.

*Reversed — Raise Water:* Raises a body of water by the same amount.

*Material component:* A pinch of dust (to lower) or a drop of water (to raise).

name: MAGE'S LUCUBRATION
description: Allows the caster to instantly re-memorize one spell of 5th level or lower that was cast within the past 24 hours. This recovered spell is ready to cast immediately. Can only be used once per day, and the recovered spell must have been cast from the caster's own memorized slots.

name: MASS SUGGESTION
description:
As Suggestion but affects one creature per caster level simultaneously. All affected creatures receive the same suggestion. Creatures that fail their Will save follow the suggestion as if it were their own idea.

*Material component:* A snake's tongue and honeycomb.

name: MIRAGE ARCANA
description: As Hallucinatory Terrain, but extends to structures and objects as well as natural terrain. The illusion can include buildings, ruins, a canyon, an oasis — any natural or architectural environment. Duration: permanent until dispelled. Casting time: 1 turn per 10-yard radius.

name: MISLEAD
description: Simultaneously turns the caster invisible (as Improved Invisibility) and creates a duplicate of the caster at the same location. The caster can direct the duplicate's actions (movement, speech, limited gestures) while remaining invisible. The duplicate can speak in the caster's voice, gesture, and move convincingly, but has no substance.

name: MONSTER SUMMONING IV
description: As Monster Summoning I, but summons 1d3 creatures of approximately 4 HD. See Appendix C.

name: MOVE EARTH
description:
Moves masses of soil, sand, and earth (not stone) at a rate of 6 feet per round. Can reshape hillsides, fill trenches, redirect streams. Cannot move rock or stone. Moving 3,000 cubic feet requires 1 turn of casting time. The movement rate is slow — creatures have time to step aside unless specifically constrained.

*Material component:* A mixture of soils in a bag.

name: PART WATER
description:
Parts a body of water, creating a trough through which the caster and companions can walk. The water walls on either side hold as long as the spell persists. Cannot affect water above 60°C or below freezing.

*Material component:* Two small blue staves.

name: PERMANENT ILLUSION
description:
As Spectral Force, but permanent until dispelled. The illusion runs on a pre-programmed sequence and requires no concentration. Has visual, auditory, olfactory, and thermal components.

*Material component:* A bit of fleece.

name: PROGRAMMED ILLUSION
description:
As Permanent Illusion but dormant until a specific trigger condition set by the caster occurs. When triggered, the illusion runs for 1 round per level before becoming dormant again. Can be triggered multiple times.

*Material component:* A bit of fleece and jade dust worth 25 gp.

name: PROJECT IMAGE
description:
Projects a perfect illusory duplicate of the caster at any point within range. The caster can see through the duplicate's "eyes." Spells cast from the caster's location appear to originate from the image — a fireball can be launched from the duplicate's position while the caster stands safely elsewhere. The image is destroyed by touch or Disbelief but cannot be harmed by attacks that don't reach it.

*Material component:* A small mirror.

name: REINCARNATE
description:
Restores a dead creature to life in a new body, determined randomly. The creature must have been dead no longer than one week. The creature retains its memories, personality, and class abilities, but inhabits a wholly new form. Roll 1d100 on the GM's reincarnation table for the new body — possibilities include all common ancestries and a range of humanoids and demi-humans. The new body is that of a young adult.

*Material component:* Rare oils and incense worth at least 1,000 gp.

name: REPULSION
description:
Creates a force field in a corridor-shaped area before the caster. Any creature attempting to move toward the caster in this path is pushed back at double their movement rate. They can move around the path to attack from the side. Ends when the duration expires or when the caster moves.

*Material component:* A pair of lodestones.

name: SHADES
description: As Shadow Monsters but the creatures have 60% of normal HP. Creatures that disbelieve take 60% damage and fight them as AC 6\.

name: STONE TO FLESH
description:
Restores a petrified creature to its normal flesh, immediately and without harm. The creature is fully restored to the state it was in when petrified — wounds, memories, and abilities intact.

*Reversed — Flesh to Stone:* Turns a living creature to stone permanently. The target makes a Fortitude save. On failure, the creature and all carried items are permanently petrified. A second Stone to Flesh can restore them.

*Material component:* Crushed lime, earth, and water (Stone to Flesh); limestone and drops of water (Flesh to Stone).

name: TRANSMUTE WATER TO DUST
description:
Converts water within the area to fine powder. Creatures composed of water take 1d6 damage per caster level (Fortitude half). Potions in the area must save or be ruined.

*Reversed — Dust to Water:* Converts powder or dust back to water.

name: TRUE SEEING
description:
The recipient sees all things as they truly are: illusions, polymorphed creatures, invisible creatures, projected images, misleads, disguises. Sees through magical darkness. Sees through up to 4 inches of normal matter. Does not grant vision of astral or ethereal planes unless the creature is currently on the Prime Material.

*Material component:* Eye ointment made from mushroom powder, saffron, and fat (at least 300 gp).

name: VEIL
description: As Seeming but affects any number of willing creatures within the area. Each creature takes on a different appearance chosen by the caster at casting. More versatile than Seeming — can make a dozen guards appear to be a noble's entourage, a tunnel mouth appear to be a wall, or a dragon appear to be an old man.

name: BANISHMENT
description:
Forces extraplanar creatures — up to 2 HD or levels per caster level total — back to their home planes. The caster must name each creature and type, providing true names where known. Creatures that survive their magic resistance check may save to resist. Each creature that saves successfully deals 2d6 damage to the caster per successful save, and stuns the caster for one round.

Each different material component included — substances dangerous, vile, or opposed to the creature's nature — reduces a creature's magic resistance by 5% and imposes −2 to its save. The GM may allow particularly effective substances to impose up to −4.

*Material component:* One or more substances opposed to each creature being banished.

name: BIG GRASPING HAND
description:
As Big Interposing Hand, but the hand can grasp and hold creatures or objects. A creature weighing up to 450 kg is held motionless; creatures between 450 and 1,800 kg are slowed to MV 1; creatures up to 7,200 kg are slowed by 50%. The hand inflicts no damage and cannot directly attack. The caster can release held creatures or dismiss the hand on command.

*Material component:* A leather glove.

name: CHARM PLANTS
description:
The caster can communicate with and command all plant life in the area. Non-intelligent plants obey immediately and can be directed to duplicate the effects of Entangle. Intelligent plant creatures receive a Will save at −4; if they fail, they are charmed as per the Charm spell and receive new saves at intervals based on their Intelligence.

*Material component:* A pinch of humus, a drop of water, and a twig or leaf.

name: CONTROL UNDEAD
description:
Directs 1d6 undead creatures to follow the caster's verbal commands. The caster points to a location within range — the closest undead (up to 6 creatures, or total HD not exceeding caster level) are affected first. Mindless undead are controlled automatically; intelligent undead with more than 3 HD receive a Will save. Controlled undead remember their subjugation after the spell ends.

*Material component:* A small piece of bone and raw meat.

name: DELAYED BLAST FIREBALL
description:
As Fireball, but deals 1d6+1 fire damage per caster level (max 10d6+10) and can be held for up to five rounds before detonating. The caster mentally triggers the explosion at any point during the delay. The bead of fire remains visible as a glowing mote during the delay.

*Material component:* A tiny ball of bat guano and sulfur.

name: DUO-DIMENSION
description:
Flattens the caster into a two-dimensional form — height and width, but no depth. When viewed from the side, they are invisible. From the front or rear, they appear normal. They can slip through paper-thin gaps and cracks wide enough for their height and width.

The caster's facing matters constantly. Flank attacks pass harmlessly through them; front and rear attacks deal double damage. Area effects harm them normally. Attacks from astral creatures deal normal damage and have a 25% chance per hit of pulling the caster completely to the Astral Plane, ending the spell.

*Material component:* A strip of parchment wrapped around an enameled ivory figurine of the caster with gold and platinum filigree and gemstones, worth at least 500 gp (destroyed on casting).

name: FINGER OF DEATH
description: Points a finger at one creature. A failed Fortitude save results in instant death — the creature cannot be raised, resurrected, or reincarnated without a Wish. Against human and humanoid targets, the caster can perform a special 1,000 gp ritual within 3 days to animate the body as a juju zombie. A creature that saves successfully still takes 2d8+1 damage.

name: FORCE CAGE
description:
Choose one effect at memorization:

*Force Cage:* Walls of alternating force bands with half-inch gaps trap creatures within but allow most spell effects to pass through. Magic resistance allows a one-time attempt to pass through the bars.

*Force Cube:* Solid walls at half the standard effective area, functioning as a Cube of Force. Completely impervious.

Both versions persist until the duration expires or until Dispel Magic succeeds.

*Material component:* Diamond dust worth at least 1,000 gp (used during memorization, not on casting).

name: INSTANT SUMMONS
description:
During a preparation ritual, the caster holds a gem worth at least 5,000 gp and inscribes a chosen item's name into it invisibly. When needed, the caster crushes the gem and calls the item by name — it appears instantly in one hand. The item must be 3 feet square or smaller, weigh no more than 3.6 kg, and be non-living. If another creature possesses the item, the spell fails but reveals the possessor's identity and general location. A Wizard Mark cast during preparation allows the item to be called away from a possessor on the same plane.

*Material component:* A gem worth at least 5,000 gp (consumed on use).

name: LIMITED WISH
description:
A constrained version of Wish capable of three forms of effect:

*Duplicating spells:* Any wizard spell of 7th level or lower from allowed schools (or 5th level or lower from prohibited schools); any priest spell of 5th level or lower from allowed spheres (or 4th level or lower from prohibited spheres). Variable effects favorable to the caster are maximized. Material components costing under 1,000 gp are not required.

*Emulating a Wish:* As Wish, but effects are temporary rather than permanent. Duration: 1d6+1 weeks, \+1 week per caster level. Casting time: approximately 3 rounds.

*Other effects:* Any effect at the power level of the above, subject to GM approval.

Casting Limited Wish ages the caster: 1 year if venerable age is 150 years or less, 2 years if 151–250, 3 years if 251–350, and so on.

name: MAGE'S MAGNIFICENT MANSION
description:
Creates a mansion within a pocket dimension accessible through a faint shimmering doorway. The mansion is comfortably appointed with food, furnishings, and an invisible staff of servitors who can cook, clean, and serve. Up to double the caster's level in creatures can be accommodated. The exterior appears as a shimmering portal; the interior is complete and comfortable. Nothing passes between the mansion and the outside world except for those the caster permits to enter. When the spell ends, all occupants are expelled.

*Material component:* A miniature ivory door, a small piece of polished marble, and a tiny silver spoon.

name: MAGE'S SWORD
description:
Conjures a shimmering blade of magical force that attacks under the caster's mental direction. The caster must concentrate to command it; they can move but take no other actions. The sword attacks as a warrior of half the caster's level, always hitting on a natural 19–20. Treats as a \+3 weapon for immunity purposes. Deals 5d4 to man-sized targets, 5d6 to large targets. Can hit ethereal, astral, and out-of-phase creatures. Persists until concentration breaks, the sword leaves range, or it is dispelled.

*Material component:* A miniature platinum sword with a copper and zinc grip and pommel, worth at least 500 gp.

name: MASS INVISIBILITY
description:
Renders up to 400 man-sized creatures invisible simultaneously. Large creatures count as 10; Huge or larger count as 50+. The invisibility is permanent but ends for all creatures simultaneously the moment any single invisible creature attacks or takes a hostile action. Creatures that leave the area or fall behind as the group moves become individually visible. As the subjects cannot see each other, coordinated movement may be penalized.

*Material component:* An eyelash in gum arabic.

name: MONSTER SUMMONING V
description:
Summons 1d3 creatures of approximately 5 HD from a random table. Roll 1d12: 1 Cockatrice, 2 Couatl, 3 Giant Hornet, 4 Hydra (7 heads), 5 Pyro/Cryo-Hydra (5 heads), 6 Subterranean Lizard, 7 Minotaur, 8 Ochre Jelly, 9 Giant Poisonous or Spitting Snake, 10 Giant Spider, 11 Winter Wolf, 12 Juju Zombie.

*Material component:* A tiny bag and a small unlit candle.

name: PHASE DOOR
description: Creates an ethereal passage through a wall, door, or similar barrier, visible only to the caster (and creatures they bring through, up to one person per use). Each passage through costs one usage. Only the caster (and a single companion per use) can traverse it. A higher-level caster is required to dispel it. Can be combined with Pass Wall. Often made permanent in lairs.

name: POWER WORD, STUN
description: Stuns one creature. Duration based on current HP at casting: up to 30 HP → 4d4 rounds; 31–60 HP → 2d4 rounds; 61–90 HP → 1d4 rounds. Creatures with more than 90 current HP are unaffected. No save is permitted.

name: PRISMATIC SPRAY
description:
Seven multicolored rays emit from the caster, each with a different effect. All creatures in the cone are struck by at least one ray. Creatures with fewer than 8 HD hit by any ray are additionally blinded for 2d4 rounds (Reflex to avoid). Roll 1d8 for each target to determine which color strikes. If two rays hit, effects resolve in ascending color order.

| Color | Effect | Save |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Red | 20 fire damage | Reflex half |
| Orange | 40 fire damage | Reflex half |
| Yellow | 80 lightning damage | Reflex half |
| Green | Death; save \= 20 damage | Fortitude |
| Blue | Petrification (as Flesh to Stone) | Fortitude |
| Indigo | Permanent insanity | Will |
| Violet | Plane shift to random plane | Will |
| 8 (both) | Roll twice, ignoring this result | — |

name: REVERSE GRAVITY
description:
Inverts gravity within the area. All unattached creatures and objects fall upward, taking falling damage if they strike a ceiling or overhead surface. When the effect ends, they fall back down, possibly taking damage again.

*Material component:* A lodestone and iron filings.

name: SEQUESTER
description:
Renders everything in the area invisible to all forms of sight and divination — scrying, locate, detect. Affected objects are tangible but visually undetectable. Living creatures within the area (unwilling ones save) not only become invisible but fall into suspended animation (as Temporal Stasis) for the duration. True artifacts and relics are immune.

*Material component:* The eyelash of a basilisk, gum arabic, and a dram of whitewash.

name: SHADOW WALK
description: Can only be cast in heavy shadow. Transports the caster and up to one additional creature per caster level to the border region between the Prime Material Plane and the Plane of Shadow. While in this border zone, the caster moves at 7 miles per turn relative to the Prime Material Plane and can choose where to re-emerge — though the blurred terrain prevents scouting. Can also be used to travel to other planes bordering the Shadow Plane, though the transit requires 1d4 hours and at least one encounter check.

name: SIMULACRUM
description:
Creates a quasi-real duplicate of any creature with HD or levels up to twice the caster's level. The base simulacrum is a mindless construct, obedient to simple commands, with 20–50% of the original's level/HD and 51–60% of its maximum HP. It can be destroyed by a standard Dispel Magic; if killed, it melts to water vapor immediately.

**Stage 1 Enhancement:** A Reincarnate spell infuses the construct with a spirit, granting intelligence, wisdom, and charisma at one-third the original's scores. It gains one language, a self-preservation instinct, and an alignment.

**Stage 2 Enhancement:** A Limited Wish grants 40–65% of the original's mental attributes along with partial memories and personality. At this stage the simulacrum may resist commands sharply contrary to the original's alignment.

A damaged simulacrum can be repaired with 100 gp of ruby dust per HP restored and 12+ hours of laboratory work.

*Material component:* A life-sized ice or snow body, powdered ruby, and a small piece of the creature being duplicated (feather, scale, hair, fang).

name: SPELL TURNING
description:
Deflects spells targeting the caster back at their origin. The GM secretly determines the number of spell levels the protection can handle (1d4+6, between 7 and 10). When a deflected spell's level exceeds remaining capacity, it is partially deflected — both casters take a proportional fraction of the effect, and Spell Turning ends.

If both casters have Spell Turning active simultaneously, a resonance field forms:

| 1d100 | Effect |
| ----- | ----- |
| 01–70 | Colorful but harmless; spell dissipates |
| 71–80 | Both casters take full effect (save allowed) |
| 81–97 | Both Spell Turning effects are nullified for 1d4 rounds |
| 98–00 | A rift opens, sending both casters to a random inner plane (no save) |

*Material component:* A small silver mirror.

name: STATUE
save: Fortitude (18% − Constitution score chance of death on transformation)\*
description:
Transforms the recipient and all worn/carried equipment into solid granite. The initial transformation takes a full round; there is an 18% chance minus the subject's Constitution score of dying during the transformation. Once in statue form, the subject can return to flesh at will for the duration — but only for action, then returns to stone. Returning to stone doesn't repeat the mortality check. In statue form the subject is invulnerable to normal weapons; it retains its senses and current HP.

*Material component:* Lime, sand, and a drop of water stirred by an iron bar.

name: TELEPORT WITHOUT ERROR
description: As Teleport but with no chance of mishap — the caster always arrives on target. Can carry up to 500 lbs of living or nonliving material per caster level (total). Operates across planes with a successful Fortitude save. Creatures can be teleported without their consent — they receive a Will save to resist.

name: VANISH
description: Teleports one object to wherever the caster desires within the current plane (same rules as Teleport Without Error). Alternatively, the caster can send the object to the Ethereal Plane, where it remains until magically retrieved. Objects in the Ethereal Plane can be found with Locate Object.

name: VISION
description:
The caster seeks a vision of a specific creature, place, or object. The vision comes as a brief but vivid scene, not a full explanation. The reliability and detail depend on how much the caster already knows. If the caster asks for forbidden or heavily protected information, the vision may provide false, metaphorical, or partial answers. The caster may age by 1d10 years when seeking dangerous visions.

*Material component:* Rare incense, the caster's holy symbol, and a sacrifice appropriate to the information sought.

name: ANTIPATHY–SYMPATHY
description:
Choose one effect at casting:

*Antipathy:* Designates a creature type or alignment. Creatures of that type or alignment entering the area or touching the object feel an overpowering revulsion and must save or flee immediately, unable to approach within 10 feet of the area.

*Sympathy:* Designates a creature type or alignment. Such creatures feel an irresistible attraction and must save or move toward and remain near the area or object for the duration, unable to leave voluntarily.

*Material component:* A lump of alum soaked in vinegar (Antipathy) or a lump of honey mixed with rose petals (Sympathy).

name: BIG CLENCHED FIST
description:
Creates a huge disembodied fist that strikes one target per round on the caster's mental command. No concentration required once cast. The fist has AC 0, HP equal to the caster's maximum, and saves as the caster.

| 1d20 | Result | Damage |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 1–11 | Glancing blow | 1d6 |
| 12–15 | Solid punch | 2d6 |
| 16–18 | Hard punch | 3d6 \+ stun 1 round |
| 19–20 | Crushing blow | 4d6 \+ stun 3 rounds |

Stunned targets grant \+4 to the fist's attack rolls.

*Material component:* A leather glove and a striking device of copper and zinc.

name: BINDING
description:
Creates a magical restraint that binds a creature. A physical restraint must be in place before casting. For extraplanar creatures, a warding circle (see Ensnarement) is also required. Up to six additional casters can assist, each adding 1 to the effective caster level; 9th-level assistants add one-third their level. Magic resistance applies unless the creature's true name and title are spoken.

Choose one effect:

*Chaining:* Restrains and immobilizes the creature for 1 year per combined caster level. Generates a field of Antipathy. Saves at normal difficulty.

*Slumber:* Comatose sleep for 1 year per combined level. Saves at \+1.

*Bound Slumber:* Both effects for 1 month per combined level. Saves at \+2.

*Hedged Prison:* Permanent imprisonment within an enclosed space sealed by magically inscribed runes (1,000 gp per HD of the creature). New save permitted each year.

*Metamorphosis:* Creature becomes incorporeal except for its face until a prescribed act frees it. New save each year.

*Minimus Containment:* Creature shrinks to 1 inch and is held within a diamond worth 1,000 gp per HD. New save each year.

name: CLONE
description:
Creates an exact physical and mental duplicate of a human, non-human, or humanoid. The clone grows from a piece of the original's living flesh (taken fresh or magically preserved) over 2d4 months. The clone has the original's Constitution minus 1 — cloning fails at Constitution 0\. It has all the memories, abilities, and experience of the original at the time the flesh was taken.

If both the original and clone exist simultaneously, each is aware of the other and consumed with destroying the other. If both remain alive after one week, roll percentile: 01–90, the clone dies; 91–98, the original dies; 99–00, both die.

*Material component:* A piece of fresh flesh from the original, used immediately or magically preserved.

name: DEMAND
description:
Functions as Sending but includes a Suggestion. The message and suggestion are always understood regardless of language or intelligence. On a failed Will save (at −2 plus the Wisdom modifier), the target exerts every effort to carry out the demanded activity. Demigods hear the demand but respond only voluntarily.

*Material component:* Two small connected cylinders and a piece of the target's hair or nail.

name: GLASS STEEL
description:
Transforms metal into a glass-like material with steel's hardness and iron's elasticity. The treated metal is visually identical to glass but has the structural properties of its original material. Used primarily to create transparent armor, invisible weapons, or glass-like structural elements that retain full metal durability.

*Material component:* A small piece of glass and powdered steel.

name: INCENDIARY CLOUD
description:
Creates a thick cloud of hot embers that scorches everything within. The cloud acts like Pyrotechnics in appearance — thick, heavily obscuring smoke. Creatures in the cloud take fire damage each round based on the round of exposure: rounds 1–2 deal 1d6+2, rounds 3–4 deal 1d6+4, subsequent rounds deal 1d6+2 decreasing. Reflex save halves each round. The cloud moves away from the caster at 10 feet per round and cannot be cast underwater.

*Material component:* A fire source and powdered lead.

name: IRRESISTIBLE DANCE
description: Forces the target to break into a shuffling, stumbling dance they cannot stop. The dancing creature has −4 to AC, cannot act offensively, and loses all Dexterity bonuses. The effect requires no attack roll to initiate, and no saving throw is permitted.

name: MASS CHARM
description: As Charm Monster but affects multiple creatures simultaneously with combined HD up to twice the caster's level. Creatures are targeted starting at the center of the area and working outward.

name: MAZE
description:
Traps one creature in an extradimensional labyrinth of shifting force walls. No saving throw. The creature remains until it navigates free; the time required depends on Intelligence. Minotaurs are immune. Teleport and Dimension Door fail within the maze; Plane Shift succeeds.

| Intelligence | Escape Time |
| ----- | ----- |
| Under 3 | 2d4 turns |
| 3–5 | 1d4 turns |
| 6–8 | 5d4 rounds |
| 9–11 | 4d4 rounds |
| 12–14 | 3d4 rounds |
| 15–17 | 2d4 rounds |
| 18+ | 1d4 rounds |

name: MIND BLANK
description: Completely shields the subject from all forms of mental detection, influence, reading, and scrying. Immune to: augury, charm, command, confusion, divination, ESP, fear, Feeblemind, mass suggestion, Phantasmal Killer, possession, suggestion, and all telepathic effects. Scrying spells and devices (crystal balls, Clairvoyance, Clairaudience) are blocked. Even Commune, Contact Other Plane, Wish, and Limited Wish cannot gather information about the subject. Only a deity can pierce this protection.

name: MONSTER SUMMONING VI
description: Summons 1d3 creatures of approximately 6 HD (appearing within 1d3 rounds). Roll 1d12: 1 Basilisk, 2 Corpse Ravager, 3 Hydra (8 heads), 4 Weretiger or Wolfwere, 5 Manticore, 6 Minotaur Lizard, 7 Ogre Mage, 8 Otyugh, 9 Pyrolisk, 10 Phase Spider, 11 Troll, 12 Wyvern.

name: PERMANENCY
description:
Makes certain spell effects permanent, as specified by those spells. Can be used to make the following wizard spells permanent on the caster: Comprehend Languages, Detect Evil, Detect Magic, Infravision, Protection from Evil, Read Magic, Tongues, Unseen Servant. Can make the following permanent on objects or areas: Darkness, Gust of Wind, Light, Magic Mouth, Solid Fog, Stinking Cloud, Wall of Fire, Wall of Force, Web, and any illusion spell that does not require concentration.

Casting Permanency on the caster costs 1 permanent point of Constitution per spell made permanent. This cost cannot be restored.

name: POLYMORPH ANY OBJECT
description:
Transforms any creature or object into any other creature or object. Cannot create matter of great intrinsic value (gems, gold, platinum) or duplicate cold iron's special properties. Duration depends on the degree of similarity between original and new form:

| Duration Factor | Duration |
| ----- | ----- |
| 0 | 20 minutes |
| 2 | 1 hour |
| 4 | 3 hours |
| 5 | 12 hours |
| 6 | 2 days |
| 7–8 | 1 week |
| 9+ | Permanent |

Factors: same kingdom (+5), same class (+2), same size (+2), same or lower intelligence (+2), related items (+1). Add all applicable.

A living creature transformed into an object that fails a special Intelligence check is affected as if Feeblemind when restored. When the transformation ends, the creature makes another System Shock check.

*Material component:* Mercury, smoke, and gum arabic.

name: POWER WORD, BLIND
description:
Blinds one or more creatures in range totaling up to 100 current HP. The caster may target one creature with up to 100 HP, or affect a group starting from the center outward. Duration depends on the total HP affected:

* Up to 25 HP: Permanent  
* 26–50 HP: 1d4+1 turns  
* 51–100 HP: 1d4+1 rounds

Cure Blindness or Dispel Magic removes the effect.

name: PRISMATIC WALL
description:
Creates an immobile wall of seven concentric prismatic layers. Vision cannot penetrate in either direction. Creatures approaching within 20 feet from outside are blinded for 2d4 rounds (Reflex to avoid). Any creature touching the wall suffers all seven effects simultaneously. Each layer protects against a different attack form and is negated by a specific magical effect, applied in ascending color order (red first).

| Color | Protects | Negated By |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Red | Non-magical missiles | Cone of Cold |
| Orange | Magical missiles | Gust of Wind |
| Yellow | Poison, gas, petrification | Disintegrate |
| Green | Breath weapons | Pass Wall |
| Blue | Detection/mental attacks | Magic Missile |
| Indigo | All magic effects | Continual Light |
| Violet | All objects and effects (Wall of Force) | Dispel Magic |

name: SCREEN
description: Creates a powerful illusory effect over an entire area that controls what can be seen, heard, smelled, or felt by scrying or direct observation. The declared scene is fixed at casting and plays on a loop for the full duration. Scrying attempts automatically encounter the illusion; direct observers receive a Will save only if they have specific reason to doubt what they see.

name: SINK
description: At the end of the round, the target becomes rooted to the ground for 4 turns. If the caster continues concentrating into the next round, the target begins to sink into the ground: one-quarter submerged per sub-round until fully buried, after which it continues sinking until entombed in suspended animation. An entombed creature can be excavated using Dig, Transmute Rock to Mud, or Freedom (from Imprisonment).

name: SPELL IMMUNITY (ARCANE)
description:
Grants a saving throw bonus against all wizard and priest spells that normally allow saves, including spell-like abilities and magical item effects.

| Spell Level | vs. Wizard | vs. Priest |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 1st–3rd | \+9 | \+7 |
| 4th–6th | \+7 | \+5 |
| 7th–8th | \+5 | \+3 |

If multiple recipients are designated, divide the total duration evenly.

*Material component:* At least 500 gp of crushed diamond sprinkled over recipients; each recipient must carry an intact diamond of any value.

name: SYMBOL
description:
Inscribes a magical rune that activates when triggered. Can be quickly scribbled (CT 3, 1 turn/level duration, lesser runes only) or carefully inscribed (CT 1 turn, activates when triggered, any rune type). A password established during careful inscription allows creatures to pass without triggering it.

**Lesser Runes** (both inscription methods):

* *Fear:* As Fear spell (Will save, −2 or −4 depending on inscription method)  
* *Hopelessness:* 3d4 turns of deep despair (Will save; −2 if carefully inscribed)  
* *Pain:* 2d10 turns of intense pain; −2 Dexterity, −4 to attacks (no save)  
* *Persuasion:* As Charm Monster (Will save, −4 if carefully inscribed)

**Greater Runes** (careful inscription only):

* *Death:* Instantly kills creatures totaling up to 80 HP (no save; Wish required to restore)  
* *Discord:* 5d4 rounds of violent argument; creatures of different alignments fight (no save)  
* *Insanity:* Permanent insanity for creatures totaling up to 120 HP (no save; Heal, Restoration, or Wish to cure)  
* *Sleep:* Catatonic sleep for 1d12+4 turns for creatures of 8 HD/level or less (no save)  
* *Spell Loss:* All spellcasters forget 1d4 memorized spells (no save)  
* *Stunning:* Creatures totaling up to 160 HP are stunned for 3d4 rounds; MV reduced to 3 (no save)

*Material component:* Mercury and phosphorus (quickly scribbled) or those plus 10,000 gp in opal and diamond dust (carefully inscribed).

name: TELEKINETIC SPHERE
description:
As Resilient Sphere, but everything inside weighs one-sixteenth normal. The caster can telekinetically move the sphere and its contents as a sustained force or violent thrust (up to the spell's range). Effects on creatures inside are similar to Feather Fall — collisions at speed are relatively harmless to the sphere's contents, but if the sphere vanishes while in mid-air, the results are catastrophic.

*Material component:* Matching hemispheres of hard gemstone and gum arabic, plus a small pair of bar magnets.

name: TRAP THE SOUL
description:
Traps a creature's life force inside a specially prepared gem. Casting takes one round per HD of the target. The gem must be prepared with Enchant an Item beforehand. The trap is triggered either by speaking the creature's name while holding the gem (which requires the creature to fail a Will save while within range) or by a trap on an object the creature touches (Will save to avoid). If triggered, the creature is permanently imprisoned in the gem. Only Wish can free it. If the gem is destroyed while the soul is inside, the creature is permanently slain.

*Material component:* A specially prepared gem worth 1,000 gp per HD of the target.

name: ASTRAL SPELL
description:
Projects the caster's life force into the Astral Plane, leaving their physical body in suspended animation. While astral-projected, the caster can enter any plane that borders the Astral Plane by forming a second physical body. The astral form is connected to the physical body by a silver cord — if severed by \+5 weapons, certain creatures, or astral winds, the caster dies immediately. If the second body or astral projection is killed, the cord snaps the caster back to their physical body, ending the spell.

Up to seven additional creatures linked in a circle with the caster can project with them. If the caster is killed, all linked travelers become stranded on the Astral Plane.

name: BIG CRUSHING HAND
description:
As Big Clenched Fist, but the hand grasps and constricts. It automatically hits and deals increasing damage each round the caster concentrates: 1d10 (round 1), 2d10 (rounds 2–3), 4d10 (round 4+). The hand has Strength 25 for crushing objects. Cannot affect incorporeal or gaseous creatures but prevents creatures that can squeeze through cracks from escaping.

*Material component:* A snakeskin glove and an eggshell.

name: CRYSTAL BRITTLE
description: Turns any metal — including living metal creatures and a target's armor — into a brittle crystalline substance shattered by any heavy blow. Non-magical items are instantly affected on contact. Magical items have a 5% chance per enchantment bonus of resisting. The GM may allow artifacts a 50%+ resistance. Disintegrate will not reverse this effect — only Wish can restore treated material.

name: ENERGY DRAIN
description:
Permanently drains 2 levels or HD from a creature on a successful touch attack. Lost levels affect HP, saves, attacks, and all level-dependent abilities permanently (until experience is regained or two Restoration spells are cast). A human or humanoid killed by this spell can be animated as a juju zombie under the caster's control.

A 5% chance exists that the caster loses 1 point of Constitution from handling the material component. If this kills the caster, they become a Shade — their alignment shifts to neutral evil and they are drawn to the Demiplane of Shadow permanently.

*Material component:* Essence of specter or vampire dust.

name: FORESIGHT
description:
Grants extraordinary danger sense. The caster cannot be surprised, gains −2 to AC (better AC), and \+2 to all saves for the duration. Alternatively, the caster can designate another creature as the subject — that creature cannot be surprised as long as the caster concentrates on them and communicates warnings, but does not gain the AC or save bonuses.

*Material component:* A hummingbird feather.

name: GATE
description: Opens a two-way portal between the caster's location and a specific creature on another plane, or a random point on a designated plane. If calling a specific creature, the caster must know its name and type. The creature is not bound to serve and arrives with its full faculties, alignment, and motivations intact — including hostility. Deities may or may not respond. The gate remains open for 1 round per caster level or until forcibly closed.

name: IMPRISONMENT
description:
Requires a successful touch attack. The caster speaks the target's name and basic background, then the creature is teleported deep beneath the earth into a state of timeless suspended animation. No magical means can locate or free an imprisoned creature. The target remains there indefinitely.

*Reversed — Freedom:* Cast over the exact site of the original Imprisonment, while speaking the prisoner's name and background as stated at the time of imprisonment. If the name is imprecise, there is a 10% chance 1d100 additional creatures are accidentally freed from random Imprisonment spells worldwide.

name: MAGE'S DISJUNCTION
description:
The most powerful dispelling effect in the wizard's arsenal. Within the area:

* All magical effects are automatically dispelled (no roll needed).  
* All permanent magical items must save vs. disintegration (Will save with bonus equal to enchantment) or become permanently non-magical.  
* Artifacts and relics have a 1% chance per caster level of losing all magical properties.

If an artifact is destroyed by this spell, the caster must save vs. spell (−4) or permanently lose all spellcasting ability. The destruction of an artifact is noticed by its patron powers — powerful beings have a 95% chance to investigate within a time the GM determines.

An Anti-Magic Shell within the area must save or be dispelled. A Globe of Invulnerability is automatically dispelled. This spell itself cannot be dispelled by any means other than a Wish.

name: METEOR SWARM
description: The caster launches either four 2-foot spheres or eight 1-foot spheres in a straight line to chosen detonation points. Each 2-foot sphere explodes in a 30-foot diameter area for 10d4 fire damage. Each 1-foot sphere explodes in a 15-foot diameter area for 5d4 fire damage. Creatures struck directly by a sphere receive no save; all others in the blast area save for half. Overlapping explosions require separate saves. Combustibles in the spheres' path must save vs. magical fire or ignite.

name: MONSTER SUMMONING VII
description:
Summons 1d2 creatures of approximately 7 HD (appearing within 1 round). Roll 1d12: 1 Greater Basilisk, 2 Behir, 3 Chimera, 4 Hill or Stone Giant, 5 Flesh Golem, 6 Hydra (10 heads), 7 Pyro/Cryo-Hydra (8 heads), 8 Fire Lizard, 9 Mummy, 10 Black Pudding, 11 Two-Headed Troll, 12 Tunnel Lurk.

Alternatively summons 1 creature of approximately 8 HD (appearing within 2 rounds). Roll 1d8: 1 Frost or Fire Giant, 2 Stone Golem, 3 Gorgimera, 4 Hydra (12 heads), 5 Lernaean Hydra (8 heads), 6 Brown Pudding, 7 Remorhaz, 8 Will-o'-Wisp.

name: POWER WORD, KILL
description: Instantly kills one creature with 60 or fewer current HP, or multiple creatures with 10 or fewer HP each totaling up to 120 HP. No saving throw is permitted. The caster chooses at casting whether to target a single creature or use the area effect.

name: PRISMATIC SPHERE
description: As Prismatic Wall but forms an immobile, opaque globe centered on the caster. Sighted creatures under 8 HD approaching within 20 feet from outside are blinded for 2d4 turns (a Reflex save reduces this to 2d4 rounds). The caster can enter and exit freely; other creatures inside cannot leave without suffering all seven effects.

name: SHAPE CHANGE
description:
The caster can assume the form of any living creature they are familiar with — below demigod rank, including gaseous forms, incorporeal creatures, plants, undead, and anything with HD no greater than the caster's level (max 25 HD). The caster retains current HP, Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, and does not gain magic resistance or innate magical abilities. All physical traits of the new form are fully gained: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, movement modes, natural attacks. Changing shape takes one second and requires no System Shock check.

If the caster is killed while in another form, the spell ends without reverting — this may prevent normal resurrection.

*Material component:* A jade circlet worth at least 5,000 gp (shatters when the spell ends; if destroyed prematurely, the spell ends).

name: SUCCORED RETREAT
description:
Enchants a prepared object worth 500 gp or more and binds it to a specific location. When a creature the caster entrusts with the object breaks it and speaks the command word, they and all worn/carried gear (up to their maximum carrying capacity) teleport instantly and without error to the bound location.

*Reversed — Succored Summons:* The caster teleports to the location of the creature breaking the object, gaining general knowledge of the situation. Unlike the normal version, the caster cannot refuse the summons.

Both versions are blocked by effects that hinder Plane Shift.

*Material component:* The object to be enchanted (500 gp; replaced each casting) and a masterwork version costing at least 5,000 gp (not destroyed).

name: TEMPORAL STASIS
description:
Places the target in a state of complete suspended animation — no aging, no biological processes, no magical deterioration, no time passing from the creature's perspective. The effect is permanent until reversed. The creature and all possessions are completely inert.

*Reversed — Temporal Reinstatement:* Frees a creature from Temporal Stasis.

*Material component:* A powder of lime, sand, and a gem worth 1,000 gp.

name: TIME STOP
description: The caster steps outside the normal flow of time for 1d3 rounds as perceived by others. During this time, only the caster moves, acts, and casts — all other creatures and objects are frozen. The caster cannot affect other creatures or objects directly (attacks pass through them), but may move freely, position themselves, cast spells with later effect (like delayed fireballs), or reposition items. The duration is unknown to the caster until it ends.

name: WEIRD
description: As Phantasmal Killer but affects all creatures in the area simultaneously. Each creature first makes a Will save — on success, the illusion is recognized and that creature is unaffected. On failure, each creature must then make a Fortitude save or die of fright. A creature that survives the Fortitude save is stunned for 1d4 rounds.

name: WISH
description:
The most powerful spell in mortal spellcasting. A Wish can:

* Duplicate any wizard spell of 8th level or lower, or any priest spell of 6th level or lower, with maximum variable results and no material component requirement under 10,000 gp.  
* Undo magical transformations (polymorph, petrification, cursed scrolls, specific other effects) permanently.  
* Grant a \+1 permanent bonus to one ability score (subject to racial maximums; each bonus beyond the first requires an additional Wish).  
* Create large quantities of valuable material up to 25,000 gp value (the GM determines exact limits based on wording).  
* Grant any effect the GM considers equivalent in power.

**The limits of Wish.** A Wish is powerful, not infinite. The GM is responsible for interpreting it literally — not charitably. A poorly worded Wish produces what was asked for, not what was intended. Wishes that attempt to kill deities, permanently alter fundamental cosmological facts, or undo the results of other Wishes always fail and may have severe consequences.

Casting Wish ages the caster: 3 years if venerable age is 150 years or less, 6 years if 151–250, 9 years if 251–350, and so on. The caster must rest for one full day per three caster levels after casting a Wish before they can act normally.

name: ANIMAL FRIENDSHIP
description:
This spell may only be cast on a neutrally aligned, mundane animal with Intelligence 1–4. If the animal fails its Will save, it sits quietly while the casting completes. The caster must genuinely intend to befriend the creature — any ulterior motive registers to the animal during the hour of casting, and the spell fails.

Befriended animals are loyal to the caster and obey simple commands. The caster may teach the animal three specific tricks per point of Intelligence: short, one-word commands — attack, guard, fetch — each requiring a week to learn and all taught within 3 months of the bonding. The caster can maintain bonds with animals totalling up to twice his level in HD. Animals left unattended for more than a week revert to their natural state and the spell ends.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and a piece of food preferred by the target animal.

name: BLESS / CURSE
description:
*Bless* raises morale, attack rolls, and saving throws against fear effects by \+1 for all allies within the area. Allies already engaged in melee are unaffected. PCs receive the attack and save bonuses; the morale bonus applies only to NPC allies and hirelings.

Optionally, a single item up to 500 g per caster level may be blessed rather than the area. A blessed weapon is treated as enchanted for the purpose of striking evil incorporeal creatures and gains a \+1 attack bonus against evil foes for the duration — but only if the weapon carries no existing magical effect. Missiles may be blessed; bows and crossbows do not transfer the blessing to their ammunition.

The reverse, *Curse*, applies a −1 penalty to morale and attack rolls against opponents not yet engaged in melee. A cursed item is correspondingly more effective against good-aligned creatures.

*Material component:* a vial of holy water (for *Bless*) or unholy water (for *Curse*), sprinkled during casting.

name: COMBINE
description:
All participating priests must have this spell memorized. The highest-level priest stands at the centre of a circle of two to four others, and all cast *Combine* simultaneously. The central priest then affects undead and casts secondary spells from memory as if they were one level higher per priest in the circle — between 2 and 4 levels of enhancement.

The circle priests must concentrate each round, losing shield and Dexterity bonuses to AC. The spell ends if any circle priest is damaged or their concentration breaks. Damaging the central priest does not end it. If *Combine* ends while a secondary spell is being cast, that spell is lost as if the caster had been struck. Secondary spells already completed retain their enhanced effects even if *Combine* later ends.

The GM may require circle priests to share a faith, a pantheon, or an alignment. For allied priests of closely shared cause, shared faith is usually sufficient.

name: COMMAND
description:
The caster issues a single-word command to one creature, stated clearly as a single action the creature can understand. The creature obeys for 1 round. Typical commands: *back, halt, flee, run, stop, fall, go, leave, surrender, sleep, rest*. Commanding a creature to *die* sends it into a catatonic state for 1 round.

Creatures with Intelligence 13 or higher, or 6 or more HD, are allowed a Will save. *Command* has no effect on the undead.

name: CREATE WATER / DESTROY WATER
description:
*Create Water* produces fresh, pure water within the area. Empty containers are required; without them the water flows to the lowest available point. The water is magical for its first round — dispel magic can unmake it during that window. Afterward it becomes ordinary water.

The reverse, *Destroy Water*, eliminates a like quantity of water within the same range, leaving no trace.

Water cannot be created or destroyed inside a living or undead creature of any kind. Water weighs roughly 1 kg per litre.

*Material component:* a drop of water to create; a pinch of dust to destroy.

name: CURE LIGHT WOUNDS / CAUSE LIGHT WOUNDS
description:
*Cure Light Wounds* restores 1d8 HP to the creature touched. The spell cannot affect creatures without a corporeal body, the undead, non-living creatures, or extra-planar entities. Only existing wounds are healed; the spell offers no protection against future damage.

The reverse, *Cause Light Wounds*, requires a successful touch attack and inflicts 1d8 HP of damage. Treat wounds so caused as ordinary injuries.

name: DETECT EVIL / DETECT GOOD
description:
Within the area, the caster detects evil auras radiating from creatures, objects, or the environment. The caster must stop and concentrate for at least one round on a specific target to detect an aura and gauge its strength — dim, faint, moderate, strong, or overwhelming. Exact alignment is never revealed. Most Prime Material creatures don't radiate an aura unless they are fully devoted to an alignment, are 9 HD or higher with appropriate intent, or are intelligent undead or extra-planar creatures.

The priest's version additionally allows a vague one-word impression of the source's intent — eager, hateful, cruel, kind, and so on — when an aura is detected. Against overwhelming auras, the priest has a 10% chance per caster level to detect whether the source leans toward law, chaos, or neutrality.

The reverse, *Detect Good*, functions identically but reveals good auras.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol, held out before casting.

name: DETECT MAGIC
description:
The caster detects magical auras within the area, scanning a 60-degree arc per round. Aura intensity — dim, faint, moderate, strong, or overwhelming — is discernible, and the caster has a 10% chance per caster level to identify the sphere of a detected effect (not its school). At least 30 cm of stone, 2.5 cm of metal, or 1 m of wood or earth blocks the spell. Multiple schools in the same area or overwhelmingly powerful auras may produce confusing, incomplete, or conflicting results.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: DETECT POISON
description:
The caster checks for poison in one object or up to a 1.5 m cube of material per round. There is a 5% chance per caster level to identify the exact type.

*Material component:* a strip of specially blessed vellum, which turns black if poison is present.

name: DETECT SNARES & PITS
description:
The caster detects natural or primitively constructed traps in the path ahead: snares, pits, deadfalls, quicksand, sinkholes, unsafe rock faces, and similar hazards made of natural materials by animals, plants, or people. Mechanical dungeon traps and magical wards are beyond the scope of this spell.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: ENDURE HEAT / ENDURE COLD
description: One chosen version is selected at casting: *Endure Cold* allows the recipient to withstand temperatures as low as −35°C without discomfort. *Endure Heat* extends tolerance up to 55°C. More extreme temperatures still inflict 1 point of damage per hour of exposure. If the recipient takes magical cold or heat damage, the first 10 points are absorbed — but the spell ends regardless of which version is in effect when hit. The two versions cancel each other.

name: ENTANGLE
description:
Plants within the area wrap, grasp, and twist to entangle creatures. Any creature entering or moving through the area must make a Reflex save or be held fast, unable to act, for the duration. A successful save allows movement at 3 m per round or one action per round; any creature remaining in the area must save again each round.

The spell requires some vegetation to function and cannot be cast in a completely barren area. Existing plants stretch to their limits — roots may emerge from ceilings or walls in underground settings where the GM judges plant roots are nearby. Sparse vegetation may grant up to a \+4 bonus to saves.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: FAERIE FIRE
description:
Creatures and objects within the area glow with faint light — blue, green, or violet, the caster's choice — as bright as a small candle. The caster may affect one man-sized or two small-sized creatures per caster level. Glowing targets are visible from 72 m in darkness and 36 m otherwise. Attackers gain a \+2 bonus to hit in darkness or moonlight, \+1 in normal conditions. Invisible creatures can be affected; incorporeal, ethereal, and gaseous creatures cannot. Multiple castings of this spell extend the area but do not increase the brightness.

*Material component:* a small chunk of foxfire.

name: INVISIBILITY TO ANIMALS
description:
Each creature touched during casting — up to one per caster level — goes undetected by non-magical animals with Intelligence below 6\. The effect suppresses sound and scent as well as sight with respect to animals. The spell ends for a given subject if they speak or make a hostile action against an animal; it remains in effect for others.

*Material component:* holly rubbed over each subject.

name: INVISIBILITY TO UNDEAD
description:
The recipient goes undetected by undead. Undead with more than 4 HD are allowed a Will save. A priest who is the subject of this spell cannot turn undead that cannot perceive them. The spell ends immediately if the recipient performs any hostile action — attacking, or forcing a saving throw on an affected creature. Casting non-hostile spells such as *Cure Light Wounds* or *Chant* does not break the effect.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: LIGHT / DARKNESS
description:
*Light* creates torch-strength illumination centred on a point within range or attached to a creature or object. If cast on an unwilling creature, a failed Reflex save means the light appears in the air directly behind them. If targeted at a creature's eyes, a failed Reflex save renders it blind for the duration. Objects beyond the glow appear as shadowy forms. Light cancels magical darkness of equal strength on contact, and vice versa. The caster may end the spell with a command word.

The priest's *Darkness* reverse plunges the area into complete darkness for half the normal duration. Only a light source at least as bright as full daylight remains visible inside it.

name: LOCATE ANIMALS OR PLANTS
description:
