The caster concentrates on one specific type of animal or plant and learns whether any exist within range — along with direction, distance, and approximate numbers. The caster can only detect subjects directly ahead in a straight path but may shift direction once per round during the duration. Common local specimens are 50% likely to be present; uncommon 30%; rare 15%; very rare 5%.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: MAGICAL STONE
description:
The caster enchants up to three small pebbles. They count as \+1 weapons for striking creatures requiring magical weapons to hit, and deal 1d4 damage (2d4 against undead). All three can be thrown in one round, with a range of 27 m ignoring range penalties.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and 1–3 ordinary pebbles.

name: PASS WITHOUT TRACE
description:
The recipient moves through any natural terrain — mud, snow, dust — without leaving footprints or scent. Normal tracking becomes impossible, though the ground where the creature has passed radiates a detectable magical aura for 1d6 turns afterward.

*Material component:* a sprig of pine or evergreen, burned to ash and scattered during casting.

name: PROTECTION FROM EVIL / PROTECTION FROM GOOD
description:
This spell creates an invisible magical shield with three simultaneous effects:

**Hinder Evil Attacks.** Attack rolls from evil creatures — or those enchanted by evil forces — suffer a −2 penalty, and saves against their attacks gain a \+2 bonus.

**Block Extra-Planar Contact.** Conjured animals, summoned monsters, and extra-planar creatures cannot make physical contact with the protected creature. Natural attacks auto-fail and the creature recoils. Magic resistance may allow a creature to overcome this. If the protected creature makes a melee attack against such a creature or forces the shield against them, this effect ends.

**Block Mind Control.** Direct mental control and possession attempts are blocked. Charm effects are not blocked, but domination, coercion, and body possession are. An already-possessing life force cannot be expelled.

The reverse, *Protection From Good*, applies the hinder effect against good creatures; effects two and three remain unchanged.

*Material components:* for *Protection From Evil*, a 1 m circle of powdered silver and burning incense drawn on the ground during casting. For the reverse, a circle of powdered iron and smoldering dung.

name: PURIFY FOOD & DRINK / PUTREFY FOOD & DRINK
description:
*Purify Food & Drink* restores spoiled, rotted, contaminated, or otherwise non-edible food and drink — meat, bread, fruit, vegetables, water, beer, wine — to an edible state. It also destroys unholy water. It cannot prevent future decay, and it has no effect on creatures, potions, or other substances.

The reverse, *Putrefy Food & Drink*, spoils good food and drink and destroys holy water within the area. It similarly has no effect on creatures, potions, or other substances.

name: REMOVE FEAR / CAUSE FEAR
description:
*Remove Fear* grants affected creatures a \+4 bonus to saves against fear attacks for 1 turn. If a creature is already under a magical fear effect, it immediately receives a new Will save with the \+4 bonus. Casting the reverse automatically counters *Remove Fear* on a target.

The reverse, *Cause Fear*, forces one creature within range to flee at maximum speed for 1d4 rounds. The creature is entitled to a Will save to resist. Casting *Remove Fear* on an affected creature automatically ends the effect. Neither version affects the undead directly, but either can counter a fear effect caused by an undead creature.

name: SANCTUARY
description:
An invisible safe haven surrounds the protected creature. Any enemy attempting to physically attack or directly target the protected creature with a harmful effect must make a Will save. Failure means the enemy abandons their current action and, for the duration, completely ignores the protected creature — as if they have forgotten their existence. Enemies who never attempt to attack are unaffected. Area spells such as *Fireball* or *Ice Storm* that cover an area rather than targeting a specific creature bypass this protection.

The spell ends immediately if the protected creature attempts to harm an enemy. Non-harmful actions — casting divinations, using *Chant*, curing allies — do not break the protection.

*Material components:* a small silver mirror and the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: SHILLELAGH
description:
The caster's wooden club or staff becomes fully magical for the duration. While in the caster's hands, it gains a \+1 bonus to attack rolls and deals 2d4 damage against man-sized and smaller creatures, or 1d4 \+ 1 against large creatures. The weapon is unharmed by the enchantment.

*Material components:* a shamrock leaf and the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: AID
description:
The caster infuses one creature — or themselves — with divine energy: a \+1 bonus to attack rolls and saving throws, plus 1d8 temporary HP for the duration. These temporary HP may push the total above the creature's normal maximum; they are lost first in combat and cannot be cured or healed away.

*Material components:* a tiny strip of white cloth with adhesive ends and the priest's holy or unholy symbol.

name: AUGURY
description:
The caster petitions for divine insight, asking whether a specific action taken in the next half hour will benefit or harm the party. Base chance of receiving a useful reply: 70% \+ 1% per caster level. Replies are brief — yes, no, I don't know, or a short cryptic riddle of no more than five words.

The GM is advised to limit this spell to once per adventure, or once per week at most. Deities do not enjoy being interrupted constantly.

*Material component:* a set of fortune-telling tokens worth at least 1,000 gp, not consumed in casting.

name: BARK SKIN
description:
The recipient's skin becomes as tough as bark, providing a base AC of 14 with an additional \+1 per 4 caster levels, and a \+1 bonus to all saving throws except magic. This spell does not function over armour or magical protection of any kind, but stacks with Dexterity bonuses and shields.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and a handful of bark from an old oak tree.

name: CHANT
description: While the caster chants without pause, all allies within the area receive a \+1 bonus to attack rolls, damage, and saving throws, and all opponents within the area suffer a −1 penalty to the same. If the caster is damaged or concentration is interrupted, the spell ends. Multiple *Chant* effects do not stack, but a second priest of the same faith casting *Prayer* during an active *Chant* increases the combined effect to \+2 and −2 for as long as both spells remain in force.

name: CHARM
description:
The target regards the caster as a trusted friend and ally unless it succeeds at a Will save. (If the target took damage from the caster or their allies in the same round the spell was cast, it gains a \+1 bonus to its save per point of damage received.) A charmed creature obeys verbal instructions within reason — it will not take suicidal actions or act wholly against its nature — and retains full memory of everything it does while charmed.

The creature is allowed a new Will save at regular intervals determined by its Intelligence:

| Intelligence | Time Between Saves |
| ----- | ----- |
| 3 or less | 3 months |
| 4–6 | 2 months |
| 7–9 | 1 month |
| 10–12 | 3 weeks |
| 13–14 | 2 weeks |
| 15–16 | 1 week |
| 17 | 3 days |
| 18 | 2 days |
| 19+ | 1 day |

The spell ends if the caster or their allies openly harm the subject, or a new save succeeds. The GM determines the exact moment each check occurs.

The priest's version also targets naturally occurring, non-magical mammals with Intelligence 1–4. An animal charmed in this way may be kept on long-term if cast alongside *Animal Friendship*.

name: DETECT CHARM / UNDETECTABLE CHARM
description:
*Detect Charm* reveals whether a creature is under a charm, compulsion, hypnosis, or similar effect. The subject is allowed a Will save; success means the caster learns nothing. On a failed save, the caster knows the creature is under influence and has a 5% chance per caster level to identify the exact nature of the effect. When multiple charms affect a creature simultaneously, the exact natures cannot be distinguished.

The reverse, *Undetectable Charm*, masks charm detection on a single creature for 24 hours or until *Dispel Magic* succeeds.

name: DUST DEVIL
description:
The caster summons a weak air elemental — a dust devil — that travels on verbal command but disperses if it moves more than 27 m from the caster. The caster is not immune to its effects.

The dust devil has AC 16, MV 18, and 2 HD, and attacks once per round with Attack Bonus \+1, dealing 1d4 damage. In dusty or debris-strewn areas, it kicks up a cloud with a 3-m diameter centred on itself. Creatures caught in the cloud are blinded for 1 round after leaving it. Spellcasters inside the cloud must succeed at a Fortitude save to maintain concentration while casting.

name: ENTHRALL
description:
The caster must speak uninterrupted for the full casting time and continue speaking for the enchantment to hold — up to one hour. Creatures in the area who understand the language and have 3 or fewer HD or Wisdom below 16 must make a Will save. Failures are peacefully enthralled, standing motionless and ignoring everything except the caster's words. About half of those who succeed their save also become hostilely enthralled — compelled to listen but heckling and booing throughout. The rest are unaffected and act normally.

Creatures from a hostile faction or opposing religion receive a \+4 bonus to their save. If the crowd is attacked, the spell ends and all enthralled listeners react against the attackers with a −10 penalty to the initial reaction roll.

A concerted effort to rouse a specific enthralled individual grants it a new save every third round. Enthralled creatures who succeed this second save join the hecklers half the time.

name: FIND TRAPS
description:
The caster detects any active trap — mundane or magical — directly ahead, regardless of how it has been concealed. A trap, for this spell, is any device or magical effect intentionally designed to inflict a sudden, unexpected, and harmful result as a direct consequence of the victim's action. Alarm spells, *Glyphs of Warding*, and mechanical spring traps all qualify. Natural hazards and ambushes do not.

The caster immediately learns whether a detected trap is magical, mechanical, or both, but not its effect or how to disarm it. Careful examination — spending a round studying the trap — reveals what actions or inactions trigger it.

name: FIRE TRAP
description:
The caster wards a closeable object — a chest, book, door, bottle — with an explosive fire trap. Any creature other than the caster (and those designated at casting) who opens the object triggers an explosion in a 1.5-m radius dealing 1d4 \+ 1 damage per caster level. A Reflex save halves the damage. The object itself is unharmed. A *Knock* spell neither triggers nor removes the trap; the trap still fires when the object is opened by an undesignated creature. Thieves treat this as a magical trap when attempting to locate and disable it.

The spell fails if a second magical trap or ward already exists on the same object; attempting to stack them negates the *Fire Trap* 25% of the time, the secondary effect 25% of the time, or both 50% of the time.

*Material components:* the caster traces the closure with a stick of charcoal, then touches the contact point with holly berries. Designating safe creatures requires a hair, scale, or claw from each.

name: FLAME BLADE
description:
A fiery blade springs from the caster's hand and can be wielded like any weapon the caster is trained to use. A successful hit inflicts 1d4 \+ 4 fire damage; against undead or creatures vulnerable to fire it deals 2 additional points, while creatures resistant or immune to fire subtract 2 points. Combustible materials contacted by the blade — paper, dry wood, cloth — must save versus magical fire or catch fire. Extra-planar fire creatures and those naturally immune to fire take no damage. The flame blade is not a magical weapon for the purpose of bypassing weapon immunities (except against undead) and cannot function underwater.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and a leaf of sumac.

name: GOOD BERRY / BAD BERRY
description:
*Good Berry* enchants 2d4 freshly picked berries. A caster of the same faith with 3rd level or higher can identify them by sight; *Detect Magic* reveals them to anyone. Eating one berry either fully nourishes a hungry man-sized creature (as a complete meal) or heals 1 HP of damage. A creature may benefit from up to 8 berries in a 24-hour period.

The reverse, *Bad Berry*, causes 2d4 berries to appear wholesome but deal 1 point of poison damage each when eaten — no save, onset 1d4 rounds.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol passed over freshly picked edible berries.

name: HEAT METAL / CHILL METAL
description:
*Heat Metal* superheats ferrous metal (iron or steel) — affecting either all the ferrous items carried by one creature per two caster levels (no two more than 9 m apart) or 11 kg of ferrous metal per caster level within a 9-m radius. Elven mail is unaffected. Magical iron or steel items receive a Fortitude save versus magical fire to avoid the effect. *Protection From Fire* or equivalent resistance negates the spell's effects.

Creatures in contact with the heated metal take damage based on the escalating rounds:

| Round | Metal State | Damage |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 1 | Warm | None |
| 2 | Hot | 1d4 |
| 3–5 | Searing | 2d4 |
| 6 | Hot | 1d4 |
| 7 | Warm | None |

A creature takes full damage if its armour is heated or if it carries metal weighing more than one-fifth of its body weight. Creatures carrying less than that minimum take only 1–2 points. On the 5th round (searing), an affected creature must make a Fortitude save or suffer a disabling burn:

| 1d10 | Location | Disability |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 1–4 | Hand/Foot | Unusable for 2d4 days |
| 5–9 | Body | Disabled for 1d4 days |
| 10 | Head | Unconscious for 1d4 turns |

Only natural healing or a *Heal* spell removes such a disability. Exposure to cold damage negates *Heat Metal* on a point-for-point basis, and vice versa. Underwater, the spell inflicts only half damage and cannot cause disability.

The reverse, *Chill Metal*, freezes ferrous metal through the same seven-round progression. A resist cold spell or cold resistance negates it. Exposure to fire damage counters it on a point-for-point basis.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: HOLD PERSON
description:
The spell holds up to 4 man-sized or smaller bipedal humans, human-adjacent ancestries, or humanoids rigid and motionless. Each target is allowed a Will save: −3 penalty if only one is targeted, −1 if two, no penalty if three or four. Held creatures remain conscious and aware but cannot move or speak; they may still use purely mental abilities. Winged creatures held in flight fall; swimmers may drown. *Hold Person* has no effect on the undead.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and a small piece of iron or steel.

name: KNOW ALIGNMENT / UNDETECTABLE ALIGNMENT
description:
The caster stands still and concentrates on a creature or object for 1 round, fully reading its alignment on both the law/chaos and good/evil axes. A Will save from the subject blocks the reading entirely — and the caster does not know whether the save succeeded. If only one round of concentration is available, only the law/chaos axis is revealed.

The reverse, *Undetectable Alignment*, masks a creature's or object's alignment from magical detection — including *Know Alignment* — for 24 hours, or until a *Dispel Magic* succeeds.

name: MESSENGER
description:
A tiny animal within range — bird, rat, insect, anything of at least animal intelligence but no greater than low intelligence — becomes a carrier. The animal is allowed a Will save to avoid the effect. If it fails, the caster gives it simple instructions for where to deliver its message and may attach a note or small item. The animal travels as directly as natural instincts and terrain allow.

*Material component:* a piece of food the animal is known to prefer.

name: OBSCUREMENT
description: The caster shrouds themselves and the surrounding area in misty vapour, reducing all vision — including infravision — within the cloud to 2d4 × 30 cm. The vapour cannot rise above 3 m and will fill narrower spaces. A strong wind reduces the duration to 25% of normal. The spell cannot be cast underwater.

name: PRODUCE FLAME
description:
A flame springs from the caster's palm — as bright as a torch, harmless to the caster's hand. Combustibles touched to it (paper, dry wood, cloth, oil) must save versus magical fire or ignite. This flame cannot be cast underwater.

The caster may hurl the flame at a target within 36 m. On impact it bursts in a 1-m diameter area, dealing 1d4 \+ 1 damage, and igniting combustible materials that fail a save. A miss scatters as an indirect missile. The caster may hurl one flame per caster level — one per round — until the duration expires or they end the spell voluntarily. Fires caused by the thrown flame cannot be extinguished by the spell.

name: RESIST FIRE / RESIST COLD
description:
One version is chosen at casting. *Resist Fire* renders the recipient immune to temperatures up to 55°C and brief contact with ordinary flame (1 round or less). Against more intense heat or magical fire attacks, the recipient gains a \+3 bonus to saves and takes only half damage on a failed save, one-quarter on a success.

*Resist Cold* functions identically against cold, providing immunity to temperatures as low as −35°C and the same save bonus and damage reduction against cold attacks.

*Material component:* a drop of mercury.

name: SILENCE, 4.5-METRE RADIUS
description:
A zone of complete silence occupies the area. No sound enters, exits, or occurs within it. Verbal communication and any spell requiring a Verbal component are impossible inside the zone. The silence also fully protects those inside from sonic-based attacks.

The area may be stationary or anchored to a moving object or creature. An unwilling creature (or a creature possessing an item the caster targets) is allowed a Reflex save; success means the centre of the zone becomes a stationary point approximately 30 cm behind that creature, allowing it to escape the zone's effect.

name: SLOW POISON
description:
Cast before a poison takes full effect, this spell arrests the poison's progress, preventing further harm for the duration. The poison is not neutralized — it resumes once the spell ends — but the window buys time to find a cure.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and a bud of crushed garlic spread on the wound or consumed by the patient.

name: SNAKE CHARM
description:
The caster hypnotizes a group of serpentine creatures within the area. The affected snakes' total HP cannot exceed the caster's maximum HP. Affected creatures sway motionlessly for the duration; they cannot attack but cannot escape either. The spell affects any variety of snake, giant snake, and snake-like monsters (naga, couatl, and similar), provided the HP total holds.

Duration depends on the snakes' state when the spell is cast: if inactive or asleep, 1d4 \+ 2 turns; if active but not aggressive, 1d3 turns; if hostile, 1d4 \+ 4 rounds.

name: SPEAK WITH ANIMALS
description: The caster holds two-way communication with any natural, non-magical animal — warm or cold-blooded — with Intelligence 1 or higher. Questions and answers are possible, but the animal is not automatically cooperative. The GM may call for a reaction check modified by Charisma. Indifferent animals may answer in passing or make irrelevant comments; hostile ones will be terse and evasive. Friendly animals or druids-linked priests may prompt additional favours at the GM's discretion.

name: SPIRITUAL HAMMER
description:
The caster hurls their war hammer at a chosen target within range. The weapon transforms mid-flight into a magical force hammer that the caster controls by concentration. It attacks any visible target within range each round — one target per round, changed at will — on the caster's initiative, using the caster's Attack Bonus but without Strength or Dexterity modifiers. It deals normal war hammer damage with an enchantment bonus of \+1 (levels 1–6), \+2 (levels 7–12), or \+3 (level 13+). Rear-attack bonuses apply when the caster faces the appropriate direction.

The spell ends early if the caster stops concentrating, or if a *Dispel Magic* targets either the caster or the hammer. Magic resistance is checked only on the first attack; if it succeeds the spell ends.

*Material component:* the war hammer itself, thrown at the first target during casting.

name: TRIP
description:
One length of vine, rope, stick, or similar object is enchanted to rise slightly and trip creatures crossing it — including the caster. The trap is 80% undetectable by normal means; the other 20% of the time a careful observer sees the object shift as someone crosses. A failed Reflex save causes the creature to fall and has a 50% chance to drop carried items. Getting up and retrieving items costs a full round. Creatures who know about the trap in advance receive a \+4 bonus to the save, and only a 25% chance to drop items.

Per 1 m of object: 2 small-sized or 1 man-sized creature can be tripped per round. A 3-m object can trip up to 6 small, 3 man-sized, or 1 large creature per round. Huge-sized or larger creatures are immune but still trigger the effect. Running creatures who fail take 1d4 \+ 1 damage and are stunned for the same number of rounds if they fall on hard ground, or 1 damage and 1 round of stun on soft ground.

name: WARP WOOD / STRAIGHTEN WOOD
description:
*Warp Wood* permanently bends and warps wooden items within range, destroying their strength and shape. A warped door springs open or jams (caster's choice, requiring a Strength check to open). A warped boat springs a leak. Warped missile weapons become useless; warped melee weapons impose a −4 penalty to attack rolls.

The caster can warp a 38-cm shaft up to 2.5 cm in diameter per caster level, or the equivalent volume. At 3rd level: 2–3 axe handles or a dozen crossbow bolts. At 5th level: a typical spear shaft. Multiple consecutive castings may warp larger objects. Enchanted wooden items can be affected only if the caster's level exceeds the caster who enchanted them. Permanent magical wooden items are treated as having been enchanted by a 12th-level caster, requiring a 20% cumulative chance per level higher to succeed. Artefacts are unaffected.

The reverse, *Straighten Wood*, warps the same quantity of wood back to its normal, straight condition.

name: WITHDRAW
description:
Time slows for the caster alone. The caster perceives the duration as 2 rounds plus 1 round per caster level, though only 1 round actually passes. During this subjective time, the caster may only think, read, cast any divination spell, or cast a curing or healing spell — but the healing version applies only to themselves. The caster cannot move, use magic items, cast any other type of spell, or become invisible. If attacked, Dexterity and shield bonuses to AC are lost. Performing any restricted action immediately ends the spell.

The GM adjudicates this spell round by round: ask the caster what they intend to do during their mental rounds. Divide the number of perceived rounds by 10 to approximate how many mental rounds occur per initiative count in the real round. Allow the caster's actions up to any point where a restriction is broken or an enemy's attack interrupts them.

name: WYVERN WATCH
description:
An insubstantial wyvern-shaped haze guards the designated area. Creatures approaching within 9 m of the area are attacked by the wyvern; those who fail a Will save are paralyzed for 1 round per caster level, until *Dispel Magic* or *Remove Paralysis* is cast, or the caster voluntarily releases them. A successful save means the wyvern missed — but it attacks again each round until it succeeds at its first hit, which ends the spell immediately.

The wyvern can be seen by creatures approaching within 9 m: 90% chance in bright light, 30% in twilight, and not at all in darkness. Infravision cannot detect it.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: ANIMATE DEAD
description:
The caster animates skeletons or zombies from the remains of dead humans, humanoids, human-adjacent ancestries, giants, or — optionally — animals. Animated undead obey simple commands but cannot be dismissed by *Dispel Magic*. The total HD of undead created cannot exceed the caster's level.

Human-scale creatures with only 1 HD in life become 1 HD skeletons or 2 HD zombies regardless of prior class. Creatures with 1 HD or more retain their HD as skeletons and gain 1 HD as zombies. Undead created this way have none of the special abilities they possessed in life.

Evil casters may use this spell freely. Neutral casters may animate the bodies of fallen enemies from non-player ancestries or do so with express permission from the deceased (via *Speak With Dead*). Good casters prefer to animate animals and approach humanoid bodies only with the same courtesy. Animating a corpse being prepared for *Raise Dead* is universally condemned — the soul requires its original body to return; if that body is animated, resurrection requires *Reincarnate* or *Wish*.

The practical reality: undead smell. Animals avoid them unless specifically trained. Hirelings routinely refuse work with parties known to travel with undead. Most civilizations regulate undead creation; some mandate immediate destruction; even tolerant jurisdictions require registration. Good and lawful casters must either obtain permits where possible or gather bodies only with legal sanction.

*Material component:* a pinch of bone powder or a bone shard.

name: CALL LIGHTNING
description:
The caster calls lightning bolts down from storm clouds. This spell can only be cast outdoors, and only when storm clouds are present or can be summoned within the duration (the GM determines availability). Once the conditions are met, the caster calls one bolt per turn, directing each at any target within the area.

Each bolt strikes a 3-m radius area and deals 2d8 \+ 1d8 per caster level to creatures within it (maximum 10d8). A Reflex save halves the damage. The bolt strikes from directly overhead; it cannot be called in enclosed spaces. If the storm is particularly severe, the GM may increase damage by 1d8 per bolt.

name: CONTINUAL LIGHT / CONTINUAL DARKNESS
description:
*Continual Light* creates a permanent globe of light equivalent to full daylight. It may be cast on an object, creature, or open air. If targeted on a creature's eyes, a failed Reflex save renders it permanently blind until *Remove Blindness* or equivalent magic is applied. The spell may be ended by a *Dispel Magic* or its reverse.

The reverse, *Continual Darkness*, creates permanent magical darkness in the area. Normal and infravision are both useless inside. Casting *Continual Light* directly into the zone cancels both effects until one duration ends naturally.

name: CREATE FOOD & WATER
description: The caster creates sufficient food and water to sustain three man-sized creatures (or one large creature) per caster level for one full day. The food is simple but nutritious and wholesome. It perishes if not consumed within 24 hours.

name: CURE BLINDNESS OR DEAFNESS / CAUSE BLINDNESS OR DEAFNESS
description:
*Cure Blindness or Deafness* reverses many forms of vision or hearing loss — natural or magical — in the creature touched. It cannot regenerate sense organs lost to physical injury or disease, and a single casting cures only one condition.

The reverse, *Cause Blindness or Deafness*, requires a successful touch attack. The creature is then allowed a Fortitude save; on a failure, the caster's choice of magical blindness or deafness is applied permanently until magical removal.

name: CURE DISEASE / CAUSE DISEASE
description:
*Cure Disease* eliminates most parasites and diseases in a creature touched. Recovery time varies — as little as 1 turn, or up to 10 days of rest, depending on how far the disease had progressed. A caster of 12th level or higher may cure lycanthropy if the spell is cast within 3 days of infection. The spell removes existing disease; it provides no immunity to future infection.

The reverse, *Cause Disease*, requires a successful touch attack and a failed Fortitude save from the target. The caster chooses the type at casting:

**Debilitating:** Within 1d6 turns, the creature begins losing 1 Strength per hour until Strength reaches 2, at which point it can barely move. Recovery requires 1d3 weeks of complete rest.

**Fatal:** The disease takes 1d6 months to kill. The creature can no longer benefit from curing spells, heals naturally at 10% of the normal rate, and permanently loses 2 Charisma per month. Only magical removal cures it; lost Charisma is not restored.

name: DISPEL MAGIC
description:
*Dispel Magic* terminates active spells in the target area. The base chance of dispelling a spell is 50% \+ 5% per level by which the dispeller exceeds the original caster, or −5% per level by which the dispeller falls short. Minimum chance to dispel: 5%. Maximum: 95%.

When targeting an area, the most recent spells are affected first, then older ones in reverse order. When targeting an object or creature, all active spells on that target may be dispelled. Permanent magical items are suppressed for 1d4 rounds on a successful dispel, not permanently destroyed.

name: FEIGN DEATH
description:
A willing creature of any level — or the caster themselves — enters a death-like coma. The comatose subject can hear, smell, and understand events but cannot see or feel anything. Physical damage is halved and shows no outward sign. The subject is immune to paralysis and energy drain for the duration; poison takes no effect until the spell ends. This protection does not extend to obviously lethal situations such as being boiled or dissolved.

The priest's version affects only man-sized or smaller humans, human-adjacent ancestries, and humanoids, without HD limitation.

A *Dispel Magic* or a command from the caster ends the spell early; the subject requires 1 full round to recover before acting normally.

name: FLAME WALK
description:
Touched creatures can walk through non-magical fire up to roughly 1,100°C without harm. Against magical fire attacks, they receive a \+2 bonus to saves and damage is reduced to half on a failed save, one-quarter on a success. This spell does not stack with *Resist Fire* or similar protection.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and powdered ruby worth at least 500 gp per creature protected.

name: GLYPH OF WARDING
description:
The caster inscribes a magical glyph to guard a passage, door, container, or chest. The glyph remains nearly invisible until triggered; it may be detected as a magical trap and is subject to *Dispel Magic*, *Read Magic* (35% chance to identify), or removal by a rogue's trap-finding ability. The caster spends 1 round per 1.5 m² of protected area tracing the glyph. Once set, it triggers when any unauthorized creature enters or touches the warded area.

Two versions exist, selected at casting:

**Blast Glyph.** Deals 1d4 damage per caster level (maximum 10d4) in a 1.5-m radius. The caster chooses fire, cold, or lightning at casting. A Reflex save halves the damage.

**Spell Glyph.** Reproduces any harmful priest spell of 3rd level or lower the caster can normally cast, triggered at the caster's level at the time of inscription. A saving throw applies as normal for the stored spell.

*Material components:* powdered diamond worth 40 gp per square metre and incense.

name: HOLD ANIMAL
description: This spell functions as *Hold Person* but targets only non-magical normal or giant-sized birds, mammals, or reptiles. It cannot affect undead animals. Save penalties: −4 if one animal is targeted, −2 if two, −1 if three, none if four.

name: LOCATE OBJECT / OBSCURE OBJECT
description:
*Locate Object* directs the caster toward either a specific known object (one the caster has personally handled) or the nearest example of a general category — swords, stairs, gemstones. A lead coating blocks the spell. The caster cannot search for creatures.

The reverse, *Obscure Object*, prevents an object touched from being found by this spell, crystal balls, or similar divination for 8 hours.

*Material components:* a piece of lodestone.

name: MAGICAL VESTMENT
description:
The caster's ceremonial robes of faith become magically protective, providing AC 15 (equivalent to chain armour), with an additional \+1 per 3 caster levels above 5th, to a maximum of AC 19 at 17th level. Dexterity bonuses to AC apply and stack. This protection cannot be combined with other armour (a shield is the only armour exception) or magical AC bonuses. If the vestment is worn over armour, only the higher AC applies. The spell ends if the caster loses consciousness.

*Material components:* the caster's vestments and holy or unholy symbol, neither consumed.

name: MELD INTO STONE
description:
The caster and all worn or carried equipment merge into a natural stone object of sufficient size. While inside, the caster is fully concealed and protected — physical attacks cannot reach them — but also cannot perceive anything outside the stone and cannot act. The variable duration is determined secretly by the GM; if the caster remains inside after the duration ends, they are expelled from the stone and stunned for 1 round.

The spell may be ended early at will, requiring 1 round. The caster cannot merge into worked stone or artificial constructions — only natural rock. Magical stone may allow the GM to rule differently.

name: NEGATIVE PLANE PROTECTION
description:
The protected creature opens a channel to the Positive Energy Plane. If struck by an attack that drains energy levels — from an undead creature, certain magical items, or specific spells — the protected creature makes a Fortitude save.

Success: a flash of light and thunderclap marks the positive energy countering the drain. The creature still takes any physical damage from the attack, but the level drain is negated. If the source was undead, that creature takes 2d6 damage from the contact.

Failure: the creature takes double the normal physical damage from the attack and suffers the level drain normally.

Either outcome ends the protection. The spell cannot function on the Negative Energy Plane.

name: PLANT GROWTH
description:
Existing vegetation within the area becomes thick, tangled, and overgrown — moving through it without magical assistance or tools costs 1 m of movement per round regardless of normal speed. The overgrowth is permanent until cut back by mundane or magical means. The spell cannot create plants where none exist; it only thickens what is already present.

The reverse, should it become available through research, reduces overgrowth to its previous state.

name: PRAYER
description:
The caster summons divine favour over the field. All allies within the area gain a \+1 bonus to attack rolls, damage, and saving throws for the duration. All enemies within the area suffer a −1 penalty to the same. Unlike *Chant*, the caster is not required to maintain concentration — they may act normally once Prayer is cast. A second caster of the same faith channelling *Chant* during an active *Prayer* increases both effects to \+2 and −2.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: PROTECTION FROM FIRE
description:
Two versions exist, chosen at casting:

**Personal (cast on self):** The caster becomes immune to mundane fire and magical fire until 12 points per caster level of magical fire damage has been absorbed. Once that threshold is reached, the spell ends.

**Non-personal (cast on another):** The recipient becomes immune to mundane fire and gains a \+4 bonus to saves against magical fire, taking only 50% damage on a failed save.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: PYROTECHNICS
description:
The caster transforms an existing fire within range into one of two effects chosen at casting:

**Fireworks:** The fire erupts into flashing, coloured aerial bursts for 1 round. Every creature within 36 m with an unobstructed view is temporarily blinded for 1d4 \+ 1 rounds unless they succeed at a Reflex save. No heat or damage is produced.

**Choking Smoke Screen:** A thick, writhing smoke pours from the fire source, obscuring vision — including infravision — beyond 60 cm. The smoke fills an area up to 100 times the original fire's volume in a roughly spherical shape, lasting 1 round per caster level. Any creature caught in the smoke must make a Fortitude save or choke, suffering a \+2 penalty to AC and a −2 penalty to all attack and saving throw rolls.

The fire source is extinguished unless it exceeds 0.6 m³ in size; larger mundane fires are only partially quenched. Magical fire sources are not extinguished. If a fire-based creature is used as the source, it takes 1d4 \+ 1 damage per caster level (priest version).

*Material component:* the existing fire itself.

name: REMOVE CURSE / BESTOW CURSE
description:
*Remove Curse* lifts a curse from a creature or object. It does not remove the curse's source — a cursed sword touched again will curse the handler again. It enables the disposal of unwanted cursed items. Higher-level or particularly potent curses may require the caster to be of greater level than the curse's caster to remove.

The reverse, *Bestow Curse*, lays a magical curse on a creature touched. A failed Will save inflicts one of the following (caster's choice): −4 to attack rolls; −4 to saves; a 50% chance each round to take no action; or −4 to one ability score (minimum 3). The GM may allow research into other curse effects of similar potency.

name: REMOVE PARALYSIS
description:
The spell frees up to 4 creatures from paralysis — whether from ghoul attacks, *Hold* spells, *Slow*, or similar effects. If cast on one creature, the effect is instantly removed. Cast on two: both creatures receive a new Fortitude save with \+4. Three or more: each gets a new save with \+2.

No physical or magical barrier may exist between the caster and the targeted creatures.

name: SNARE
description:
The caster enchants a length of vine, thong, or rope into a nearly invisible magical snare (90% undetectable by normal means). The snare is set with one end in a loop that contracts around the limbs — or sometimes the neck — of any creature that steps through it. If a strong, supple tree is nearby, the snare yanks the creature upward, dealing 1d6 damage; if no tree is available, the cord entangles the creature in place.

A creature trapped in the snare requires Strength 23 to escape in the first hour; each subsequent hour reduces the required Strength by 1, to a minimum of 18 after 6 hours. The snare has AC 13 and 5 HP; a magical weapon or mundane edged weapon wielded by someone with at least a \+2 Strength bonus or magical bonus to attack can cut through it. After 12 hours, the snare's enchantment fails and it releases automatically.

*Material components:* snakeskin, sinew from a strong animal woven into the cord, and the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: SPEAK WITH DEAD
description:
The caster asks a dead creature a limited number of questions and receives answers in their mind. The creature knows only what it knew in life. The caster must share a language the dead creature knew. An unwilling dead creature whose alignment differs from the caster's, or whose HD at death exceeded the caster's level, is allowed a Will save to refuse.

Dead creatures answer truthfully but tend to interpret questions literally — their answers are typically brief, evasive, and cryptic. No individual corpse may be questioned more than once per week.

| Caster Level | Max Time Dead | Question Time | Questions |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 1–7 | 1 week | 1 round | 2 |
| 7–8 | 1 month | 3 rounds | 3 |
| 9–12 | 1 year | 1 turn | 4 |
| 13–15 | 10 years | 2 turns | 5 |
| 16–20 | 100 years | 3 turns | 6 |
| 21+ | 1,000 years | 1 hour | 7 |

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol, burning incense, and some part of the dead creature's physical remains. The remains are not destroyed.

name: SPIKE GROWTH
description:
Any ground-covering vegetation or surface roots within the area become razor-sharp — as if the ground were carpeted in caltrops — without appearing to change. Creatures moving on foot through the area take 2d4 piercing damage per 3 m of movement. Any creature damaged must succeed at a Reflex save or suffer injuries that reduce movement by one-third (minimum 1\) for 24 hours; only a *Heal* spell or twice the incurred damage in lesser curing removes this penalty.

The trap is invisible to normal perception — not even entering and taking damage reveals the extent of the hazard. Only *True Seeing*, *Detect Traps*, or equivalent magic locates it.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and seven sharp thorns or small sharpened twigs.

name: STAR SHINE
description:
The area is lit with soft starlight equivalent to a bright, moonlit night — clear enough to see by, dim enough to cast soft shadows. Infravision is unaffected. The effect is partially illusory: creatures look upward and perceive a clear night sky above them regardless of actual ceiling height. A Will save to disbelieve reveals only that the stars are illusory. The spell does not function underwater.

*Material components:* holly berries and stalks from a belladonna lily.

name: STONE SHAPE
description:
The caster forms an existing piece of stone into any shape of equivalent volume. Rough shapes are entirely possible; fine detail is not. A stone door may be shaped into a wall, a passage carved into a rock face, a crude idol formed from a boulder. Any shape incorporating moving parts has a 30% chance of not working. The priest's version affects approximately three times the volume of the wizard version.

*Material component:* soft clay worked roughly into the desired shape and pressed to the stone during casting.

name: SUMMON INSECTS
description:
A swarm of biting, stinging insects engulfs one creature within range. The target is unable to cast spells or concentrate on any action requiring sustained focus — spellcasting requires a Fortitude save each round to maintain. The target takes 1 point of damage per round from the bites and stings. Creatures inside a full suit of closed armour (plate and equivalent) are partially protected, taking no distraction penalty and only 1 point of damage per 2 rounds. Smoke, fire, or moving into water disperses the swarm in 1 round.

This spell requires insects to be naturally present in the area. It has no effect in environments where no insects exist.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: TREE
description:
The caster assumes the form of a small, medium, or large tree — their choice among available tree types nearby, within natural size ranges. While in tree form, the caster is indistinguishable from a mundane tree by normal observation. The caster can observe their surroundings, think, and count time; they cannot move, cast spells, or perform any action until they resume their normal form.

Resuming human form requires 1 round. The caster can alternate between forms throughout the duration. *Detect Magic* reveals the transformation; a successful *Dispel Magic* forces return to human form immediately.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: WATER BREATHING / AIR BREATHING
description:
*Water Breathing* enables one or more touched creatures to breathe water for the duration. The total duration is divided equally among all recipients. The spell does not prevent normal air breathing — recipients can breathe in both environments.

The reverse, *Air Breathing*, enables water-breathing creatures to also breathe air for the same duration and under the same terms.

The priest's version requires no material component for either version.

name: WATER WALK
description:
The caster empowers themselves and one additional creature per caster level above 5th to tread on any liquid as if it were solid ground. Mud, oil, snow, quicksand, running water, ice, and even lava can be crossed — the recipients' feet hover a few centimetres above the surface on an invisible field of force. Creatures crossing molten lava still take heat damage from proximity. Normal movement — walking, running, charging — functions across liquid surfaces.

If cast while creatures are submerged, they rise to the surface at a rate of 36 m per round. Unconscious or heavily encumbered subjects rise at 18 m per round. Subjects must also treat liquids as solid for the purpose of fall damage.

*Material components:* a piece of cork and the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: ABJURE / IMPLORE
description:
*Abjure* sends an extra-planar creature — other than a deity or demigod — back to its home plane. The caster must speak the creature's name and type, including its true name and title if known. The caster must then overcome any magic resistance the creature possesses. If that succeeds, the caster rolls 1d20 \+ (caster level − creature's HD), with a result of 11 or more sending it away. If the creature's HD exceeds the caster's level, the difference is a penalty. A banished creature must succeed at a Fortitude save on arrival or die; if its Constitution is unknown, the survival check is 70% \+ 2% per HD.

If the spell fails, the caster cannot attempt to abjure the same creature again until gaining another caster level.

The reverse, *Implore*, summons a willing extra-planar creature of the caster's alignment (and less than demigod status). Unwilling creatures resist the call with magic resistance and the same 1d20 \+ difference roll. Success does not guarantee goodwill.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol, holy or unholy water, and material that opposes or attracts the target creature (for *Abjure* and *Implore* respectively).

name: ANIMAL SUMMONING I
description:
The caster calls up to eight specific animals of 4 HD or fewer. Only animals within range at the moment of casting can answer. The GM determines availability and numbers. The caster may attempt up to three different animal types per casting, until eight animals are summoned or all attempts are exhausted. Summoned animals are loyal to the caster and remain in service until a task is completed, the duration runs out, or they are killed.

Only natural, non-magical animals can be summoned.

name: CALL WOODLAND BEINGS
description:
The caster calls one type of woodland creature by name. The casting continues — naming up to three different types in sequence — for a maximum of 2 turns, or until one type answers. Creatures that hear the call are allowed a Will save at −4 to refuse. Those who fail travel immediately to the caster and are friendly, helping within reason. Unlike conjured creatures, they make morale checks and will not perform acts against their nature.

If the caster or any companion is evil, the woodland beings are allowed a new Will save with a \+4 bonus when they come within 9 m. If this second save succeeds, they flee.

The spell functions only outdoors or in wilderness. If the caster knows the true name of a specific creature, that creature can hear the call from twice the normal range.

*Material components:* a pinecone and eight holly berries.

name: CLOAK OF BRAVERY / CLOAK OF FEAR
description:
*Cloak of Bravery* bestows a fear resistance bonus on up to four willing creatures touched. The bonus varies with the number affected: \+4 for one creature, \+3 for two, \+2 for three, \+1 for four. The protection covers the first fear attack each subject faces; whether they save or not, the cloak's effect on that creature ends. If no fear attack occurs within 8 hours, the spell expires.

The reverse, *Cloak of Fear*, empowers one creature to radiate a 1-m aura of fear on command. All creatures in the aura — including allies — must make a Will save or flee for 2d8 rounds and have a 50% chance to drop held items. The aura can be activated once, within 8 hours of casting. Undead are immune.

*Material components:* an eagle or hawk feather. The reverse requires tail feathers from a vulture or chicken.

name: CONTROL TEMPERATURE
description:
The caster raises or lowers the temperature in their immediate area by up to 6°C per caster level. While it does not halt precipitation or wind, this keeps the caster and companions comfortably warm or cool even in extreme conditions. It also provides a measure of protection against fire or cold damage: if a fire or cold attack strikes a creature in the area, the spell reduces the damage by 5 points per caster level (regardless of whether the save was made). The spell ends after absorbing its first such attack.

*Material component:* a strip of willow bark to lower temperature; raspberry leaves to raise it.

name: CURE SERIOUS WOUNDS / CAUSE SERIOUS WOUNDS
description: Functions as *Cure Light Wounds*, but restores — or inflicts — 2d8 \+ 1 HP.

name: DETECT LIE / UNDETECTABLE LIE
description:
*Detect Lie* reveals when the subject is knowingly and deliberately speaking falsehoods. The caster does not learn the truth — only that an untruth was spoken. The subject is allowed a Will save when the spell is first cast, at a penalty equal to the caster's Wisdom mental defense modifier; the caster does not know whether the save succeeded. If it fails, the GM reports each answer as truthful, completely dishonest, or a minor exaggeration.

The reverse, *Undetectable Lie*, prevents detection of intentional spoken falsehoods on the subject for 24 hours, or until a *Dispel Magic* succeeds.

*Material component:* 1 gp of gold dust for *Detect Lie*; brass dust for the reverse.

name: DIVINATION
description:
The caster requests divine guidance regarding a specific goal, event, or activity that will occur within the next week. Base chance of a useful reply: 60% \+ 1% per caster level, modified by the GM based on the nature of the question. The caster knows if the spell fails, unless false-reading magic is active. A successful result yields a short phrase, cryptic verse, or omen — the GM determines both the form and the content. If the guidance goes unheeded for a week, events will have changed enough to render it obsolete.

*Material components:* a sacrificial offering, incense, and the caster's holy or unholy symbol. Particularly critical guidance may require valuable sacrifices.

name: FREE ACTION
description:
The recipient moves and acts normally even under effects that restrict movement — *Hold*, paralysis, *Slow*, *Web*, *Entangle*, and similar. They can also slip free from the tightest mundane bonds or snares. Underwater, the recipient swims at normal speed and inflicts normal melee damage, without the need to breathe unless other magic provides that.

*Material component:* a leather thong tied around the arm or equivalent limb, which crumbles to dust when the spell ends.

name: GIANT INSECT / SHRINK INSECT
description:
*Giant Insect* enlarges normal, non-magical insects of one type up to giant size. The maximum HD per insect and total HD available depend on caster level:

| Caster Level | Max HD per Insect | Max Total HD |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 7–9 | 3 | 9 |
| 10–12 | 4 | 12 |
| 13+ | 6 | 15 |

Giant insects obey the caster's simple one-word commands and hand gestures (attack, guard, stop, fly, land) and will not harm the caster unless magically influenced after enlargement. Left unguided, they attack the nearest other creature. If the casting is interrupted, or the insects were under any magical influence when targeted, the spell fails and the insects die. Arachnids, crustaceans, and intelligent insect-like creatures are unaffected.

The reverse, *Shrink Insect*, reduces giant insects. The caster's maximum total HD (from the table) is subtracted from the targeted insects' HD. If an insect reaches 0 HD, it becomes normal size; otherwise the spell fails.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: HALLUCINATORY FOREST / BREAK HALLUCINATION
description:
*Hallucinatory Forest* creates a convincing illusory forest complete with sight, sound, smell, and feel. Buildings, equipment, and creatures are not concealed. Natural hazards — pools, quicksand — retain their actual properties. Druids, nature-dedicated priests, and intelligent woodland beings (centaurs, dryads) automatically disbelieve. All other creatures believe the forest is real and behave accordingly. No amount of ordinary scrutiny convinces non-druids otherwise; only the reverse or a *Dispel Magic* cast inside the area ends the spell.

The reverse, *Break Hallucination*, automatically ends a *Hallucinatory Forest* within its area of effect.

name: HOLD PLANT
description: All vegetation in the targeted area — including mobile plants, intelligent plants, fungi, slimes, molds, and magically animated plants — freezes completely. Affected plants cannot move, grab, entangle, close, grow, or produce sound. The caster may target 1–4 individual plants within a 12-m square, or 1–4 patches of smaller ground cover (4 m × 4 m each). Non-mobile, unintelligent plants with no HD are automatically affected. Mobile or intelligent plants make Will saves, with penalties: −4 for one target, −2 for two, −1 for three, none for four.

name: IMBUE WITH SPELL ABILITY
description:
The caster transfers memorized spells into another creature, giving that creature the ability to cast them. The transfer is permanent — the caster effectively loses those spell slots — until the recipient casts the spells or is killed.

Only non-magical, non-spellcasting creatures with Wisdom 9 or higher may receive the enchantment — a fighter or rogue with high Wisdom qualifies, but not a wizard or creature with any innate magical ability. Only spells from the Divination, Protection, or Healing spheres may be transferred. Attempting to transfer any other type ends the spell immediately.

The maximum number and level of spells that can be transferred are determined by the recipient's level; the GM adjudicates this based on the system established in Chapter 3\.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and a minor item from the recipient symbolic of their class.

name: LOWER WATER / RAISE WATER
description:
*Lower Water* drains water or similar liquids by the effective area volume, creating a trough. The liquid cannot be reduced below 2.5 cm depth. Cast on a large body of water, it creates a whirlpool; swimmers, boats, and ships become trapped and risk capsizing. Cast on a water elemental, it imposes the equivalent of a *Slow* spell with no save. The water returns to its previous level when the duration ends.

The reverse, *Raise Water*, raises a body of liquid by the effective area, up to its natural maximum level — flood stage, high tide. Ships, boats, and swimmers are forced outward. At duration's end, water returns to normal.

The priest's version uses a 30-m wide trough instead of 6 m. *Material components:* a vial of dust (*Lower Water*) or water (*Raise Water*), plus the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: NEUTRALIZE POISON / POISON
description:
*Neutralize Poison* eliminates any venom within a creature or object touched — a victim, a poisoned weapon, even a venomous creature. An unwilling creature requires a successful touch attack; touching a held poisoned weapon requires a touch attack with the called-shot penalty. A poison victim can be saved if the spell reaches them before death. The spell removes existing poison but does not prevent re-envenomation.

The reverse, *Poison*, requires a successful touch attack. A failed Fortitude save incapacitates the victim for 1 turn and then kills them. *Slow Poison* remains effective against this; only *Neutralize Poison* or stronger magic cures it outright.

name: PLANT DOOR
description:
The caster opens an ethereal passage through dense natural or magical plant growth — trees, undergrowth, thickets — but not through plant-based creatures. The plants must be packed closely enough to fill the passage; any air gap large enough for a man-sized creature breaks it. Only the caster, a higher-level caster, or a dryad can perceive the passage entrance. If the plants are destroyed while the caster is inside, they must exit before the plants are gone or they die along with them.

*Plant Door* can be chained with another *Plant Door* and, under the right conditions, linked to a *Pass Wall* or *Phase Door* to create longer passages through mixed terrain.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: PRODUCE FIRE / QUENCH FIRE
description:
*Produce Fire* creates a normal fire within the area for 1 round. The fire ignites combustibles that fail a save versus mundane fire, and creatures in the area take 1d4 \+ 1 damage per caster level.

The reverse, *Quench Fire*, douses all normal non-magical fires within the same area.

*Material components:* sulphur and wax formed into a paste orb, thrown at the target area.

name: PROTECTION FROM EVIL, 9-METRE RADIUS / PROTECTION FROM GOOD, 9-METRE RADIUS
description:
Functions as *Protection From Evil*, centred on and moving with one targeted creature, but covers a 9-m radius and lasts longer. Any protected creature in the radius that makes a melee attack against an extra-planar, conjured, or summoned creature ends the barrier against such creatures for that individual. If the targeted creature is larger than the radius, the spell protects only that creature.

*Material components:* a 6-m circle of powdered silver (or iron for the reverse) and burning incense (or smoldering dung for the reverse), drawn on the ground during casting, plus holy water (or unholy water for the reverse).

name: PROTECTION FROM LIGHTNING
description:
Two versions, chosen at casting:

**Personal (cast on self):** The caster becomes immune to all electrical attacks until the spell has absorbed 10 points of electrical damage per caster level.

**Non-personal:** The recipient gains a \+4 bonus to saves against electrical attacks and takes only 50% damage from them.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: REFLECTING POOL
description:
A naturally occurring pool of still water, no more than 60 cm diameter per caster level, is enchanted to function as a crystal ball. Its reach extends to the Ethereal Plane and Inner Planes only. Detect Magic, Detect Snares & Pits, and Detect Poison may be cast through the pool with a 5% chance of success per caster level. The image is always slightly hazy — text cannot be read through the pool. The GM may limit use to once per day.

*Material components:* three 30-gram measures of refined hickory, walnut, or similar oil, dropped on the pool's surface.

name: REPEL INSECTS
description:
An invisible barrier prevents insects from entering the area. Normal and giant insects with fewer HD than one-third the caster's level cannot enter at all. Monstrous insects with HD equal to one-third or more the caster's level can force through on a Will save, but take 1d6 damage as they pass the barrier. Arachnids, crustaceans, and intelligent insect-like beings are unaffected.

*Material component:* crushed marigold, crushed leek, crushed stinging nettle, or a lump of camphor resin.

name: SPEAK WITH PLANTS
description:
Functions as *Speak With Animals*, but enables two-way communication with all plant life within the area — mundane plants, plant creatures, fungi, and plantlike monsters. Plant creatures with Intelligence 1 or higher are treated as animals for reaction purposes. Normal plants are always friendly and can be commanded to entangle opponents (as *Entangle*), part to allow passage, or release ensnared creatures. They cannot uproot and walk, but can stretch, lean, and flex within their nature.

*Material components:* a pinch of burning dung and a drop of water.

name: SPELL IMMUNITY—DIVINE
description:
The recipient becomes immune to one specific spell effect, named by the caster at casting. The effect may come from a spell, a creature's special ability, or a magical item. The caster must have personally experienced the named effect at least once. Breath weapons and gaze attacks cannot be blocked by this spell. The recipient must not already benefit from a potion, ring, or other magical protection.

*Material component:* the same material component (if any) required by the named spell being protected against.

name: STICKS TO SNAKES / SNAKES TO STICKS
description:
*Sticks to Snakes* transforms sticks — no smaller than a torch, no larger than a staff — into snakes under the caster's command. Sticks in another creature's possession may avoid the effect if the creature makes a Reflex save. Magical staves, wands, and spears cannot be affected. The caster has a 5% chance per caster level to create venomous snakes.

The reverse, *Snakes to Sticks*, transforms normal, non-magical snakes into sticks for the duration, and can also partially reverse an active *Sticks to Snakes* effect.

*Material components:* the sticks (or snakes), a piece of bark, and several snake scales.

name: TONGUES / CONFUSE TONGUES
description:
*Tongues* allows the caster to speak and understand one additional language per three caster levels. The caster need not name a specific language — they may simply target any creature within the area and the spell bridges the gap. All creatures in the area understand the caster, though they are not automatically cooperative. The caster cannot communicate with animals or mindless creatures with this spell.

The reverse, *Confuse Tongues*, disrupts all speech within the area, or cancels an active *Tongues* effect.

The priest's version requires no material component.

name: AIR WALK
description:
A large-sized or smaller creature walks on air as if it were solid ground. The recipient can move skyward at a maximum 45-degree angle at half their normal speed and descend at 45 degrees or less at normal speed. Strong winds add or subtract 3 m of movement per 16 km/h of velocity.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and a bit of thistledown.

name: ANIMAL GROWTH / SHRINK ANIMAL
area: Up to 8 animals in a 6-m square
description:
*Animal Growth* doubles the size of up to eight animals in the area. Their HD, HP, and damage are doubled, improving attacks and saves. Movement and AC are unaffected. Weight and carrying capacity are multiplied by 8\.

The reverse, *Shrink Animal*, halves the animals' size, halving HD, HP, and damage and reducing their attack and save rolls. Movement and AC are unaffected. Weight and carrying capacity are multiplied by 0.125.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and a scrap of food.

name: ANIMAL SUMMONING II
description: Functions as *Animal Summoning I*, but calls up to six animals of 8 HD or less, or twelve animals of 4 HD or less.

name: ANTI-PLANT SHELL
description: An invisible mobile barrier surrounds the caster, preventing all living plants and vegetable matter from entering. Inanimate grass and flowers are harmlessly pushed aside or uprooted. Forcing the barrier against an animated, attacking, or intelligent plant (such as a treant) collapses the field and ends the spell.

name: ATONEMENT
description:
This spell removes the burden of unwilling or unknowing misdeeds and the effects of magical alignment changes from a willing, repentant creature. It cannot atone for deliberate sins — those require the *Quest* spell. The subject must be unaffected by magical compulsion and must genuinely repent.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol, a prayer wheel, prayer beads or book, and incense.

name: COMMUNE
description:
The caster contacts their deity or divine agents, asking one question per caster level. Questions must be posed quickly — hesitation, distraction, or discussion ends the spell. Answers are typically one word: yes, no, maybe, never, unknown, unrelated, or a phrase of no more than five words. The answers are true within the deity's knowledge, though deities are not omniscient and may occasionally shape their replies to their own ends.

The GM is strongly advised to limit this spell to once per adventure or week at most.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol, holy or unholy water, and incense. If a particularly powerful commune is desired, a sacrifice proportionate to the obscurity of the answers may be required.

name: COMMUNE WITH NATURE
description:
The caster learns one piece of detailed information per caster level about their natural surroundings. Outdoors, the range is 800 m per caster level. In natural underground caverns, it shrinks to 9 m per caster level. The spell does not function in artificial environments such as cities or dungeons.

The caster asks about specific knowledge in a given direction: ecological health, ground composition, water or mineral presence, plant types, animal or humanoid populations, or the presence of unnatural creatures. As with *Commune*, the GM should limit use to once per adventure or week.

name: CONTROL WINDS
description: The caster alters the wind speed and direction within the area by up to one category per 3 caster levels, or 3 categories at 9th level (see the weather table under *Control Weather*). Wind speed shifts by 5 km/h per round until the target category is reached. Within a 12-m radius of the caster, the wind is always calm; in confined spaces, this calm area shrinks proportionately. Once the spell ends, wind returns to its previous strength at the same rate of change.

name: CURE CRITICAL WOUNDS / CAUSE CRITICAL WOUNDS
description: Functions as *Cure Light Wounds*, but restores — or inflicts — 3d8 \+ 3 HP.

name: DISPEL EVIL / DISPEL GOOD
description:
While this spell is active, evil extra-planar creatures, summoned evil creatures, and creatures summoned by evil casters suffer a −7 penalty to attack rolls against the caster. If any such creature is touched (requiring a successful touch attack), it must make a Will save or be sent back to its plane of origin.

Additionally, if an enchantment cast by an evil creature is subject to *Dispel Magic*, this spell removes it automatically on touch.

Each use of the banishment or dispel effect ends the spell immediately.

The reverse, *Dispel Good*, functions identically against good creatures and enchantments.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and holy or unholy water.

name: FLAME STRIKE
description:
A vertical column of divine fire descends from overhead, dealing 6d8 damage to all creatures in the area and igniting combustibles. A Reflex save halves the damage.

*Material component:* a pinch of sulphur.

name: INSECT PLAGUE
description:
The caster summons a thick, stationary cloud of insects anywhere within range. The GM must determine whether enough insects are naturally available in the environment; if not, the spell fails. Creatures within the cloud have their vision reduced to 3 m and cannot cast spells. The insects bypass armour and deal 1 point of damage per round, even to invisible creatures.

Creatures of 2 HD or less automatically flee in a random direction until more than 220 m from the cloud. Those below 5 HD must make morale checks each round or also flee. PCs do not make morale checks. The cloud can be dispersed by a *Dispel Magic* or heavy smoke, strong winds, large fires, or ice storms covering the full area.

*Material components:* a pinch of sugar and a few kernels of grain smeared with fat.

name: MAGIC FONT
description:
The caster enchants a sacred basin of holy or unholy water to act as a crystal ball for the spell's duration: 1 round per vial of holy or unholy water poured in, up to a maximum of 1 hour. The caster must be in good standing with their faith. The font can also be used to cast *Detect Magic*, *Detect Snares & Pits*, or *Detect Poison* through its surface, with a 5% chance of success per caster level per attempt (each attempt requiring 1 round of concentration).

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol, the font itself, and 1–60 vials of holy or unholy water.

name: MOONBEAM
description:
The caster projects a beam of natural moonlight onto any area within range, movable once per round by pointing. The light is soft and shadowy — clear enough to see by but dim enough to cast shadows. It highlights creatures trying to hide in shadows within the area and casts faint light for 9 m beyond the main radius, but produces no visible light beyond 9 m. The spell does not interfere with infravision. Its light has all the properties of natural moonlight, including the ability to trigger lycanthropic changes.

*Material components:* seeds from a moonseed plant and a piece of moonstone.

name: PASS PLANT
description:
The caster enters a living tree large enough to contain them and, in a single round, exits through another living tree of the same species, regardless of the distance between them. The caster states a direction during casting; movement places them in the closest appropriate tree in that direction. If no suitable tree exists in the desired direction, the nearest qualifying tree in any direction is used. If no qualifying tree exists at all within range, the command to move fails.

Maximum travel distances by tree species:

| Tree Type | Distance |
| ----- | ----- |
| Oak | 540 m |
| Ash | 490 m |
| Yew | 435 m |
| Elm | 380 m |
| Linden | 325 m |
| Other deciduous | 275 m |
| Coniferous | 220 m |
| Other | 165 m |

If the entry tree is destroyed while the caster is inside, they die.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: PLANE SHIFT
description:
The caster and up to eight other creatures (linked by holding hands) physically travel to another plane. The group arrives 1d100 miles from the intended destination. They remain on the new plane until returned by similar means.

An unwilling target requires a successful touch attack followed by a failed Will save. Unusual energy fields or magical effects may make the spell hazardous or impossible at the GM's discretion.

*Material component:* a small forked metal rod. The specific metal and size determines the destination plane; planar forks must be acquired through play.

name: QUEST
description:
The caster lays a magical directive upon one creature, compelling it to perform a specific task and return with proof of completion. Creatures sharing the caster's faith who are undergoing atonement receive no saving throw, and the quest is reasonable reparation. Creatures who willingly accept or are tricked into a quest gain no save. Unwilling creatures are allowed a Will save; those of the caster's alignment save at −4. Unreasonable quests grant up to \+4 to the save.

A creature that delays, deviates, or perverts the quest accumulates −1 per day to all saving throws until it resumes the task. The penalty remains until the quest is fulfilled or lifted by the caster.

*Quest* is immune to *Dispel Magic* and *Mage's Disjunction*. Only the caster, another priest of the same faith who outlevels the caster at casting, or (rarely) divine intervention can lift it.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: RAINBOW
description:
One of two effects, chosen at casting:

**Bow:** A magical composite short bow of shimmering rainbow light appears, along with 7 enchanted arrows — each a different spectral colour. The bow functions as \+2 for any creature permitted to use bows with Strength 6 or higher. Each colour has a special effect: red doubles damage against fire-based creatures; orange against earth-based; yellow against plant-based; green against water-based; blue against air-based; indigo against acid-and-poison users; violet against metal creatures and regenerators. After all seven arrows are fired, the bow vanishes. Magic resistance completely negates each arrow's damage on a successful check.

**Bridge:** A rainbow-coloured bridge 1 m wide per caster level, spanning 18–120 m, appears for the duration or until the caster dismisses it.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol, a vial of holy or unholy water, and a visible rainbow — or a diamond worth at least 1,000 gp blessed with *Bless* and *Prayer* in sight of a rainbow. The water and diamond are consumed.

name: RAISE DEAD / SLAY LIVING
description:
*Raise Dead* restores life to a dead creature whose soul remains bound to the material world — humans, dwarves, gnomes, halflings, and similar ancestries. The creature must have been dead no longer than 1 day per caster level. Death caused by a death spell or effect cannot be reversed by this spell; constructs, elementals, outer-planar beings, and most undead cannot be raised.

The body must be complete; missing parts are not restored. Poison, disease, and afflictions persist and must be treated separately. A creature dead of old age cannot be raised.

The creature must succeed at a Fortitude save (Constitution check) to survive the return. Success: the creature is restored with 1 HP, loses 1 Constitution permanently, and must rest 1 day per day spent dead. If the save fails, only a *Wish* can restore them. Each time a character is raised, their maximum number of future raises is reduced by 1 (equal to their starting Constitution score in total).

The reverse, *Slay Living*, kills any creature within range unless it succeeds at a Fortitude save versus death. Even a successful save inflicts 2d8 \+ 1 HP of damage.

name: SPIKE STONES
description:
Rocky ground or stone floors within the area reshape into long, sharp points that blend invisibly into the surface. Creatures moving through the area on foot move at half speed and are attacked each round by the trap, using the caster's Attack Bonus, dealing 1d4 per successful hit. Charging or running creatures suffer two attacks per round. A careful observer has a 25% chance to notice the hazard before entering.

Any creature damaged must make a Reflex save or suffer foot and leg injuries that halve movement (minimum 1\) for 24 hours; only a *Heal* spell or twice the incurred HP in curing removes the penalty. When used above a pit trap, the spike stones attack creatures falling into it (6 attacks per 3 m fallen at \+2 bonus, with a \+2 bonus per 3 m to damage), in addition to normal falling damage.

*Material components:* four small stalactites.

name: TRANSMUTE ROCK TO MUD / TRANSMUTE MUD TO ROCK
description:
*Transmute Rock to Mud* converts natural, uncut stone within the area into mud of equivalent volume. Magical or enchanted stone is unaffected. Mud depth cannot exceed 3 m.

Small-sized or smaller creatures, those adapted to muddy surfaces, or those that can fly or levitate avoid effects entirely. Larger creatures sink: one-third of their height per round until chest-deep (movement 1, −2 attack, \+2 AC penalty), and fully submerged by the third round. Creatures more than 3 m tall sink to the bottom immediately and wade. Cast overhead, the mud falls, dealing 8d6 damage (Reflex save for half).

Mud lasts until *Dispel Magic* or the reverse restores it, or until it naturally dries over 1d6 days per 3 m³.

The reverse, *Transmute Mud to Rock*, permanently hardens mud or quicksand into soft stone. Creatures in the mud get a Reflex save to escape before hardening.

*Material components:* clay and water. The reverse uses sand, lime, and water.

name: TRUE SEEING / FALSE SEEING
description:
The priest's *True Seeing* functions as the wizard version, with these differences: the visual range is 36 m; the caster can see a creature's aura and determine alignment; and the spell is reversible. Once per round, the subject may shift their vision to perceive the Ethereal, Astral, or adjacent planes.

The reverse, *False Seeing*, overlays false appearances on what the subject sees for the duration. An unwilling victim requires a successful touch attack — tricking a willing subject is more common. The caster cannot make things appear where nothing exists or render something invisible, but can substitute one appearance for another within a 50% size variance. Because the caster directly overrides the subject's senses, this fools even a subject using *True Seeing*, provided the caster knows the subject is perceiving with such magic and concentrates throughout.

*Material component:* an eye ointment made from powdered mushroom, saffron, and fat, costing at least 300 gp per dose and aged 1d6 months. The reverse uses oil, poppy dust, and pink orchid essence at the same cost.

name: WALL OF FIRE
description:
The priest's version creates a wall identical in shape to the wizard's, but the flames are yellowish-green. Creatures passing through the wall take 4d4 \+ 1 per caster level damage. The heat side of the wall deals 2d4 damage to creatures within 3 m and 1d4 to those within 6 m each round. The wall lasts while the caster concentrates, plus 1 round per caster level thereafter.

*Material component:* phosphorus.

name: AERIAL SERVANT
description:
An invisible aerial servant is summoned from the Elemental Plane of Air. Aerial servants cannot fight; they can only locate and retrieve one specified object or creature. The caster must be protected by a *Protection From Evil* effect, inside a warding circle, or wielding a special controlling item, or the servant will immediately try to kill its summoner and return home.

The servant can carry up to 450 kg and tracks any target automatically within 24 hours of their passing. It returns when its task is complete, the duration expires, it is killed, or a *Dispel Magic* is cast against it. It attacks non-lethally — a successful hit means it has grabbed the target, and the target cannot physically escape without magic.

An aerial servant gains surprise against targets who cannot see invisible creatures; those who can suffer −2 on surprise checks.

name: ANIMAL SUMMONING III
description: Functions as *Animal Summoning I*, but calls up to four animals of 16 HD or less, eight of 8 HD or less, or sixteen of 4 HD or less.

name: ANIMATE OBJECT
description:
The caster brings inanimate, non-flesh objects within the area to life — wood, stone, metal, cloth, and similar. The total volume animated increases with caster level:

| Object Size | Volume | HD | Move | AC | Damage |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Tiny (15 cm) | 0.015 m³ | ½ | 4 | 14 | 1d2 |
| Small (30 cm) | 0.03 m³ | 1+1 | 3 | 14 | 1d4 |
| Medium (60 cm) | 0.06 m³ | 2+2 | 3 | 14 | 1d6 |
| Large (120 cm) | 0.12 m³ | 4+3 | 2 | 15 | 2d4 |
| Huge (240 cm) | 0.24 m³ | 8+4 | 2 | 16 | 3d4 |
| Gigantic (480 cm+) | 0.48 m³ | 14+6 | 1 | 18 | 4d4 |

Animated objects obey the caster's verbal commands. Attempting to animate objects in a creature's possession allows the creature a Reflex save to prevent it. Animated objects save as warriors. Movement bonuses apply by form: two legs \+1, multiple legs \+2, wheeled \+4.

Special abilities by shape: sheet-like objects can blind opponents (wrap around their face); flexible objects like ropes can constrict; large objects can trample.

name: ANTI-ANIMAL SHELL
description:
