A hemisphere of force moves with the caster, blocking the entry of any creature that is wholly or partially a natural creature of the Prime Material Plane — including fey, humanoids, monsters, and giants. Purely magical, conjured, or extra-planar creatures are unaffected. Forcing the barrier against any blocked creature collapses the field.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and a handful of pepper.

name: BLADE BARRIER
description: The caster creates a stationary wall of spinning blades centred on a point within range. Any creature attempting to pass through takes 8d8 damage. Creatures caught in the area when the barrier is created may make a Reflex save to automatically dodge out of the area; those who succeed take no damage.

name: CONJURE ANIMALS
description:
The caster conjures a number of HD of animals equal to twice their caster level (for random animals) or up to their caster level in HD (for a requested animal type). Animals follow verbal commands to attack; they vanish when the spell ends or they are killed. Commanding them to perform non-combat tasks makes them angry — they may refuse, flee, or attack the caster at the GM's discretion.

Random animal type is determined by HD on the table in FG\&G; the GM may use or adapt this to the Known World's bestiary when Book III is complete.

name: CONJURE FIRE ELEMENTAL
description:
The caster conjures a powerful fire elemental. Roll: 65% chance of a 12 HD elemental; 20% a 16 HD elemental; 9% a pair of salamanders; 4% an efreeti; 2% a 20 \+ 1d4 HD fire elemental. The elemental obeys verbal commands and treats the caster as a friend, remaining until destroyed, struck by *Dispel Magic*, *Dismissal*, or *Holy Word*, or the duration expires.

The reverse dismisses one fire elemental within range without benefit of a save.

name: FIND THE PATH / LOSE THE PATH
description:
*Find the Path* reveals to the subject the quickest, most direct route to and from any named location on the same plane. The knowledge comes progressively as needed — not all at once — guiding the subject step by step. The route accounts for traps (revealing when to step over a wire, what password bypasses a *Glyph of Warding*) without disabling them. It gives no information about creatures along the route. If trapped in a *Maze* spell, the subject escapes in 1 round.

The location must be a known or named place; the subject cannot find something based solely on a creature or object's presence.

The reverse, *Lose the Path*, causes the target (who must be touched and who then fails a Reflex save) to become unable to find their way for the duration. They can follow others but cannot navigate independently.

*Material component:* a set of divination tokens (bones, cards, carved runes). The reverse requires a marked or deliberately biased set.

name: FIRE SEEDS
description:
One effect is chosen at casting:

**Acorn Grenades:** Up to 4 acorns are enchanted and can be thrown up to 36 m (ignoring range penalties; attack roll required). Each bursts on impact in a 3-m radius, dealing 2d8 \+ 1 per caster level fire damage; combustibles must save or ignite. Direct hits take full damage with no save.

**Holly Berry Bombs:** Up to 8 holly berries become remote-triggered bombs. They are too light to throw effectively (landing 1d6 × 30 cm away) and work best as placed charges. They detonate on a command word spoken within 36 m of placement, dealing 1d8 \+ 1 per caster level fire damage in a 1.5-m burst.

*Material component:* the appropriate seed type.

name: FORBIDDANCE
description:
This spell wards a consecrated area against unauthorized entry — including teleportation, plane shifting, and ethereal passage. A *Dispel Magic* can only be cast by a caster of equal or greater level than the original caster. Creatures violating the ward are affected based on alignment disparity:

**Identical alignment:** No effect if unlocked; speak the password (if set) to enter freely.

**Different on law/chaos axis:** May enter with the password; without it, a Fortitude save — failure causes 2d6 damage and permanent exclusion for the spell's duration.

**Different on good/evil axis:** Whether locked or not, a Fortitude save is required — failure causes 4d6 damage and permanent exclusion. Even those who save feel uneasy inside.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol, holy or unholy water, and rare incense worth 1,000 gp per 18-m cube. Password-locking requires an additional 5,000 gp of incense per cube.

name: HEAL / HARM
description:
*Heal* completely restores all lost HP and cures all afflictions — disease, poison, most blindness, deafness, and insanity, including the effects of *Feeble Mind*. It cannot affect the undead, extra-planar creatures, or non-corporeal beings. It does not prevent future damage.

The reverse, *Harm*, requires a successful touch attack and reduces the target's HP to 1d4, simultaneously infecting them with a debilitating disease (as *Cause Disease*). No save is allowed against either effect.

name: HEROES' FEAST
description:
A complete feast materialises — food, drink, table, chairs, and serving ware — sufficient for one man-sized creature per caster level. The feast requires a full hour to consume. If interrupted before completion, the spell ends without benefit. If completed, each feaster immediately recovers 1d4 \+ 4 HP, is cured of all disease, and gains the effects of *Bless* and immunity to poison, fear, hopelessness, and panic for 12 hours thereafter.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and fermented honey from royal bee larvae.

name: LIVE OAK
description:
A healthy oak or similar tree near the caster's home, a sacred site, or an item the caster wants guarded becomes a guardian treant-equivalent. The caster cannot have more than one *Live Oak* active at a time. The tree activates on a trigger phrase of up to one word per caster level stated at casting — for example, "attack any creature approaching the vault without speaking 'ember'." The enchanted tree has AC 20, 2 attacks dealing 4d6 each, and a movement of 3\. If damaged, it regenerates to full HP between uses.

| Tree Size | Height | HD | Damage |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Small | 3.5–4.5 m | 8 | 2d8 |
| Medium | 5–6 m | 10 | 3d6 |
| Large | 6–7 m+ | 12 | 4d6 |

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: PART WATER
description:
A trough 9 m wide parts through water or similar liquids within range, holding open for the duration. Swimmers, boats, and ships can enter only with great effort. Cast on a water elemental or water-based creature, it acts as *Slow* with no save. If cast underwater, it creates an air shaft of the area's dimensions.

The priest's version creates a 9-m wide trough.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: SPEAK WITH MONSTERS
description: Functions as *Speak With Animals*, but enables two-way communication with any living creature of Intelligence 1 or higher, regardless of the form that communication takes — including pheromonal, tactile, or empathic signals. Only one creature type can be communicated with per round; the caster may switch types each round.

name: STONE TELL
description:
The caster speaks with natural or worked stone within the area, who can describe who or what has touched them, and what is concealed behind or under them. Stones answer truthfully but their perspective, perception, and knowledge are limited — they may not know what kind of creature touched them, only that something did.

*Material components:* a bit of soft clay and a drop of mercury.

name: TRANSMUTE WATER TO DUST / TRANSMUTE DUST TO WATER
description:
*Transmute Water to Dust* reduces a volume of water-based liquid to fine dust. The dust is magical for 1 round — *Dispel Magic* can restore the water during that window. If the area still contains water after the spell, it mixes with the dust to form silt-laden mud. Cast on a creature of the Elemental Plane of Water, the priest's version slays the creature unless it succeeds at a Fortitude save (a successful save inflicts only 1d6 per caster level).

The reverse, *Transmute Dust to Water*, functions as a powerful *Create Water*, producing clean, normal water from the area.

*Material components:* diamond dust worth 500 gp and a bit of seashell. The reverse adds a pinch of dust.

name: TRANSPORT VIA PLANTS
description:
The caster enters any living plant large enough to accommodate them and exits through another living plant of the same species in a single round, regardless of distance. The caster need not know the destination plant, only the direction and approximate distance; arrival is within 1.5 km, with a 20% − 1%/level chance of ending up 1d100 km off course instead.

The caster may bring equipment up to their carry weight, plus one willing man-sized creature per three caster levels. All must be in contact with each other and at least one with the caster. Unwilling creatures get a Reflex save to resist being taken along. If the destination plant is destroyed while occupied, all inside die.

name: TURN WOOD
description: Waves of force radiate from the caster in a chosen direction, pushing all wooden objects in the path back to the spell's range limit at 12 m per round. Objects wider than 7.5 cm that are fixed in place splinter and break, their fragments swept along with the force. Carried wooden items — shields, spear shafts, bows, arrows — are pushed back and drag their carriers. A creature may release a dragged item to stop being pushed. The path is set at casting; the caster may act normally thereafter. *Dispel Magic* ends the effect.

name: WALL OF THORNS
description:
The caster creates a barrier of tough, tangled brush studded with finger-length thorns (up to one 3-m cube per caster level, or two 3-m × 3-m × 1.5-m blocks per level). The effective area can be shaped in any basic form. Creatures able to pass through overgrowth unimpeded can move through freely. Others take slashing damage per round of movement equal to 8 plus their AC (up to 18 maximum; negative AC reduces the damage to 0; Dexterity AC bonuses don't count). Creatures caught in the area when it forms may make a Reflex save to escape without damage.

Forced passage requires a Strength check — for every point below the required Strength, the creature moves 1 m and takes damage. Clearing a path from outside with edged weapons takes 4 turns per 3 m of depth. Magical fire at 3rd level or higher burns the wall but causes it to act as a *Wall of Fire* for 2 turns (the caster of the *Wall of Thorns* always faces the cool side).

name: WEATHER SUMMONING
description:
The caster invokes severe weather conditions — tornadoes, blizzards, ice storms, heat waves, and similar extremes appropriate to the region and season. The specific type is chosen at casting; once called, it cannot be changed. The effects become noticeable 1d4 turns after casting, reaching full strength in 1d12 \+ 5 turns. At full strength, the effective area is between 1 and 100 km² and the duration may range from 1 turn to several days. A *Dispel Magic* can halt the weather before it reaches full strength; once at full strength, it cannot be dispelled.

Multiple casters combining *Weather Summoning* with *Control Weather*, *Control Winds*, or similar spells can invoke the most extreme conditions.

name: WORD OF RECALL
description:
When the caster utters a single word, they are instantly teleported — without error — to a sanctuary they designated when the spell was memorized. The sanctuary must be a location well known to the caster. Arrival is in a designated 3-m × 3-m area within the sanctuary. The caster may bring up to 11 kg per caster level of equipment or creatures; all must be in contact with the caster, and at least one creature must be touching the caster directly. Exceeding the weight limit causes the spell to fail.

Unwilling creatures cannot be transported. A creature's magic resistance or a successful Will save prevents their possessions from being taken along. Plane travel carries a 10% cumulative risk per plane crossed that the caster is permanently lost in the Astral Plane.

name: ANIMATE ROCK
description:
The caster instills life into an unattached stone object. The stone receives one set of instructions of 12 words or fewer and immediately begins performing that task. It cannot receive new instructions until the first task is complete.

Animated stone uses the caster's Attack Bonus. A man-sized stone has movement 6; smaller stones move faster, larger stones slower. AC is 15 or better, HP are 1d3 per 0.03 m³, and damage is 1d2 per 0.06 m³. Stone can damage structures as a ram. Animated rocks typically mass 1d3 × 50 kg per 0.03 m³.

*Material components:* a stone and a drop of the caster's blood.

name: ASTRAL SPELL
description: Functions as the wizard version. The caster's life force is projected into the Astral Plane while the physical body remains in suspended animation. The caster may bring up to seven other willing creatures, linked in a circle. Guest travelers have silver cords but are dependent on the caster; if the caster's cord is severed or the spell ends while guests are not in contact with the caster, they are stranded.

name: CHANGE STAFF
description:
A specially prepared staff is transformed into a treant-like guardian, 7 m tall, with 12 HD (40 HP), AC 20, movement 12, and 2 attacks per round dealing 4d6 each. The guardian is loyal and obeys verbal commands. If it is killed, the staff is also destroyed. If it survives the duration and the caster dismisses it, it returns to staff form and regenerates to full HP before the next use. A staff can only be used for this spell once every 24 hours.

The staff must be crafted from a lightning-struck ash, oak, or yew branch, personally cured for 28 days and worked for another 28 days. The finished staff is sealed with holly berry juice and infused with *Speak With Plants* before it can serve as the component.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol, or leaves from the same tree as the staff.

name: CHARIOT OF THE SUN
description:
A flaming chariot drawn by two horses from the Elemental Plane of Fire materialises. Overland movement is 24; flying movement is 48 with average manoeuvre. The chariot carries the caster plus up to seven man-sized creatures, all of whom must be touched during casting. Passengers are protected from the flames; other creatures within 1.5 m may take 2d4 fire damage (Reflex save avoids).

The horses obey verbal commands. They and the chariot are vulnerable only to \+1 or better weapons and water; water inflicts 1 point per litre poured on them. Passengers are immune to mundane fire; magical fire can still harm them. The chariot and both horses share a pool of 90 HP (AC 18 each). This spell can be cast only once per week.

*Material components:* a small piece of wood, two holly berries, and a fire at least the size of a torch.

name: CONFUSION
description:
The priest's *Confusion* functions as the wizard version, but affects 1d4 \+ 1 creature per two caster levels in a 12-m square area. Victims make a Will save at −2, modified by the Will save bonus. Those who fail act randomly:

| 1d10 | Action |
| ----- | ----- |
| 1 | Wander away for the remainder of the duration |
| 2–6 | Stand confused for one round, then roll again |
| 7–9 | Attack the nearest creature for one round, then roll again |
| 10 | Act normally for one round, then roll again |

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: CONJURE EARTH ELEMENTAL
description:
The caster conjures an earth elemental: 60% chance of 12 HD; 35% of 16 HD; 5% of 20 \+ 1d4 HD. The elemental obeys verbal commands and treats the caster as a friend, remaining until destroyed, struck by *Dispel Magic*, *Dismissal*, or *Holy Word*, or the duration expires.

The reverse dismisses one earth elemental within range without a save.

name: CONTROL WEATHER
description:
The caster changes weather conditions in the surrounding area, controlling precipitation, temperature, and wind direction/strength. Each change moves conditions one category up or down the relevant scale (see the weather tables), taking 1d4 turns to become noticeable. Contradictory conditions (snow in sweltering heat) are not possible.

If Weather is a major sphere for the caster, the duration and effective area are doubled, and the caster can shift by two categories. Multiple castings with overlapping durations can stack or counter each other.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol, incense, and prayer beads.

name: CREEPING DOOM
description:
The caster summons a horde of (1d6 \+ 4\) × 100 venomous biting and stinging insects, arachnids, and myriapods. The swarm can be verbally directed to move 3 m per round toward any point within range. Any creature in the swarm's area that can be affected by normal weapons takes 1 point of damage per bug automatically, killing each bug that deals damage and subtracting it from the total. The swarm can attack a single target or spread to attack multiple creatures in its area.

The swarm can be directed beyond the spell's range at a cost of 50 bugs per 9 m of additional distance. Normal dispersal methods — smoke, fire, wind — apply; exact results are at the GM's discretion.

name: EARTHQUAKE
description:
A powerful tremor ripples through the circular area. Creatures within cannot move, cast spells, or take actions for the duration. Specific terrain effects:

**Cave/Cavern/Tunnel:** Roof collapses. 8d6 damage; Reflex save halves and avoids being pinned. Pinned creatures take 1d6 per round until freed.

**Cliff/Wall:** Landslide. 8d6 damage to those in the slide's horizontal path (equal to vertical fall height); Reflex save halves and avoids being pinned.

**Open Ground:** Ground cracks and shifts. Reflex save to avoid falling. Fissures open: small creatures roll 1d4, man-sized 1d6, large or larger 1d8 — on a 1, the creature falls in and is crushed when fissures close at the end of the round.

**River/Lake/Marsh:** Drains away and floods. Swampland becomes quicksand. Reflex save or sink. Water returns at round's end, potentially drowning those trapped in mud.

**Above-ground structures:** 5d12 damage to the structure; interior creatures take 8d6 (Reflex save halves and avoids being pinned).

*Material components:* a pinch of dirt, a piece of rock, and a lump of clay.

name: EXACTION
description:
The caster exacts a service from a specific extra-planar creature of less than demigod status in exchange for eliminating a debt, a valuable payment, or a future service. The creature's true name and title must be spoken. Creatures opposed in alignment submit only if they choose to; creatures of similar alignment may avoid the spell through magic resistance.

The caster must either name a debt the creature owes to the caster's faith, or offer a fair trade. The GM ensures the agreement is equitable. If the caster demands too much, the creature may leave or attack. Once terms are agreed, the effect of a *Quest* spell binds the creature until the service is complete, after which it returns to collect payment. If the caster breaches the agreement, the creature immediately becomes immune to the caster's spells and may demand one future exaction as compensation.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol, material from the creature's home plane, and a written contract burned to seal the agreement.

name: FIRE STORM / FIRE QUENCH
description:
*Fire Storm* fills the area with roaring fire equal in heat to a *Wall of Fire*, igniting combustibles. Creatures in the area and within 3 m of its edge take 2d8 \+ 1 per caster level each round; a Reflex save halves the damage. The storm height is either 3 or 6 m; remaining volume extends horizontally.

The reverse, *Fire Quench*, smothers magical fires within the area and normal fires within twice the area. Extra-planar fire creatures of less than demigod status on non-home planes have a 5% chance per caster level of being instantly killed.

name: GATE
description:
The caster opens an extra-dimensional rift and notifies a powerful extra-planar being of their need. Either the named creature or one of its associates or agents will step through — the exact individual is at the GM's discretion. What the creature does upon arrival depends on the alignments involved, the urgency of the caster's situation, and what would interest or offend the being. If the problem is trivial, the creature may leave immediately or punish the caster for wasting its time.

Casting this spell ages the priest: 5 years if their venerable age is 150 or less, 10 years if 151–250, 15 years if 251–350, and so on.

name: HOLY WORD / UNHOLY WORD
description:
On their home plane, the caster can drive evil extra-planar creatures back to their native plane with a single word. Banished creatures cannot return for at least 1 day. In addition, all evil creatures within the area suffer effects based on their HD:

| Creature HD | Effect |
| ----- | ----- |
| Less than 4 | Killed |
| 4–7 | Paralyzed for 1d4 turns |
| 8–11 | Slowed for 2d4 rounds: −50% movement, −4 attack |
| 12+ | Deafened for 1d4 rounds: −25% movement, −2 attack, 50% spell failure |

Creatures already silenced or deafened are not subject to the HD-based penalties but can still be banished if extra-planar.

The reverse, *Unholy Word*, operates identically against good-aligned creatures.

name: REGENERATE / WITHER
description:
*Regenerate* regrows lost limbs, organs, fingers, toes, and tails on a living creature. If the missing parts are present and touching the creature, regeneration takes 1 round; otherwise it takes 2d4 turns. If the parts are unavailable or have been missing longer than 1 day per caster level, the creature must succeed at a Fortitude save or the regeneration fails.

The reverse, *Wither*, requires a successful touch attack. The targeted limb or organ becomes useless within 1 round and crumbles to dust in 2d4 turns.

*Material components:* any prayer device and holy or unholy water.

name: REINCARNATE
description:
The priest's *Reincarnate* brings back a life force that has been dead for up to 1 week per caster level into an entirely new young adult body formed from natural elements. No Constitution check, system shock, or resurrection chance is required. All physical ailments and afflictions are resolved by the new form. Constructs, elementals, outer-planar beings, and undead cannot be reincarnated; creatures who died of old age or self-inflicted wounds may also be excluded at the GM's discretion.

The creature returns with its Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma, knowledge, and memories intact, but gains all physical traits of the new form. The new Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution depend on the form; for humanoid results, use the Priest's Reincarnation Table or the wizard table at the GM's discretion.

**Sample Priest Forms (1d100):**

| Roll | New Form | Str | Dex | Con |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 01–03 | Badger | 8 | 17 | 15 |
| 04–08 | Bear, black | 19 | 13 | 15 |
| 09–12 | Bear, brown | 20 | 13 | 19 |
| 13–16 | Wild boar | 15 | 10 | 17 |
| 17–19 | Centaur | \+0 | −2 | \+1 |
| 20–23 | Dryad | \+0 | \+1 | −1 |
| 24–28 | Eagle | 10 | 15 | 12 |
| 29–31 | Elf | \+0 | \+1 | −1 |
| 32–34 | Fox | 13 | 17 | 15 |
| 35–36 | Gnome | \+0 | \+0 | \+0 |
| 37–40 | Hawk | 6 | 17 | 10 |
| 41–44 | Human | \+0 | \+0 | \+0 |
| 45–58 | Lynx | 16 | 19 | 15 |
| 59–61 | Owl | 4 | 17 | 10 |
| 62–64 | Pixie | −1 | \+1 | −1 |
| 65–68 | Raccoon | 5 | 16 | 12 |
| 69–70 | Satyr | \+0 | \+1 | \+1 |
| 71–75 | Stag | 15 | 20 | 12 |
| 76–80 | Wolf | 13 | 15 | 15 |
| 81–85 | Wolverine | 14 | 15 | 19 |
| 86–00 | GM choice or roll on Wizard's Table | — | — | — |

The GM may use divine intervention to influence the result. The caster's holy or unholy symbol replaces the drum used in the wizard version and is not consumed.

name: RESTORATION / ENERGY DRAIN
description:
*Restoration* restores 1 drained level to a creature, provided the level was lost within 1 day per caster level. It returns the creature's XP to the minimum needed for the recovered level. It also cures *Feeble Mind*, *Confusion*, and most other insanity effects. Both the caster and subject are aged: 2 years if venerable age is 150 or less, 4 years if 151–250, and so on.

The reverse, *Energy Drain*, drains 1 level from a creature via a successful touch attack, exactly as a powerful undead would. The reverse does not cause unnatural aging.

name: RESURRECTION / DESTRUCTION
description:
*Resurrection* restores life, limb, and full health to any dead creature — including elves and others with spirits — who has been dead no longer than 10 years per caster level. A complete body is not required; the spell forms a new body from available remains and elements. Poison, disease, and afflictions are absent from the new body. The new body is the same age as the old, so it cannot raise those who died of old age.

The creature must succeed at a Fortitude save (Constitution–resurrection chance). Success: the creature is immediately restored to life and full HP, losing 1 Constitution permanently. The creature retains any spells remaining in memory at death. A character's maximum times they can be raised in their own body equals their starting Constitution. Failure: only a *Wish* can restore them.

Casting ages the priest: 3 years if venerable age is 150 or less, 6 years if 151–250, and so on. The caster must rest completely for 1 day per HD or level of the revived creature.

The reverse, *Destruction*, instantly kills a creature on a successful touch attack and reduces it to dust unless it succeeds at a Fortitude save at −4. A creature that makes its save still takes 8d6 damage, which may kill it without destroying the body. Only a *Wish* or divine intervention restores a creature slain by *Destruction*. The reverse does not age the caster.

*Material components:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol and holy or unholy water.

name: SUCCORED RETREAT / SUCCORED SUMMONS
description:
The caster enchants a specially prepared object worth at least 200 gp and binds it to a known sanctuary. When a creature the caster has willingly given the object to destroys it and speaks the command word, they are instantly teleported (without error) to the sanctuary, along with all worn and carried gear up to their carry weight. No other creatures can be brought along, except a familiar.

The reverse, *Succored Summons*, teleports the caster to wherever the holder is when the object is broken and the command word spoken. The caster instantly knows the general situation at the target location and may choose whether or not to answer the call (this is the key distinction from the wizard version, which compels the caster to respond). The decision must be made immediately and is final.

Planar travel requires material components of at least 500 gp and 5,000 gp.

*Material components:* the enchanted object (consumed each use) and a master-crafted object of similar type worth at least ten times as much (not consumed).

name: SUNRAY
description:
Each round the caster takes no other action (aside from movement), they may invoke an area of pure natural sunlight at any point within range. Creatures in the area must make a Reflex save or be blinded for 1d3 rounds; those using infravision are blinded for 2d4 rounds on a failed save. Creatures harmed by natural sunlight are permanently blinded on a failed save, or blinded 2d6 rounds on success. All creatures within the main area and 6 m beyond it lose infravision for 1d4 \+ 1 rounds.

The sunray functions as natural sunlight for all special weaknesses. Against undead: a failed save destroys certain types immediately (per the bestiary); others take 8d6 damage per round in the main area (Reflex save halves), and 3d6 per round within 6 m of the edge (Reflex save avoids). Fungi and fungal creatures are treated as undead for damage purposes with no save.

*Material components:* an aster seed and a sunstone.

name: SYMBOL
description:
Functions identically to the wizard version. The priest's version has the same casting modes (quickly scribbled or carefully inscribed), the same trigger options, and the same available runes. All immunization, password, and permanency rules apply equally.

*Lesser Runes:* Fear, Hopelessness, Pain, Persuasion. *Greater Runes:* Death, Discord, Insanity, Sleep, Spell Loss, Stunning.

*Material components:* mercury and phosphorus for a quickly scribbled symbol; mercury, phosphorus, and powdered opal and diamond worth 10,000 gp combined for a carefully inscribed symbol.

name: TRANSMUTE METAL TO WOOD
description:
The caster converts a metal object of up to 4.5 kg per caster level into wood. Objects with magical properties have 90% resistance to the effect. Objects carried, held, or worn by a creature receive a Fortitude save equal to the creature's save versus spells. Artefacts and relics cannot be affected. Restoration requires a *Limited Wish*, *Wish*, or equivalent.

Wooden weapons converted from metal suffer −2 to attack and damage rolls and splinter on a natural roll of 1 or 2\. Wooden armour converted from metal loses 2 AC and takes an additional −1 AC penalty each time it is struck with a natural roll of 19 or 20\.

*Material component:* the caster's holy or unholy symbol.

name: WIND WALK
description:
The caster and up to three other touched creatures alter their bodies into cloudlike vapour, moving through the air at speeds ranging from movement 6 (manoeuvre class 1\) to 100 km/h at high altitude. Wind walkers appear misty and translucent. They can resume physical form and later return to vapour form at will; each transition takes 1 round and counts against the duration. The caster can end the effect for the whole group or for individuals. During the last round, creatures still in vapour form automatically descend to ground level.

In vapour form, creatures are immune to mundane weapon attacks. No spellcasting is possible while vaporous.

*Material components:* fire and holy or unholy water.
